name: BraTS 2021
multi_sequence_detail: T1, T1Gd (T1c), T2, T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: enhancing tumor, tumor core, whole tumor
description: Multi-parametric MRI of adult glioma with expert tumor sub-region segmentations and MGMT methylation status; also mirrored on TCIA and Kaggle.

label_schema: Label 1 — necrotic tumor core (NCR)
Label 2 — peritumoral edematous/invaded tissue (ED)
Label 4 — GD-enhancing tumor (ET)
Evaluated regions: Whole Tumor (1+2+4), Tumor Core (1+4), Enhancing Tumor (4)
details.file_structure: One folder per case: BraTS2021_XXXXX/ with _t1.nii.gz, _t1ce.nii.gz, _t2.nii.gz, _flair.nii.gz and _seg.nii.gz (all co-registered, skull-stripped, 1mm³).
details.splits: 1,251 training (public labels) / 219 validation / 570 hidden test
details.extra: Classification track adds a per-subject binary MGMT promoter methylation label (CSV).

name: BraTS-Africa
full_name: BraTS 2023: Glioma Segmentation in Sub-Saharan Africa
multi_sequence_detail: T1, T1c, T2, T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: enhancing tumor, necrotic core, peritumoral edema
description: Multi-parametric brain MRI of glioma and other CNS tumor patients from Sub-Saharan Africa with expert tumor annotations.

label_schema: Voxel-wise tumor segmentation using the BraTS 2023 '3-label system' (three distinct classes)
Label 1 - Non-enhancing tumor core (NETC): non-enhancing part of the tumor core, including necrosis, cystic change, calcification
Label 2 - Surrounding non-enhancing FLAIR hyperintensity (SNFH): peritumoral edematous / infiltrated tissue (vasogenic edema)
Label 3 - Enhancing tumor (ET): gadolinium/post-contrast enhancing tumor region
Label 0 - background
Standard evaluation sub-regions derived from these: ET, Tumor Core (TC = ET + NETC), Whole Tumor (WT = ET + NETC + SNFH)
details.file_structure: One folder per case (BraTS-SSA-XXXXX-000 style) holding four co-registered, skull-stripped, 1mm-isotropic NIfTI (.nii.gz) volumes -- T1 (t1n), post-contrast T1/T1CE (t1c), T2 (t2w), and T2-FLAIR (t2f) -- plus a single multi-label ground-truth segmentation mask (-seg.nii.gz).
details.splits: Challenge dataset partitioned into training 70% / validation 10% / testing 20% (of 146 total cases); no fixed integer counts published in the paper.
details.extra: Only the training subset is released with public ground-truth labels -- validation and testing segmentation labels are withheld by the challenge. Data preprocessing (DICOM->NIfTI, co-registration to SRI24 template, 1mm isotropic resampling, skull-stripping) is identical to BraTS 2017-2022; images are notably lower-resolution/contrast reflecting real-world Sub-Saharan African MRI.

name: BraTS-Mets
multi_sequence_detail: T1, T1c, T2, T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: enhancing tumor, non-enhancing core, FLAIR hyperintensity
description: Pre-treatment multi-parametric MRI of brain metastasis patients with expert tumor sub-region segmentations.

label_schema: Label 1 - Non-enhancing tumor core (NETC): presumed necrotic core, a non-enhancing focus surrounded by enhancing tumor
Label 2 - Surrounding non-enhancing FLAIR hyperintensity (SNFH): peritumoral edema / tumor-infiltrated tissue, hyperintense on T2-FLAIR
Label 3 - Gd-enhancing tumor (ET): enhancing portion, hyperintense on T1Gd relative to T1
Label 0 - background/everything else
Standard evaluation regions derived from these: whole tumor (WT), tumor core (TC), enhancing tumor (ET)
details.file_structure: Per-case folders (e.g. BraTS-MET-XXXXX-000) each containing four co-registered, skull-stripped MRI volumes (T1 native t1n, T1-Gd t1c, T2 t2w, T2-FLAIR t2f) plus the seg label volume, all in NIfTI (.nii.gz) format on a common 1mm isotropic atlas.
details.splits: Training: 402 studies (3,076 lesions, public with labels); Validation: 31 studies (139 lesions, labels held out); Testing: 59 studies (218 lesions, private)
details.extra: Ground-truth segmentations were produced via a multi-stage pipeline (automated UNET pre-segmentation, then correction by a trainee, a neuroradiologist, and a final approving neuroradiologist); annotations cover untreated/pre-treatment brain metastases and follow the standardized BraTS 2023 labeling where the former label 4 (enhancing tumor) is renumbered to label 3.

name: BraTS-PEDs
multi_sequence_detail: T1, T1c, T2, T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: enhancing tumor, tumor core, whole tumor
description: Multi-parametric MRI of pediatric brain tumor patients with expert tumor sub-region segmentations.

label_schema: Voxel-wise multi-class segmentation of pediatric high-grade glioma into tumor subregions (one NIfTI label map per case)
Released to participants as 3 labels: ET = enhancing tumor; NC = non-enhancing component (a merge of non-enhancing tumor + cystic component + necrosis); ED = peritumoral edema
ET (enhancing tumor): areas of enhancement/brightness on post-contrast T1 (T1CE) relative to pre-contrast T1
NC (non-enhancing component): combines non-enhancing tumor (NET), cystic component (CC), and necrosis
ED (peritumoral edema): abnormal hyperintense signal on T2-FLAIR, finger-like spreading surrounding the tumor
Underlying expert annotations originally covered 4 subregions (RAPNO-recommended): ET, NET (non-enhancing tumor), CC (cystic component), ED (edema); NET+CC+necrosis were fused into NC for release
Evaluation/scoring is on 3 composite regions: ET (enhancing tumor), TC (tumor core = ET + cystic component + necrosis), and WT (whole tumor = entire tumorous region)
Note: exact integer voxel values for each label class are not stated in the challenge manuscript, so the class-to-integer mapping is unconfirmed here

name: BraTS-MEN
multi_sequence_detail: T1, T1c, T2, T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: enhancing tumor, non-enhancing core, FLAIR hyperintensity
description: Pre-operative multi-parametric MRI of intracranial meningioma patients with expert tumor segmentations.

label_schema: Label 0 - background
Label 1 - NETC: non-enhancing tumor core
Label 2 - SNFH: surrounding non-enhancing T2/FLAIR hyperintensity (peritumoral edema)
Label 3 - ET: enhancing tumor
Segmentation is a single multi-label volume; commonly evaluated as the overlapping regions Whole Tumor (WT = ET+SNFH+NETC), Tumor Core (TC = ET+NETC), and Enhancing Tumor (ET)
details.file_structure: One folder per subject (e.g. BraTS-MEN-XXXXX-000) containing four co-registered, skull-stripped MRI volumes as gzipped NIfTI (suffixes -t1n = pre-contrast T1, -t1c = post-contrast T1, -t2w = T2, -t2f = T2-FLAIR; all .nii.gz) plus one multi-label segmentation mask (-seg.nii.gz), each resampled to 1 mm isotropic resolution.
details.extra: Data (~1,650 preoperative mpMRI cases from multiple academic centers, converted DICOM->NIfTI and co-registered to a common atlas) are for the RSNA-ASNR-MICCAI BraTS 2023 Meningioma segmentation challenge; because meningiomas are typically extra-axial and often lack a necrotic/non-enhancing core, label 1 (NETC) is absent in many cases. Official train/validation/test split counts could not be verified from public sources.

name: UPenn-GBM
paired_text_detail: structured clinical, genomic and radiomic data (not reports)
multi_sequence_detail: T1, T1Gd, T2, T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: tumor subregions and whole-brain masks
description: Multiparametric MRI from 630 glioblastoma patients with expert-corrected tumor and whole-brain segmentations plus clinical, genomic and survival data.

label_schema: Tumor sub-region segmentation (BraTS labeling convention): Label 1 - necrotic and non-enhancing tumor core (NCR/NET)
Label 2 - peritumoral edematous / infiltrated tissue (ED)
Label 4 - GD-enhancing tumor (ET); Label 0 - background
Whole-brain segmentation masks (brain extraction / skull-strip) provided separately
Two segmentation versions per subject: computer-aided AUTOMATED labels and MANUALLY-CORRECTED labels reviewed by board-certified neuroradiologists
details.file_structure: Per-subject original DICOM plus preprocessed NIfTI multiparametric MRI (T1, T1-GD/post-contrast, T2, T2-FLAIR) co-registered to the SRI24 atlas; segmentation labels are single-channel NIfTI (.nii.gz) volumes aligned to the NIfTI images, delivered in separate 'automated' and 'manually-corrected' segmentation sets.
details.extra: Companion data (not image labels) includes a radiomic-feature CSV, clinical/molecular tables (e.g. MGMT, IDH, overall survival) and histopathology whole-slide images for a 34-patient subset; caveat: the atlas-registered NIfTI images and their segmentations do NOT spatially align with the original DICOM series by design.

name: ISLES 2022
multi_sequence_detail: DWI (b=1000), ADC, FLAIR
segmentation_detail: expert ischemic stroke lesion masks
description: Multi-center MRI of 250 acute/sub-acute ischemic stroke cases with DWI, ADC and FLAIR and expert lesion segmentations (NIfTI/BIDS).

label_schema: Binary ischemic stroke lesion mask (DWI-defined)
details.file_structure: BIDS layout: sub-strokeXXXX/ses-*/ with dwi, adc, flair NIfTI + derivatives/ holding msk.nii.gz.
details.splits: 250 public (train) / 150 hidden test

name: ATLAS R2.0
full_name: Anatomical Tracings of Lesions After Stroke, Release 2.0
segmentation_detail: manual whole-brain stroke lesion masks
description: Large open T1-weighted MRI stroke dataset with manually traced lesion masks, accessed after agreeing to terms of use.

label_schema: Binary whole-brain stroke lesion segmentation mask on T1-weighted MRI
Value 1 - lesion (manually segmented stroke lesion voxels)
Value 0 - background/non-lesion
Single lesion class only (no lesion subtyping or multi-class labels)
details.file_structure: T1w images and lesion masks are stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz) files organized in BIDS derivatives format, with preprocessed volumes registered to MNI-152 template space; each subject has a T1w image (sub-XXXX_ses-1_..._T1w.nii.gz) and, for the training set, a corresponding binary lesion mask (..._label-L_desc-T1lesion_mask.nii.gz).
details.splits: Training 655 subjects (T1w + public lesion masks), Test 300 subjects (T1w only, masks hidden), Generalizability/hidden 316 subjects (fully withheld); 1,271 total.
details.extra: Lesions were manually segmented by trained raters following a standardized ATLAS tracing protocol; test and generalizability masks are withheld and scored only via the challenge's automated evaluator.

name: WMH Segmentation Challenge
full_name: MICCAI 2017 White Matter Hyperintensity Segmentation Challenge
multi_sequence_detail: FLAIR, T1
segmentation_detail: manual binary white matter hyperintensity masks
description: Multi-scanner brain MRI of 60 subjects with co-registered FLAIR and T1 and manual white matter hyperintensity annotations.

label_schema: Voxel-wise segmentation mask (single label volume, wmh.nii.gz)
Label 1 - white matter hyperintensity (WMH) of presumed vascular origin (the target class)
Label 2 - other pathology (e.g. lacunes/infarcts and other non-WMH lesions), excluded/ignored during evaluation
Label 0 - background (all other tissue / non-WMH)
details.file_structure: Per-subject folders each contain 'orig/' (original) and 'pre/' (bias-corrected, brain-extracted, co-registered) subfolders holding FLAIR.nii.gz and T1.nii.gz, plus a manual reference segmentation 'wmh.nii.gz'; all volumes are NIfTI (.nii.gz).
details.splits: 60 training cases (3 MR scanners/sites: UMC Utrecht, NUHS Singapore, VU Amsterdam) and 110 test cases (5 MR scanners, including two additional held-out scanners); no official validation split.
details.extra: Effectively a binary task: because label-2 'other pathology' voxels are masked out of the metrics, evaluation compares only WMH (label 1) vs background; reference masks are single-observer manual annotations.

name: MSSEG-2
full_name: MICCAI 2021 MS New Lesions Segmentation Challenge
multi_sequence_detail: 3D FLAIR at two longitudinal time-points
segmentation_detail: masks of new MS lesions at follow-up
description: Longitudinal 3D FLAIR MRI of 100 MS patients for detecting and segmenting newly appearing lesions.

label_schema: Single foreground class: binary voxel mask of NEW multiple-sclerosis lesions appearing at the second (follow-up) timepoint relative to the first timepoint
Mask value 1 = new lesion voxel, 0 = background
Ground truth is a consensus reference: 4 expert neuroradiologists independently segmented new lesions, a senior expert reviewed disputed lesions, then majority voting fused the segmentations
Patients with no new lesions have an empty (all-zero) ground-truth mask
details.file_structure: Per-patient folders each containing two co-registered 3D FLAIR volumes (first and second timepoint, rigidly registered to a common middle space) and one consensus lesion mask, all as NIfTI (.nii.gz) volumes; the FLAIR image and the ground-truth mask are the same-shape 3D grids (e.g. flair_time01_on_middle_space.nii.gz, flair_time02_on_middle_space.nii.gz, ground_truth.nii.gz).
details.splits: 40 training patients, 60 testing patients (100 total, each with 2 timepoints)
details.extra: Data from the OFSEP French MS cohort spanning 15 scanners (GE, Philips, Siemens; 1.5T and 3T); the annotation task is longitudinal change detection between two FLAIR timepoints, not single-image lesion segmentation. Only the training ground truth was released; test masks were held out for challenge evaluation.

name: Shifts MS
multi_sequence_detail: FLAIR, T1
segmentation_detail: consensus white matter MS lesion masks
description: Multi-center white matter MS lesion dataset with FLAIR and T1 and consensus masks aggregating several cohorts.

label_schema: Binary voxel-wise segmentation of white matter multiple sclerosis (MS) lesions on 3D brain MRI
Value 1 = white matter MS lesion (foreground)
Value 0 = background / non-lesion
Ground-truth masks are a consensus of multiple expert annotators (single-rater mask for the Best and Lausanne sites)
Each sample also provides a brain/foreground mask (fg_mask) used to restrict evaluation to intracranial tissue
details.file_structure: Distributed as NIfTI (.nii.gz) volumes, all resampled to 1mm isovoxel space, with each case providing FLAIR (and T1) input scans plus a ground-truth binary lesion mask (and a brain mask for the test/eval sets), organized into separate train / validation / test directories referenced by the mswml baseline code.
details.splits: In-domain: train 33 patients, dev-in 7, eval-in 33 (all from Rennes, Bordeaux, Lyon, Best); distributional-shift: dev-out 25 (Ljubljana), eval-out 74 (Lausanne).
details.extra: This is a canonical distributional-shift benchmark: the dev-out (Ljubljana) and eval-out (Lausanne) splits come from different sites/scanners than the training data to test robustness and uncertainty estimation; number of consensus raters varies by site (Rennes/Bordeaux/Lyon ~7, Ljubljana 3, Best/Lausanne single mask). Access is restricted and requires signing the OFSEP data usage agreement (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0).

name: EPISURG
full_name: EPISURG: Postoperative MRI for Resection Neurosurgery in Epilepsy
segmentation_detail: resection cavity masks by 3 raters (~200 subjects)
description: Anonymized postoperative brain MRIs from 430 refractory epilepsy patients with resection-cavity segmentations.

label_schema: Binary resection-cavity segmentation mask: voxel value 1 = resection cavity, 0 = background (single foreground class)
Manual cavity masks provided by up to 3 human raters per labeled subject, each stored as a separate mask file with the rater number encoded in the filename (e.g. ...seg-1, ...seg-2, ...seg-3)
Rater 1 (neuroimaging researcher): 133 subjects; Rater 2 (clinical scientist): 34 subjects; Rater 3 (neurologist): 33 subjects, on partially overlapping subsets
Per-subject metadata (subjects.csv): Subject ID, Hemisphere, and surgery Type
details.file_structure: Layout EPISURG/subjects/<subject_id>/ with preop/ and postop/ subfolders holding gzipped NIfTI (.nii.gz) T1-weighted MRIs (filenames contain 'preop' or 'postop-t1mri'); resection-cavity masks sit alongside the postop image as '*seg*.nii.gz' NIfTI label maps, plus a top-level EPISURG/subjects.csv metadata table.
details.extra: 430 postoperative T1w MRIs (269 with a paired preoperative MRI); images are anonymised and defaced. Only ~133+ subjects carry manual masks and a subset are segmented by multiple raters, enabling inter-rater variability analysis. License CC BY-NC-SA 4.0; requires citing Perez-Garcia et al. (MICCAI 2020) and the UCL RDR data-use agreement.

name: ADNI
multi_sequence_detail: T1w, T2/FLAIR, plus FDG, amyloid (PiB/AV45) and tau PET
description: Longitudinal multimodal MRI and PET with genetics, cognition and biomarkers for Alzheimer's disease, shared after application review.

label_schema: Participant-level clinical diagnosis (not image segmentation) is the primary label
Cognitively Normal / Unimpaired (CN) - healthy elderly control
Significant Memory Concern (SMC) - subjective memory complaint, normal cognition (introduced in ADNI-2)
Early Mild Cognitive Impairment (EMCI)
Late Mild Cognitive Impairment (LMCI)
Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) - generic MCI label used in ADNI-1
Alzheimer's Disease / Dementia (AD)
Companion clinical/cognitive scores accompany each visit (e.g., MMSE, CDR/CDR-SB, ADAS-Cog), plus CSF and genetic biomarkers
No voxel-level segmentation masks are provided as an official label set

name: OASIS-1
full_name: Open Access Series of Imaging Studies, Cross-sectional
segmentation_detail: brain masks and tissue segmentations in processed subset
description: Cross-sectional T1-weighted brain MRI from 416 subjects with clinical dementia ratings.

label_schema: FSL/FAST tissue segmentation of the atlas-registered T1 image into 3 tissue classes: CSF, gray matter, white matter (hidden-Markov-random-field segmentation)
Segmentation integer labels follow FSL FAST convention for T1: 1 = CSF, 2 = gray matter, 3 = white matter
Brain mask: skull-stripped mask applied to the atlas-registered, gain-field-corrected, motion-averaged T1 image
Per-subject clinical/demographic labels (spreadsheet): CDR (Clinical Dementia Rating: 0 nondemented, 0.5 very mild, 1 mild, 2 moderate AD), MMSE, Age, Sex (M/F), Handedness, Education, SES
Per-subject derived measures: eTIV (estimated total intracranial volume), nWBV (normalized whole-brain volume), ASF (atlas scaling factor)
details.file_structure: Data organized per subject (folders named OAS1_XXXX_MRn); each contains RAW T1-weighted scans and PROCESSED derivatives — the atlas-registered gain-field-corrected averaged image, the brain-masked image, and the FSL_SEG tissue-segmentation (_fseg) volume — stored in Analyze 7.5 format (.img/.hdr, gzipped); clinical/demographic labels are provided in a separate cross-sectional CSV/Excel spreadsheet (FreeSurfer outputs are also distributed as add-on archives).
details.extra: 416 subjects aged 18-96 (434 MR sessions); 100 subjects over age 60 are clinically diagnosed with very mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease (CDR > 0), and a 20-subject reliability subset was rescanned within 90 days. Segmentation/labels are algorithm-generated (FSL), not manual expert annotations.

name: OASIS-3
full_name: Open Access Series of Imaging Studies, Longitudinal Multimodal
multi_sequence_detail: T1w, T2w, FLAIR, ASL, SWI, TOF, BOLD fMRI, DTI, plus PiB, AV45, FDG, tau PET
segmentation_detail: FreeSurfer segmentation and PUP-processed PET derivatives
description: Longitudinal multimodal MRI (incl. fMRI, DTI) and amyloid/metabolic/tau PET for normal aging and Alzheimer disease.

label_schema: Labels are automated FreeSurfer recon-all segmentations, not manual annotations

Subcortical volumetric segmentation (aseg) using the standard FreeSurferColorLUT integer indices (e.g. 17 Left-Hippocampus, 18 Left-Amygdala, 53 Right-Hippocampus, plus thalamus, caudate, putamen, pallidum, ventricles, brainstem, cerebellum, etc.)
Cortical parcellation via the Desikan-Killiany atlas (aparc, ~34 gyral regions per hemisphere); Destrieux atlas (aparc.a2009s, ~74 regions/hemisphere) also produced by recon-all
Per-region morphometrics tabulated in aseg.stats / aparc.stats (regional volume, cortical thickness, surface area)
PUP (PET Unified Pipeline) derivatives: regional tracer uptake / SUVR (standardized uptake value ratios) computed over FreeSurfer-defined ROIs
PET tracers: PIB (amyloid), AV45 / florbetapir (amyloid), FDG (glucose metabolism); plus 451 AV1451 / flortaucipir (tau) sessions
FreeSurfer processing versions 5.0, 5.1, and 5.3 (version encoded in the derivative ID)
details.file_structure: Derivatives are hosted on XNAT and delivered per session: FreeSurfer output in folders named like OAS30001_Freesurfer53_d0129 containing standard recon-all structure (mri/ segmentation volumes as .mgz, stats/ tables as .stats text, plus label/ and surf/), and PUP output in folders like OAS30001_AV45_PUPTIMECOURSE_d2430 with regional SUVR tables; raw images are DICOM/NIfTI in BIDS-like layout.
details.extra: No official ML train/validation/test split; labels are automated pipeline outputs (FreeSurfer + PUP), so segmentations/regional stats are not manually reviewed, and mixed FreeSurfer versions (5.0/5.1/5.3) across sessions can introduce version-dependent measurement differences.

name: IXI
multi_sequence_detail: T1, T2, PD-weighted, MRA, and DWI (15 directions)
description: Multi-sequence brain MRI (T1, T2, PD, MRA, DWI) from nearly 600 healthy subjects at three London hospitals.

label_schema: No image-level segmentation, detection, or radiology-report labels; the only 'labels' are per-subject demographic/metadata fields in the IXI.xls spreadsheet
IXI_ID - subject identifier (matches image filename prefix, e.g. IXI002)
SEX_ID - biological sex (1 = male, 2 = female)
AGE - age at scan (years, continuous)
DOB - date of birth
HEIGHT and WEIGHT
ETHNIC_ID - ethnicity code
MARITAL_ID - marital status code
OCCUPATION_ID - occupation code
QUALIFICATION_ID - education/qualification code
STUDY_DATE / DATE_AVAILABLE - scan/acquisition dates
All subjects are normal, healthy volunteers (no disease/diagnosis labels)
details.file_structure: Images are NIfTI (.nii.gz) distributed as per-modality compressed tar archives (IXI-T1.tar, IXI-T2.tar, IXI-PD.tar, IXI-MRA.tar, IXI-DTI.tar with bvecs.txt/bvals.txt); filenames encode subject and acquisition site (e.g. IXI002-Guys-0828-T1.nii.gz, sites Guys/HH/IOP); labels are a single Excel file IXI.xls keyed by subject ID, not per-image mask files.
details.extra: ~600 scans from three London hospitals (Guy's, Hammersmith, Institute of Psychiatry) across five modalities (T1, T2, PD-weighted, MRA, and 15-direction diffusion/DTI); released under CC BY-SA 3.0. Not all subjects have all five modalities.

name: ABIDE
multi_sequence_detail: resting-state fMRI plus anatomical T1-weighted MRI
description: Aggregated resting-state fMRI and structural MRI with phenotypic data from individuals with autism and typical controls.

label_schema: Primary group label DX_GROUP: 1 = Autism (ASD), 2 = Control (Typical)
DSM_IV_TR diagnostic subtype label distinguishing Control / Autism / Asperger's / PDD-NOS
Clinical diagnostic-instrument scores: ADOS and ADI-R subscores
IQ measures: full-scale, verbal, and performance IQ
Demographic/subject variables: age at scan, sex, handedness
Acquisition site identifier (SITE_ID) and subject ID (SUB_ID)
No pixel-level segmentation or lesion masks: labels are subject-level phenotype only
details.file_structure: Per-subject resting-state fMRI and anatomical MRI volumes (NIfTI), with all annotations held in a single composite phenotypic CSV keyed by subject ID plus an accompanying 'Phenotypic Data Legend' document defining every column; the PCP preprocessed release adds derivative NIfTI volumes, ROI time-series (.1D), and connectivity matrices.
details.extra: Two releases: ABIDE I (1,112 subjects = 539 ASD / 573 TC across 17 sites) and ABIDE II (1,114 datasets = 521 ASD / 593 controls across 19 sites); the Preprocessed Connectomes Project provides derivatives across multiple pipelines (CCS, CPAC, DPARSF, NIAK) and atlas parcellations, but there is no official train/validation/test split.

name: HCP Young Adult
full_name: Human Connectome Project — WU-Minn Young Adult (1200 Subjects)
multi_sequence_detail: 3T T1w, T2w, resting-state and task fMRI, HARDI diffusion; 7T and MEG subsets
segmentation_detail: FreeSurfer cortical/subcortical segmentations and surface parcellations
license: HCP Open Access Data Use Terms
description: High-resolution multimodal 3T/7T MRI (structural, fMRI, diffusion) from ~1200 healthy young adults.

label_schema: Per-subject labels are FreeSurfer recon-all outputs from the HCP structural pipeline (FreeSurfer 5.3.0-HCP); the pipeline runs -aparc2aseg, -wmparc, -cortparc and -cortparc2 (Ref. Manual p.129)

aseg: whole-brain subcortical volumetric segmentation using the standard FreeSurfer FreeSurferColorLUT integer labels (e.g. hippocampus, amygdala, thalamus, caudate, putamen, pallidum, ventricles, brainstem, cerebellum)
aparc+aseg: volumetric segmentation combining subcortical aseg structures with cortical gray-matter parcels
wmparc: white-matter parcellation labeling WM adjacent to each cortical parcel
Cortical surface parcellation - Desikan-Killiany atlas (aparc): 34 gyral-based regions per hemisphere
Cortical surface parcellation - Destrieux atlas (aparc.a2009s): 74 gyral/sulcal regions per hemisphere
White and pial cortical surfaces reconstructed and folding-registered to FreeSurfer fsaverage, then to Conte69 164k_fs_LR and downsampled 32k_fs_LR meshes

Group-average companion parcellations (separate GroupAvg package): Glasser et al. 2016 multimodal HCP-MMP1.0 cortical areas, Gordon333, Brodmann (Broadmann09), and Yeo/RSN networks, as CIFTI .dlabel.nii on 32k_fs_LR
details.file_structure: Per subject, volumetric segmentations (aseg/aparc+aseg/wmparc) live under T1w/<subject>/ as FreeSurfer .mgz plus AC-PC-aligned NIfTI (.nii.gz), FreeSurfer surfaces and cortical parcellation .annot files under T1w/Native/, and Workbench-format GIFTI (.gii) surfaces and CIFTI (.dscalar.nii/.dlabel.nii) files under MNINonLinear/ and MNINonLinear/fsaverage_LR32k/, all downloaded as per-subject packages from ConnectomeDB / AWS S3.
details.extra: No train/val/test split; this is a research dataset of 1206 healthy young adults (1113 with 3T structural MR) organized around family/twin structure, so users must respect subject relatedness when partitioning. The Glasser HCP-MMP1.0 multimodal parcellation is a group-average label file distributed in the separate HCP_S1200_GroupAvg package, not per subject.

name: RSNA 2019 ICH Detection
description: Over 25,000 non-contrast head CT exams labeled for presence and five subtypes of acute intracranial hemorrhage.

label_schema: Multi-label binary classification at the CT slice (DICOM image) level
epidural - epidural hemorrhage (0/1)
intraparenchymal - intraparenchymal hemorrhage (0/1)
intraventricular - intraventricular hemorrhage (0/1)
subarachnoid - subarachnoid hemorrhage (0/1)
subdural - subdural hemorrhage (0/1)
any - present if any of the 5 subtypes is present (0/1)
details.file_structure: Images are individual axial head-CT slices stored as DICOM (.dcm) files, one file per slice; labels are provided in a long-format CSV (e.g. stage_2_train.csv) with an 'ID' column formatted 'ID_<SOPInstanceUID>_<subtype>' and a binary 'Label' column, so each image contributes 6 rows (5 subtypes + 'any').
details.extra: Per the RSNA challenge, the dataset comprises more than 25,000 annotated cranial CT exams (~870k slices), labeled at the image level by volunteer neuroradiologists from the American Society of Neuroradiology (ASNR); the competition ran in two stages (stage_1 and stage_2 train/test releases). Exact per-image train/test counts could not be verified from a primary source in this session.

name: RSNA 2025 Aneurysm Detection
full_name: RSNA Intracranial Aneurysm Detection AI Challenge 2025
multi_sequence_detail: CT angiography, MR angiography, T1 post-contrast and T2-weighted MRI
segmentation_detail: ~200 studies include aneurysm segmentations
description: Multi-modality brain studies from 18 sites annotated for aneurysm presence and location across 13 arterial territories.

label_schema: Multi-label binary classification: 14 targets per series = 13 anatomical location labels + 1 overall 'Aneurysm Present' flag (0=absent, 1=present)
Location 1 - Left Infraclinoid Internal Carotid Artery
Location 2 - Right Infraclinoid Internal Carotid Artery
Location 3 - Left Supraclinoid Internal Carotid Artery
Location 4 - Right Supraclinoid Internal Carotid Artery
Location 5 - Left Middle Cerebral Artery
Location 6 - Right Middle Cerebral Artery
Location 7 - Anterior Communicating Artery
Location 8 - Left Anterior Cerebral Artery
Location 9 - Right Anterior Cerebral Artery
Location 10 - Left Posterior Communicating Artery
Location 11 - Right Posterior Communicating Artery
Location 12 - Basilar Tip
Location 13 - Other Posterior Circulation
Target 14 - 'Aneurysm Present' (binary, 1 if any aneurysm anywhere in the scan)
train_localizers.csv gives point (not box) annotations: aneurysm center as {'x','y'} pixel coordinates on a specific slice (SOPInstanceUID) with its location name
Modalities labeled per series: CTA, MRA, MRI T2, MRI T1 post-contrast
details.file_structure: Imaging is DICOM organized in per-series folders keyed by SeriesInstanceUID (mostly single-frame multi-file series, ~7% multi-frame); series-level labels are in train.csv (SeriesInstanceUID, Modality, PatientAge/Sex, 13 location binaries + Aneurysm Present), point annotations in train_localizers.csv, and voxel segmentation masks for a subset stored as separate files.
details.splits: Training: 4,348 series from 4,348 unique patients (exactly one series/modality per patient); ~1,864 (42.9%) contain aneurysms. Test set is hidden/held-out (community-estimated ~2,500 series); no official public validation split.
details.extra: Reference dataset built from CT and MR studies across 18 sites on five continents, annotated by ~60 expert radiologists; a subset (~200 studies) additionally includes voxel-wise aneurysm segmentation masks as companion labels, and localizer point annotations cover ~99.9% of aneurysm-positive series.

name: CQ500
description: 491 head CT scans with consensus reads by three radiologists for hemorrhage, fractures, midline shift and mass effect.

label_schema: Scan-level binary (present/absent) labels, no segmentation masks
Intracranial hemorrhage (ICH) - overall presence
5 ICH subtypes: Intraparenchymal (IPH), Intraventricular (IVH), Subdural (SDH), Extradural/epidural (EDH), Subarachnoid (SAH)
BleedLocation-Left and BleedLocation-Right (hemisphere of bleed)
ChronicBleed (chronic vs acute)
Fracture, CalvarialFracture, and OtherFracture
MassEffect
MidlineShift
Each of the above labeled independently by 3 radiologists (columns prefixed R1:, R2:, R3:); majority/consensus of the 3 reads is used as the reference standard
details.file_structure: Non-contrast head CT studies distributed as per-scan DICOM folders (CQ500CT0 ... CQ500CT490, often multiple series/windows per scan); all annotations are in a single reads.csv with one row per scan and one column per (reader x finding) binary field.
details.extra: CQ500 is a validation/test-only benchmark with no train split; it is a multi-reader dataset (3 independent radiologists per scan) and provides no pixel-level segmentation or bounding boxes - only study-level binary findings.

name: ADAM
multi_sequence_detail: TOF-MRA plus one structural MR image (T1, T2 or FLAIR)
segmentation_detail: binary masks of unruptured intracranial aneurysms
description: TOF-MRA challenge for detecting and segmenting unruptured intracranial aneurysms with a hidden test set.

label_schema: Voxel-wise segmentation mask (aneurysms.nii.gz) with 3 label values
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - untreated, unruptured intracranial aneurysm (the evaluation target)
Label 2 - treated aneurysm (ignored/excluded during evaluation)
Companion location.txt gives the center voxel coordinate of each aneurysm (weak/point label for the detection task)
Two tasks: (1) detection (scored by sensitivity + false-positive count), (2) segmentation (scored by Dice, 95th-percentile modified Hausdorff distance, volumetric similarity)
details.file_structure: NIfTI (.nii.gz) per subject in a folder: 'orig/' and 'pre/' (bias-corrected, aligned) subfolders each hold TOF.nii.gz (TOF-MRA) and a structural MR (struct.nii.gz; T1/T2/FLAIR), plus a per-subject aneurysms.nii.gz label mask and a location.txt listing aneurysm center voxels.
details.splits: Public training set: 113 cases (93 with >=1 untreated aneurysm + 20 controls; 129 UIAs total). Hidden test set: 141 cases (153 UIAs), used only by organizers. No separate official validation split.
details.extra: Voxel-wise annotations were drawn by two radiologists; label-2 (treated) aneurysms are present in some masks but are not counted in scoring, so many users remap them to background before training.

name: CADA
full_name: Cerebral Aneurysm Detection and Analysis Challenge 2020
segmentation_detail: aneurysm center positions and segmented masks; rupture state
description: 3D rotational X-ray angiography volumes from 115 patients with aneurysm locations, segmentations and rupture status.

label_schema: Aneurysm segmentation: voxel masks marking each aneurysm (per-aneurysm foreground vs background) plus corresponding STL surface meshes
Aneurysm detection: aneurysm center positions / locations (bounding boxes are derivable from the segmentation masks)
Rupture state (binary per aneurysm): ruptured vs not-ruptured, distributed ~42% ruptured vs 58% not ruptured
Source images: 3D rotational angiography (3D DSA) volumes of cerebral vasculature
details.file_structure: Per-case angiography image volumes with matching aneurysm segmentation labels provided both as image (mask) files and as STL surface meshes; rupture state is provided separately as a table listing each aneurysm with its rupture label.
details.splits: Training: 109 datasets containing 127 annotated aneurysms; test set released as image data only (no public labels).
details.extra: The challenge spans three tasks (detection, segmentation, rupture risk estimation) over the same cohort; the public test set contains images only, so ground-truth labels are available for the 109 training cases.

name: INSTANCE 2022
full_name: 2022 Intracranial Hemorrhage Segmentation on Non-Contrast head CT
segmentation_detail: voxel-level intracranial hemorrhage masks
description: MICCAI 2022 benchmark for 3D intracranial hemorrhage segmentation on non-contrast head CT with expert voxel labels.

label_schema: Single-class binary segmentation mask of intracranial hemorrhage region on Non-Contrast head CT (NCCT)
Label 1 - intracranial hemorrhage; Label 0 - background
Hemorrhage includes multiple bleed subtypes (intraparenchymal, intraventricular, subarachnoid, subdural, epidural) all merged into one foreground class (no per-subtype labels)
details.file_structure: 3D head CT volumes and their voxel-level hemorrhage masks are provided as NIfTI (.nii.gz) files, one image volume and one matching label volume per case.
details.splits: Training: 100 cases (with ground-truth masks); Validation/open-test: 30 cases (no public labels); held-out closed test: 70 cases. Total 200 volumes.
details.extra: Ground-truth annotations were refined/reviewed in collaboration with 10 experienced radiologists; official evaluation uses Dice (DSC), Hausdorff Distance (HD), and Relative Volume Difference (RVD).

name: VerSe
segmentation_detail: voxel masks of individual vertebrae (C1-L6 + sacrum)
description: MICCAI 2019-2020 challenge with spine CT, per-vertebra segmentation masks and vertebral centroid keypoints.

label_schema: Per-vertebra integer masks: 1–7 cervical (C1–C7), 8–19 thoracic (T1–T12), 20–25 lumbar (L1–L6), 26 sacrum, 28 T13 variant
Vertebral centroid keypoints (JSON) per label
details.file_structure: Per scan: *_ct.nii.gz, *_seg.nii.gz and *_ctd.json (centroids).
details.splits: VerSe'19 + '20 combined: 374 scans across train/val/test

name: RSNA 2022 Cervical Spine Fracture
full_name: RSNA 2022 Cervical Spine Fracture Detection Challenge
segmentation_detail: vertebral-level masks for a subset; fracture presence/level labels
description: Large public adult cervical-spine CT dataset with fracture presence/level annotations and vertebral segmentation for a subset.

label_schema: train.csv study-level target 'patient_overall' (binary; 1 = any cervical fracture present in the study)
train.csv per-vertebra fracture labels: C1, C2, C3, C4, C5, C6, C7 (each binary; 1 = that cervical vertebra is fractured)
Segmentation masks (subset): voxel value 1-7 = cervical vertebrae C1 through C7 respectively
Segmentation masks (subset): voxel values 8-19 = thoracic vertebrae T1 through T12; 0 = background
train_bounding_boxes.csv: 2D fracture bounding boxes on individual slices (columns StudyInstanceUID, slice_number, x, y, width, height) for a subset of studies
details.file_structure: CT scans stored as DICOM slices under train_images/<StudyInstanceUID>/<slice>.dcm (one folder per study); study-level and per-vertebra binary fracture labels in train.csv; per-study vertebra segmentation masks provided as NIfTI (.nii) files in the segmentations/ folder for a subset; slice-level fracture bounding boxes in train_bounding_boxes.csv.
details.splits: ~2,019 training studies with public labels; test set images are held out/hidden with no public labels; no official validation split.
details.extra: Segmentation (vertebral-level) masks are provided for only a small subset (~87 studies) and bounding-box fracture annotations only for a subset of training studies, so pixel-level and localization supervision is far sparser than the study-level fracture labels; some scans were defaced for privacy.

name: Calgary-Campinas CC-359
segmentation_detail: skull-stripping masks and hippocampus segmentations
description: Multi-vendor, multi-field-strength T1w brain MRI of 359 adults with skull-stripping and hippocampus masks plus raw k-space.

label_schema: Skull-stripping / brain masks: binary foreground mask (label 1 = brain, 0 = background) for 359 T1w volumes
Skull-stripping silver-standard: STAPLE-fused probability mask combined from 8 automated methods (HWA, ROBEX, BET, OptiBET, ANTs, BSE, MBWSS, BEaST), plus a thresholded binary mask
Skull-stripping: 12 subjects additionally have manual (expert) brain-mask segmentations
Hippocampus masks: binary hippocampus segmentation (label 1 = hippocampus) for 357 volumes (subjects CC0303 and CC0309 excluded)
Hippocampus silver-standard: STAPLE-fused mask from 3 tools (FreeSurfer, FSL/FIRST, Hippodeep); hippocampal subfield labels also provided via FreeSurfer
Brain-tissue silver-standard segmentations for white matter, gray matter, and CSF
details.file_structure: Per-subject T1-weighted volumes and their masks are stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz), organized into folders by annotation task (skull-stripping, hippocampus, tissue); each subject has an ID like CC0001 and separate mask files (e.g. STAPLE probability mask, binary mask, and where available manual segmentations).
details.extra: The 359 subjects are acquired across 3 vendors (GE, Philips, Siemens) at both 1.5T and 3T (six scanner/field-strength groups), making it a multi-vendor domain-shift benchmark; newer companion benchmarks on the same site add hypothalamus and thalamus masks that partly draw on other datasets (IXI, OASIS, MiLI, HCP) rather than only CC-359 subjects.

name: Mindboggle-101
segmentation_detail: manually labeled cortical (DKT) and subcortical structures
description: Largest collection of free, manually labeled human brain MRI surfaces and volumes using the DKT cortical protocol.

label_schema: Manually-edited whole-brain segmentation following the Desikan-Killiany-Tourville (DKT) cortical labeling protocol, using FreeSurfer-style integer label values (left hemisphere = 1000-series, right = 2000-series)
DKT31 protocol = 31 cortical gyral labels per hemisphere (four regions removed from the original 35-region Desikan-Killiany protocol); a coarser DKT25 variant merges some subdivided gyri

Left-hemisphere cortical labels (1002-1035): 1002 caudal anterior cingulate, 1003 caudal middle frontal, 1005 cuneus, 1006 entorhinal, 1007 fusiform, 1008 inferior parietal, 1009 inferior temporal, 1010 isthmus cingulate, 1011 lateral occipital, 1012 lateral orbitofrontal, 1013 lingual, 1014 medial orbitofrontal, 1015 middle temporal, 1016 parahippocampal, 1017 paracentral, 1018 pars opercularis, 1019 pars orbitalis, 1020 pars triangularis, 1021 pericalcarine, 1022 postcentral, 1023 posterior cingulate, 1024 precentral, 1025 precuneus, 1026 rostral anterior cingulate, 1027 rostral middle frontal, 1028 superior frontal, 1029 superior parietal, 1030 superior temporal, 1031 supramarginal, 1034 transverse temporal, 1035 insula
Right-hemisphere cortical labels use the mirrored 2002-2035 values with the same region names (e.g. 2002 right caudal anterior cingulate, 2035 right insula)

Subcortical / non-cortical structures follow FreeSurfer aseg integer values, e.g. 16 brainstem; 10-13 & 49-52 thalamus/caudate/putamen/pallidum; 17-18 & 53-54 hippocampus/amygdala; 26 & 58 accumbens; ventricles 4,5,7,14,15,43,44; plus cerebellar labels
details.file_structure: Each of the 101 subjects is a folder containing the labeled brain in multiple formats: integer-valued NIfTI label volumes, VTK surface meshes with per-vertex labels, and FreeSurfer files (surfaces plus .annot cortical annotations); the raw/normalized T1 anatomical volume is included alongside the label maps.
details.extra: 101 brains pooled from named cohorts: NKI-RS-22 (22), NKI-TRT-20 (20), MMRR-21 (21), MMRR-3T7T-2 (2), HLN-12 (12), OASIS-TRT-20 (20), Twins-2 (2), Afterthought-1 (1), and Colin27 (1). Labels were seeded by a FreeSurfer classifier atlas then manually edited by human raters to conform to the DKT protocol; the dataset also ships derived DKT40/OASIS-TRT-20 joint-fusion atlases and templates.

name: fastMRI Brain
full_name: NYU Langone & FAIR fastMRI Brain Dataset
multi_sequence_detail: axial T1-weighted, T2-weighted and FLAIR
description: Large collection of fully-sampled raw k-space and DICOM brain MRIs (T1/T2/FLAIR) for accelerated MRI reconstruction research.

label_schema: 2D bounding-box + study-level pathology annotations (fastMRI+ companion), one row = one annotation
CSV columns: file, slice, study_level, x, y, width, height, label
Bounding-box labels: study_level='No', with pixel coords x,y,width,height on a given slice
Study-level labels: study_level='Yes', no box coordinates (whole-study finding)

~27 pathology label classes, including: Nonspecific white matter lesion, Likely cysts, Normal for age, Mass, Resection cavity, Craniotomy, Lacunar infarct, Extra-axial mass, Edema, Craniectomy with Cranioplasty, Nonspecific lesion, Paranasal sinus opacification, Possible artifact, Posttreatment change, Dural thickening, Encephalomalacia, Enlarged ventricles, Global Ischemia
Reviewed-but-normal studies appear in brain_file_list.csv with no entry in brain.csv (no findings)
details.file_structure: Base fastMRI brain images are per-volume HDF5 (.h5) files holding raw multi-coil k-space (plus DICOM); the labels are a separate fastMRI+ companion repo with plain CSV files (brain.csv for annotations, brain_file_list.csv for reviewed files), keyed to volumes by the 'file' column and slice index — no DICOM-SEG/RTSTRUCT/NIfTI.
details.extra: Annotations cover ~1000 brain studies; each image was labeled by a SINGLE radiologist without other views, so labels are explicitly not clinical ground truth. Note: DICOM conversion flips pixel arrays up/down, which matters for interpreting box coordinates.

name: MRBrainS
multi_sequence_detail: T1-weighted, T1 inversion recovery, and T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: brain tissue (GM, WM, CSF) and up to ten structures
description: MICCAI multi-sequence 3T brain MRI challenge for segmenting gray matter, white matter and CSF in elderly subjects.

label_schema: Voxel-wise segmentation labels (single integer-valued label volume, 10 label values):
Label 1 - Cortical gray matter
Label 2 - Basal ganglia
Label 3 - White matter
Label 4 - White matter lesions
Label 5 - Cerebrospinal fluid in the extracerebral space
Label 6 - Ventricles
Label 7 - Cerebellum
Label 8 - Brain stem
Label 9 - Infarction (annotated but excluded/masked out during scoring)
Label 10 - Other (annotated but excluded/masked out during scoring)
Official challenge scores only 8 tissue classes (labels 1-8); labels 9 and 10 are removed before evaluation. A reduced 3-class variant (CSF/GM/WM) is also used (bundling structures into GM, WM, CSF).
Evaluation metrics per label: Dice Similarity Coefficient, 95% Hausdorff distance, Volume Similarity
details.file_structure: Per-subject folders (e.g. training/<subject>/) containing multi-modal MRI scans under a 'pre' subfolder (reg_T1.nii.gz, IR.nii.gz, FLAIR.nii.gz - registered/bias-corrected T1, T1-IR inversion recovery, and T2-FLAIR) plus the manual reference segmentation segm.nii.gz; all volumes are NIfTI (.nii.gz), and predictions are submitted as result.nii.gz.
details.splits: 7 subjects with publicly released reference annotations for training; the remaining scans form a hidden test set evaluated only via online submission (test ground truth not released).
details.extra: Multi-sequence 3T MRI (T1, T1-IR, FLAIR) from the same scanner as MRBrainS13; annotations include pathology (white matter lesions, infarction) but infarction and 'other' are masked out of the official 8-class scoring.

name: iSeg
multi_sequence_detail: T1-weighted and T2-weighted
segmentation_detail: infant brain tissue (WM, GM, CSF)
description: MICCAI challenge with T1/T2 MRI of 6-month-old infants (isointense phase) and manual WM/GM/CSF tissue labels.

label_schema: Voxel-wise tissue segmentation of 6-month infant brain MRI (isointense stage)
Label 0 - Background (everything outside the brain)
Label 1 - Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF)
Label 2 - Gray matter (GM)
Label 3 - White matter (WM)
details.file_structure: Each subject has co-registered T1-weighted and T2-weighted volumes plus a manual label volume, distributed as Analyze-format image pairs (.hdr/.img) named subject-<n>-T1, subject-<n>-T2, and subject-<n>-label (ground-truth label only released for training subjects).
details.splits: 10 training subjects, 13 validation subjects, 16 testing subjects (testing drawn from 3 sites: 6 BCP, 5 Stanford, 5 Emory)
details.extra: iSeg-2019 is a multi-site extension of iSeg-2017; targets the isointense phase (~6 months) where WM/GM contrast is very low, and ground-truth labels were produced by manual annotation. Note the dataset is infant (6-month) brain, not fetal brain.

name: FeTA
full_name: Fetal Tissue Annotation and Segmentation Dataset
segmentation_detail: seven fetal brain tissues
description: First public dataset of super-resolution reconstructed fetal brain MRI with manual multi-tissue annotations.

label_schema: Multi-class voxel segmentation label map (single dseg NIfTI per subject) with 7 fetal brain tissue classes, values 1-7 plus 0 = background
Label 1 - External Cerebrospinal Fluid (eCSF)
Label 2 - Grey Matter (cortical gray matter)
Label 3 - White Matter
Label 4 - Ventricles (lateral ventricles / CSF)
Label 5 - Cerebellum
Label 6 - Deep Grey Matter
Label 7 - Brainstem (brainstem / spinal cord)
details.file_structure: BIDS-organized folders (sub-XXX/anat/) containing gzipped NIfTI files: a T2-weighted super-resolution brain reconstruction (e.g. sub-XXX_rec-{mial,irtk}_T2w.nii.gz) and a matching integer-valued segmentation label map (sub-XXX_rec-..._dseg.nii.gz), each 256x256x256 voxels at 0.5 mm isotropic, with a participants.tsv listing gestational age and neurotypical/pathological status.
details.splits: Publicly released manually-annotated training set of 80 subjects (FeTA 2021), expanded to 120 subjects from two institutions (Zurich + Vienna) for FeTA 2022; the multi-institution test set (up to 180 cases across 5 sites) is held out and not publicly released. Original 2021 data descriptor described 50 subjects.
details.extra: Reconstructions were produced with two super-resolution pipelines (MIAL and Simple IRTK), indicated by the rec- entity in filenames; dataset includes both neurotypical and pathological fetal brains across a range of gestational ages, with per-subject gestational age and pathology metadata in participants.tsv.

name: NIH ChestX-ray14
paired_text_detail: 14 disease labels text-mined from reports (reports not released)
description: 112,120 frontal chest X-rays from 30,805 patients labeled with 14 thoracic findings via NLP, plus 984 hand-drawn bounding boxes.

label_schema: 14 binary disease labels (NLP-mined): Atelectasis, Cardiomegaly, Effusion, Infiltration, Mass, Nodule, Pneumonia, Pneumothorax, Consolidation, Edema, Emphysema, Fibrosis, Pleural_Thickening, Hernia
984 bounding boxes (8 pathologies) in BBox_List_2017.csv
details.file_structure: 1024×1024 PNGs + Data_Entry_2017.csv (labels, patient age/sex/view) + train_val/test split lists.
details.splits: ~86k train-val / ~25k test (official patient-wise split)

name: CheXpert
full_name: CheXpert: Large Chest Radiograph Dataset with Uncertainty Labels
paired_text_detail: 14 labels from a rule-based labeler; free-text reports in the separate CheXpert Plus release
description: 224,316 chest radiographs from 65,240 Stanford patients labeled for 14 observations as positive, negative or uncertain.

label_schema: 14 observations, each labeled 1 (positive) / 0 (negative) / -1 (uncertain) / blank:
No Finding, Enlarged Cardiomediastinum, Cardiomegaly, Lung Opacity, Lung Lesion, Edema, Consolidation, Pneumonia, Atelectasis, Pneumothorax, Pleural Effusion, Pleural Other, Fracture, Support Devices
details.file_structure: Patient/study/view JPEG hierarchy + train.csv / valid.csv with the 14 label columns and view metadata.
details.splits: 223,414 train / 234 val / 668 test (expert-labeled)

name: MIMIC-CXR
paired_text_detail: free-text English radiology reports, one per study
description: 377,110 DICOM chest radiographs from Beth Israel Deaconess paired with de-identified free-text radiology reports.

label_schema: Free-text reports (per study .txt): sections incl. FINDINGS and IMPRESSION
Derived CheXpert & NegBio label CSVs (14 categories, in MIMIC-CXR-JPG)
details.file_structure: p1x/pXXXXXXXX/sYYYYYYYY/ folders with DICOMs + a matching sYYYYYYYY.txt report; metadata CSV links study→view→labels.
details.splits: Official train/validate/test split provided

name: MIMIC-CXR-JPG
full_name: MIMIC-CXR-JPG — chest radiographs with structured labels
paired_text_detail: structured CheXpert & NegBio labels (14 categories); reports live in companion MIMIC-CXR
description: JPG-converted derivative of MIMIC-CXR with structured CheXpert and NegBio labels across 14 categories.

label_schema: Image-level multi-label classification derived from free-text reports; 14 CheXpert findings: Atelectasis, Cardiomegaly, Consolidation, Edema, Enlarged Cardiomediastinum, Fracture, Lung Lesion, Lung Opacity, Pleural Effusion, Pneumonia, Pneumothorax, Pleural Other, Support Devices, No Finding
Each finding takes one of four values: 1.0 = positive/present, 0.0 = negative/absent, -1.0 = uncertain, blank = not mentioned in report
Two independent label sets provided, generated by different NLP labelers: CheXpert (mimic-cxr-2.0.0-chexpert.csv) and NegBio (mimic-cxr-2.0.0-negbio.csv), each with the same 14 finding columns
Labels are keyed by subject_id and study_id (study-level), not per-pixel; no segmentation masks or bounding boxes
details.file_structure: JPG images (converted from DICOM, quality 95, histogram-equalized) laid out as files/p<10>/p<subject_id>/s<study_id>/<dicom_id>.jpg across 10 top-level folders; labels and metadata are gzip-compressed CSVs at the root (mimic-cxr-2.0.0-chexpert.csv.gz, -negbio.csv.gz, -metadata.csv.gz, -split.csv.gz).
details.splits: Official split file (mimic-cxr-2.0.0-split.csv.gz) assigns each study to 'train', 'validate', or 'test'; dataset totals 377,110 JPG images over 227,827 studies, but exact per-split counts were not confirmed from the source.
details.extra: A separate radiologist-annotated test-set label file (mimic-cxr-2.1.0-test-set-labeled.csv) provides expert ground-truth for the test split, distinct from the NLP-derived CheXpert/NegBio labels; requires PhysioNet credentialed access and a signed data use agreement.

name: PadChest
full_name: PadChest: chest X-ray dataset with multi-label annotated reports
paired_text_detail: Spanish free-text reports; ~27% labels manually annotated, rest by RNN; UMLS-mapped
description: 160,868 16-bit chest X-rays paired with Spanish reports and 174 findings, 19 diagnoses and 104 location labels mapped to UMLS.

label_schema: Multi-label image-level classification (no segmentation masks)
174 different radiographic findings labels (e.g. pneumonia, effusion, cardiomegaly, nodule, etc.)
19 differential diagnoses labels
104 anatomic location / localization terms paired to findings
All labels mapped to standard UMLS terminology as Concept Unique Identifiers (CUIs), organized as a hierarchical taxonomy
Each study also includes the free-text Spanish radiology report
6 projection/position views recorded per image (e.g. PA, AP, lateral, AP-horizontal, pediatric, costal)
details.file_structure: 16-bit grayscale PNG images (converted from DICOM) stored in numbered zip folders, with all annotations, DICOM metadata, UMLS-mapped labels, localizations and reports provided in a single CSV file (PADCHEST_chest_x_ray_images_labels_160K_01.02.19.csv).
details.extra: Only ~27% of the studies (about 27,593 images) were manually annotated by trained physicians; the remaining ~73% were labeled automatically by an attention-based RNN text-labeling model (0.93 micro-F1 on a held-out test set), so automatic labels carry some noise. Dataset totals ~160,000 images from 67,000 patients (2009-2017, Hospital San Juan, Spain).

name: VinDr-CXR
full_name: VinDr-CXR: chest X-rays with radiologist annotations
paired_text_detail: radiologist bounding boxes and image-level labels; no reports
description: 18,000 DICOM chest radiographs with radiologist bounding boxes for 22 local findings and 6 global labels.

label_schema: 22 local labels (annotated with bounding boxes): Aortic enlargement, Atelectasis, Calcification, Cardiomegaly, Clavicle fracture, Consolidation, Edema, Emphysema, Enlarged PA, Interstitial lung disease (ILD), Infiltration, Lung cavity, Lung cyst, Lung opacity, Mediastinal shift, Nodule/Mass, Pulmonary fibrosis, Pneumothorax, Pleural thickening, Pleural effusion, Rib fracture, Other lesion
6 global labels (image-level diagnoses): Lung tumor, Pneumonia, Tuberculosis, Other diseases, COPD, No finding
Object detection: bounding boxes (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max) per local finding class
Image-level classification: binary (0/1) presence labels for global diagnoses

name: VinDr-PCXR
paired_text_detail: lesion-level bounding boxes and image-level labels; no reports
description: Pediatric chest X-ray dataset of 9,125 DICOM studies annotated with lesion-level boxes for 36 findings and 15 diseases.

label_schema: Dual annotation scheme: 36 local findings (bounding boxes) + 15 global diagnoses (image-level)

36 local bounding-box findings, incl.: Boot-shaped heart, Peribronchovascular interstitial opacity (PIO), Reticulonodular opacity, Bronchial thickening, Enlarged PA, Cardiomegaly, Intrathoracic digestive structure, Diffuse alveolar opacity, Consolidation, Mediastinal shift, Anterior mediastinal mass, Other nodule/mass, Dextrocardia, Aortic enlargement, Pleural effusion, Stomach on the right side, Atelectasis, Calcification, Interstitial lung disease (ILD), Lung hyperinflation, Egg on string sign, Pulmonary fibrosis, Infiltration, Lung cavity, Pneumothorax, Edema, Pleural thickening, Clavicle fracture, Chest wall mass, Lung cyst, Emphysema, Bronchiectasis, Expanded edges of anterior ribs, Paravertebral mass, Other opacity, Other lesion

15 global image-level diagnoses: No finding, Bronchitis, Broncho-pneumonia, Bronchiolitis, Situs inversus, Pneumonia, Pleuro-pneumonia, Diaphragmatic hernia, Tuberculosis, Congenital emphysema, CPAM (congenital pulmonary airway malformation), Hyaline membrane disease, Mediastinal tumor, Lung tumor, Other diseases
Local labels stored as bounding boxes (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max); global labels stored as binary present/absent vectors
Cohort: pediatric patients (aged 10 years and younger)
details.file_structure: DICOM images (filenames = anonymized SOP Instance UID-derived IDs) with labels in CSV files: annotations_train.csv / annotations_test.csv hold image ID, radiologist ID, finding name and bounding-box coordinates; image_labels_train.csv / image_labels_test.csv hold image ID, radiologist ID and binary finding/diagnosis labels.
details.splits: Train: 7,728 scans; Test: 1,397 scans; Total: 9,125
details.extra: Annotated by 3 pediatric radiologists (>=15 years experience) via the VinDr-Lab web platform; each training image was labeled by a single reader (no per-image multi-reader overlap in the training set).

name: RSNA Pneumonia Detection
description: Frontal chest radiographs in DICOM with radiologist bounding boxes localizing lung opacities indicative of pneumonia.

label_schema: Object detection: bounding boxes marking regions of lung opacity suspicious for pneumonia
Binary target column: Target=1 (pneumonia/lung opacity present, box provided) vs Target=0 (no opacity, empty box fields)
Bounding box columns: x, y (upper-left corner in pixels), width, height
Companion 3-class label (stage_2_detailed_class_info.csv): 'Normal', 'No Lung Opacity / Not Normal', 'Lung Opacity'
details.file_structure: Chest radiographs are stored as DICOM files (one per patientId); labels are provided as CSVs — stage_2_train_labels.csv (patientId, x, y, width, height, Target; multiple rows per patient when several boxes exist) and stage_2_detailed_class_info.csv (patientId, class).
details.extra: Data was released in two stages; the winning approaches trained on stage-1 data. The dataset re-annotates NIH ChestX-ray14 images with expert box annotations, and a single positive image can have multiple opacity boxes (one CSV row each).

name: SIIM-ACR Pneumothorax
segmentation_detail: pixel-level pneumothorax masks (RLE)
description: Chest radiographs in DICOM with run-length-encoded segmentation masks marking pneumothorax regions.

label_schema: Binary segmentation of pneumothorax (collapsed lung) region on chest radiographs
Single foreground class: pneumothorax (region of air in the pleural space)
Positive case: run-length-encoded (RLE) pixel mask of the pneumothorax region
Negative case: EncodedPixels = -1 (no pneumothorax present)
Task also usable as image-level classification (pneumothorax present vs. absent)
details.file_structure: Images are DICOM chest X-rays at 1024x1024 resolution stored in hierarchical folders (dicom-images-train/ and dicom-images-test/); labels are provided in train-rle.csv with columns ImageId (matching the DICOM SOP Instance UID) and EncodedPixels (RLE mask string, or -1 for negative cases).
details.extra: An image can have multiple RLE rows in train-rle.csv when more than one separate pneumothorax region is annotated; masks were expert-annotated by SIIM/ACR radiologists. Exact official train/test image counts could not be verified from the sources fetched.

name: Open-i / Indiana University CXR
full_name: Indiana University Chest X-ray Collection (NLM Open-i)
paired_text_detail: de-identified free-text reports (indication, findings, impression) with MeSH/RadLex
description: Open NLM collection of frontal/lateral chest radiographs paired with de-identified reports, widely used for report generation.

label_schema: Free-text radiology reports (not fixed-class labels); each report has labeled sections: Comparison, Indication, Findings, Impression
Manual controlled-vocabulary tags per report: MeSH (Medical Subject Headings) terms plus RadLex codes covering imaging terms outside MeSH
Coders assigned 101 distinct MeSH codes and 76 RadLex codes across abnormal reports; only positive/present findings were coded (normal vs abnormal two-pass scheme)
Each MeSH tag has a major term plus optional attributes (e.g., anatomy, severity/degree, location) - e.g. 'Cardiomegaly/mild', 'Pulmonary Atelectasis/base/left'
Automatic MTI (Medical Text Indexer) MeSH tags also provided alongside the manual tags
No pixel-level segmentation or bounding-box annotations
details.file_structure: Distributed via Open-i as one XML file per report (containing the Comparison/Indication/Findings/Impression sections plus <MeSH> major/automatic tag elements and image ID links) with the corresponding frontal/lateral chest images as PNG files; common redistributions also flatten reports and image projection info into CSV files.
details.extra: Public de-identified subset is 3,955 reports and 7,470 images (from an initial 3,996 reports / 8,121 images); reports were coded independently by two trained annotators with a third adjudicating disagreements. No official train/val/test split is provided.

name: Shenzhen Hospital CXR Set
full_name: Shenzhen No.3 People's Hospital Chest X-ray Set (NLM TB)
paired_text_detail: brief radiologist clinical readings as text files
description: 662 frontal chest radiographs from Shenzhen labeled normal or tuberculosis for TB screening research.

label_schema: Image-level binary classification: TB status encoded as the last character of the filename
Suffix 0 = normal chest X-ray (326 images)
Suffix 1 = abnormal, showing manifestations of tuberculosis (336 images)
Companion clinical reading per image (free-text .txt): patient age, gender, and radiologist's description of the lung abnormality if any (e.g. 'male 46yrs, bilateral PTB')
details.file_structure: Frontal chest X-rays stored as PNG files (~3K x 3K px) named CHNCXR_####_0.png (normal) or CHNCXR_####_1.png (TB), each paired with an identically named .txt file holding the patient's age, gender, and clinical reading.
details.extra: No official train/test split. Original release ships only image-level labels plus text clinical readings; lung segmentation masks exist only for a subset via third-party contributions (e.g. Stirenko et al.), not part of the NLM release.

name: Montgomery County CXR Set
full_name: Montgomery County Chest X-ray Set (NLM TB)
paired_text_detail: brief radiologist clinical readings as text files
segmentation_detail: left/right lung segmentation masks
description: 138 frontal chest radiographs from Montgomery County's TB program, labeled normal or TB with lung segmentation masks.

label_schema: Binary TB classification (138 frontal CXRs): 80 normal, 58 abnormal with TB manifestations
Class encoded in filename suffix: MCUCXR_####_0 = normal, MCUCXR_####_1 = abnormal (TB)
Left lung segmentation mask - binary (foreground = lung region)
Right lung segmentation mask - binary (foreground = lung region)
Per-image clinical reading (text): patient age, gender, and radiologist-noted lung abnormality/diagnosis
details.file_structure: Chest X-rays are 12-bit grayscale PNGs (approx. 4020x4892) named MCUCXR_####_X.png (X=0 normal, 1 abnormal), with matching per-image clinical .txt readings, and manually drawn binary lung masks stored under separate leftMask/ and rightMask/ folders using the same filenames.
details.extra: Lung masks were manually segmented under radiologist supervision (following heart/diaphragm anatomical boundaries) and are provided as separate left/right masks; DICOM versions are available on request. Collected from the Montgomery County, Maryland TB screening program (Jaeger et al., 2014).

name: Object-CXR
full_name: object-CXR: Foreign Objects on Chest X-rays
segmentation_detail: foreign objects annotated with boxes/ellipses/polygons
description: 10,000 chest X-rays from Chinese township hospitals annotated for foreign objects; now distributed via GitHub.

label_schema: Single target class: foreign object on frontal chest X-ray (presence + location); no multi-class object taxonomy
Each object encoded by a shape-type prefix: 0 = rectangle/bounding box (0 x1 y1 x2 y2), 1 = ellipse (1 x1 y1 x2 y2), 2 = polygon (2 x1 y1 x2 y2 ... xn yn)
Coordinates are pixel positions, Cartesian, origin (0,0) at upper-left, x rightward and y downward
Empty annotation = image contains no foreign object (negative case)
Only foreign objects within the lung fields are annotated; support devices were explicitly excluded
details.file_structure: CSV annotation files (train/dev/test) each with two columns, image_path and annotation; the annotation string lists one or more objects, each object separated by a semicolon, each object being a shape-type-prefixed list of pixel coordinates; images are JPG frontal chest X-rays referenced by their path.
details.splits: Train 8,000 (4,000 with objects / 4,000 without); Validation (dev) 1,000 (500 / 500); Test 1,000 (500 / 500); 10,000 total.
details.extra: Images sourced from ~300 township hospitals in China and annotated by 12 medically-trained radiologists (1-3 years experience); released for non-commercial research use, with training and validation sets distributed via Google Drive.

name: CANDID-PTX
paired_text_detail: anonymized free-text radiology reports
segmentation_detail: RLE masks for pneumothorax, acute rib fracture, and intercostal chest tubes
description: New Zealand chest radiographs with segmentation for pneumothorax, rib fractures and chest tubes plus free-text reports.

label_schema: Segmentation mask (run-length-encoded) - pneumothorax
Segmentation mask (run-length-encoded) - acute rib fracture
Segmentation mask (run-length-encoded) - intercostal chest tube
Free-text anonymized radiology report per study (report-level text label)
details.file_structure: 19,237 anonymized adult chest x-rays as 1024x1024 DICOM images, with segmentation annotations converted to run-length-encoded (RLE) masks stored in CSV files and free-text radiology reports provided alongside; patient identifiers and acquisition dates are preserved in filenames for longitudinal analysis.
details.extra: Sourced from Dunedin Hospital, New Zealand (2010-2020) and manually labeled by medical professionals; as of Feb 2024 access is restricted to Health New Zealand and no longer publicly downloadable (figshare hosts only screenshots).

name: BRAX
full_name: BRAX, a Brazilian labeled chest X-ray dataset
paired_text_detail: 14 CheXpert-style labels from NLP on Portuguese reports (reports not provided)
description: Brazilian chest X-ray dataset of 40,967 images (Hospital Albert Einstein) in DICOM and PNG with 14 automatically extracted labels.

label_schema: 14 image-level findings (CheXpert-style schema adapted to Portuguese reports): Atelectasis, Cardiomegaly, Consolidation, Edema, Enlarged Cardiomediastinum, Fracture, Lung Lesion, Lung Opacity, No Finding, Pleural Effusion, Pleural Other, Pneumonia, Pneumothorax, Support Devices
Each finding uses a 3-value encoding: 1 = positive, 0 = negative/negation, -1 = uncertain (blank/NaN = not mentioned)
details.file_structure: Chest X-ray images are provided as both DICOM and PNG in a hierarchical folder tree (patient id / study / series / image); a master_spreadsheet.csv holds the per-image finding labels along with image paths, view position, and patient demographics.
details.extra: Labels are not manual annotations: they were generated automatically by NLP from free-text Portuguese radiology reports, adapting the CheXpert label extraction algorithm and a Portuguese-adapted NegEx (negation/uncertainty triggers translated and validated by Brazilian radiologists). ~40,967 images / 24,959 studies / 19,351 patients.

name: BIMCV-COVID19
paired_text_detail: Spanish free-text radiographic reports, UMLS-mapped findings
segmentation_detail: semantic segmentation of findings for a small subset
description: Large Valencian COVID-19 dataset with chest X-rays and CT plus Spanish reports, UMLS-coded findings and PCR/serology results.

label_schema: Image-level multi-label findings: 336 distinct labels total (the PadChest label set plus two added terms: 'COVID-19' CUI C5203670 and 'COVID-19 uncertain' CUI C5203671)
Labels cover diagnoses, radiological findings, and anatomical localizations, each mapped to UMLS Concept Unique Identifiers (CUIs) and organized in semantic concept trees
Labels auto-extracted from Spanish radiology reports (images not used) by a bidirectional-LSTM-with-attention multi-label text classifier (the PadChest labeler, retrained with COVID terms)
Example finding CUIs: infiltrates C0277877, interstitial pattern C2073538, ground glass pattern C3544344, alveolar pattern C1332240, consolidation C0521530, air bronchogram C3669021

Most frequent findings (count): COVID-19 (802), increased density (657), pneumonia (605), unchanged (494), consolidation (357), infiltrates (346), interstitial pattern (317), alveolar pattern (291), normal (284), ground glass pattern (240), cardiomegaly (166), pleural effusion (149)

Pixel-level semantic segmentation ROI subset uses 14 finding classes (Table 4): 1 Ground glass XRay, 2 Consolidation XRay, 3 Pleural effusion XRay, 4 Interstitial RX, 5 Ground glass CT, 6 Crazy paving CT, 7 Inverted halo CT, 8 Vascular thickening in ground-glass CT, 9 Ground glass and consolidation focus CT, 10 Nodular consolidation CT, 11 Normal consolidation CT, 12 Sprouting/tree-in-bud pattern CT, 13 Pleural effusion CT, 14 No RX or CT findings
Each image also carries per-study diagnostic-test labels: PCR / IgG / IgM results, each positive, negative, or indeterminate
details.file_structure: Organized in MIDS format (a BIDS extension) as subject/session/series folders; images are extracted from DICOM and stored as 16-bit PNG (2D) and/or .nii.gz, per-series DICOM metadata as JSON, reports plus extracted CUI disease/location labels in TSV (derivatives/EHR/labels/labels_COVID-19_posi.tsv), the CUI hierarchy in a CSV tree file, diagnostic tests in il_reg_covid.csv, and the small pixel-level segmentation subset stored as XNAT OHIF Viewer XML ROI-path files.
details.extra: The pixel-level semantic segmentation subset is very small: in the first iteration (v1, 2020) only 10 chest images were manually annotated with ROIs by a team of 8 radiologists using the XNAT OHIF Viewer; the dataset is incremental, so later iterations may include more.

name: COVID-19 Radiography Database
segmentation_detail: lung segmentation masks for all images
description: Kaggle chest X-ray database of 21,165 images across COVID-19, Normal, Lung Opacity and Viral Pneumonia classes with lung masks.

label_schema: Image-level classification into 4 classes
COVID-19 - 3,616 chest X-ray images
Normal (healthy) - 10,192 images
Lung Opacity (non-COVID lung infection) - 6,012 images
Viral Pneumonia - 1,345 images
Binary lung segmentation masks (lung region vs. background) provided for every image
details.file_structure: COVID-19_Radiography_Dataset/ holds one folder per class (COVID, Normal, Lung_Opacity, Viral Pneumonia), each split into an images/ subfolder of PNG chest X-rays (299x299) and a matching masks/ subfolder of PNG lung-segmentation masks (256x256), with per-class .metadata.xlsx files listing file names and sources.
details.extra: Total 21,165 images; no official train/val/test partition is provided. The masks are lung-field segmentations (not COVID lesion/infection masks); the related COVID-QU-Ex release adds pixel-level infection masks.

name: COVIDx CXR
description: Open benchmark chest X-ray dataset built by COVID-Net from multiple public sources, labeled COVID-19 / non-COVID pneumonia / normal.

label_schema: 3-class (COVIDx CXR-3A): normal, pneumonia, COVID-19
Binary variant (COVIDx CXR-3B): negative, positive (COVID-19)
Image-level classification labels only (no bounding boxes or segmentation masks)
details.file_structure: Chest X-ray images are stored as PNG/JPG files in separate train/ and test/ folders, with plain-text label files (train.txt / test.txt) where each line lists patient ID, image filename, class label, and source dataset.
details.splits: 3-class train: normal 8,085 / pneumonia 5,555 / COVID-19 15,994 = 29,634; 3-class test: normal 100 / pneumonia 100 / COVID-19 200 = 400. Binary train: negative 13,992 / positive 15,994 = 29,986; binary test: negative 200 / positive 200 = 400 (COVIDx CXR-3).
details.extra: Not a standalone image collection: COVIDx is a curated aggregation of several open source datasets (e.g. Kaggle collections and multiple public repositories) reconstructed via provided scripts; class composition and counts vary across released versions (CXR, CXR-2, CXR-3, CXR-4).

name: LIDC-IDRI
full_name: Lung Image Database Consortium and Image Database Resource Initiative
segmentation_detail: nodule contours from four thoracic radiologists (XML)
description: 1,018 thoracic CT scans with lesion markups and subjective nodule characteristic ratings from four radiologists.

label_schema: Per-nodule contours from up to 4 radiologists (no forced consensus)
Nodule categories: nodule ≥3mm, nodule <3mm, non-nodule ≥3mm
Subjective characteristics (1–5): subtlety, calcification, sphericity, margin, lobulation, spiculation, texture, malignancy
details.file_structure: DICOM image series per patient + one XML per scan holding each reader's nodule contours and characteristic ratings.
details.splits: No official split (1,018 scans); LUNA16 defines a 10-fold subset.

name: LUNA16
segmentation_detail: whole-lung masks plus nodule center coordinates and diameters
description: 888 CT scans derived from LIDC-IDRI with 1,186 nodule annotations for detection and false-positive reduction.

label_schema: annotations.csv — nodule center (x,y,z world coords) + diameter_mm, for nodules accepted by ≥3/4 radiologists
candidates_V2.csv — candidate locations with class 0/1 for false-positive reduction
seg-lungs-LUNA16 — whole-lung binary masks
details.file_structure: 888 CT volumes as .mhd/.raw (MetaImage), organized in 10 subsets for cross-validation.
details.splits: 10 predefined folds (subset0–subset9)

name: NSCLC-Radiomics
paired_text_detail: clinical outcome data (survival, stage, histology) in CSV; not free-text reports
segmentation_detail: radiation-oncologist gross tumor volume (GTV-1) as DICOM RTSTRUCT/SEG
description: Pretreatment CT from 422 NSCLC patients with tumor delineations and outcome data, used to build a prognostic radiomic signature.

label_schema: Manual radiation-oncologist delineations on pretreatment CT (named ROI contours, not integer-indexed)
GTV-1 - primary gross tumor volume
Lung (left/right lung organ-at-risk contours)
Heart (organ-at-risk contour)
Esophagus (organ-at-risk contour)
details.file_structure: DICOM throughout: per-patient folders each holding the CT image series plus a segmentation object, provided as DICOM RTSTRUCT (structure sets) and also as DICOM SEG (which contains a subset of the RTSTRUCT annotations).
details.extra: 422 NSCLC patients / 1,265 series / ~52,073 images (~35.8 GB); the DICOM SEG objects hold only a subset of the annotations available in the RTSTRUCT files.

name: NSCLC-Radiogenomics
paired_text_detail: clinical/demographic data, semantic tumor annotations, matched genomic data; not free-text reports
multi_sequence_detail: multi-modality CT and PET/CT with genomic profiling
segmentation_detail: tumor segmentation maps on CT
description: 211 NSCLC patients with CT and PET/CT, tumor segmentations and matched genomic data (RNA-seq, mutation status).

label_schema: Segmentation: single foreground class — primary NSCLC tumor volume delineated on CT (3D binary tumor mask)
Semantic image annotations: qualitative tumor characteristics scored by radiologists using a controlled vocabulary (delivered as AIM annotation objects)
Clinical/molecular tabular labels (CSV): demographics, exposure/smoking history, follow-up/survival, treatment, and molecular-test (mutation) status
Gene-expression labels: RNA-sequencing profiles for a 130-subject subset (available via NCBI GEO)
details.file_structure: Per-subject DICOM image series (CT and PET) with the primary-tumor segmentation stored as a DICOM-SEG object co-registered to the CT; semantic tumor annotations provided as AIM files (XML/ZIP), and clinical + molecular variables provided in a single downloadable CSV.
details.extra: Total collection is 211 subjects, but CT tumor segmentations and semantic AIM annotations cover only a subset of subjects (exact per-modality counts not stated on the TCIA page); RNA-seq expression data for 130 of the subjects is hosted separately on NCBI GEO.

name: RSNA-STR Pulmonary Embolism CT
segmentation_detail: exam- and slice-level PE annotations only
description: Large chest CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) dataset with expert PE annotations from the RSNA 2020 challenge.

label_schema: 14 binary labels total: 1 image(slice)-level + 13 exam-level; no pixel masks or bounding boxes
Image/slice-level: pe_present_on_image (1 = PE visible on that CT slice)
Exam-level status: negative_exam_for_pe, indeterminate (scan not evaluable for PE)
Exam-level PE location: rightsided_pe, leftsided_pe, central_pe
Exam-level PE chronicity: chronic_pe, acute_and_chronic_pe (acute-only is implied, no explicit column)
Exam-level RV/LV ratio: rv_lv_ratio_gte_1, rv_lv_ratio_lt_1
Exam-level QA / other findings: qa_motion, qa_contrast, flow_artifact, true_filling_defect_not_pe
Keys: StudyInstanceUID (exam), SeriesInstanceUID, SOPInstanceUID (slice)
details.file_structure: DICOM images are stored as train/<StudyInstanceUID>/<SeriesInstanceUID>/<SOPInstanceUID>.dcm, and every label lives in a single train.csv with one row per slice keyed by those three UID columns (the exam-level labels are repeated across all slices of a study); there are no mask/RTSTRUCT/NIfTI files.
details.extra: Despite the 'segmentation'/PE-annotation framing, the data contains NO pixel masks or bounding boxes: PE is annotated only as a per-slice binary flag (pe_present_on_image) plus 13 exam-level multilabel attributes, and the competition metric enforces a label-consistency hierarchy between the slice-level and exam-level labels; the test set labels are hidden.

name: LNDb
segmentation_detail: nodule segmentation masks with multiple radiologist annotations
description: CT lung nodule database of 294 scans from Porto with radiologist nodule annotations, segmentations and Fleischner scores.

label_schema: Nodule vs non-nodule flag per finding (1 = nodule, 0 = non-nodule)
Nodules split by size: nodule >=3mm (segmented) and nodule <3mm (centroid only)
3D binary segmentation masks: voxel value = the finding ID of that nodule (from trainNodules.csv), background = 0
Per-finding world (x,y,z) centroid coordinates and estimated nodule volume (mm^3)
Texture rating 1-5 (subjective: 1=ground-glass ... 5=solid; 0 for non-nodules)
Per-scan Fleischner follow-up score ground truth (trainFleischner.csv)
details.file_structure: CT scans and radiologist segmentations are MetaImage files (.mhd header + .raw data); each scan is LNDbXXXX.mhd and each reader's mask is LNDbXXXX_radR.mhd, with point/nodule annotations, merged consensus, and Fleischner labels in accompanying CSVs (trainNodules.csv, trainNodules_gt.csv, trainFleischner.csv).
details.splits: 294 CTs total, 58 withheld as test set (~236 training)
details.extra: Multi-reader: up to 5 radiologists (>=4 years experience) annotated each scan single-blind with no forced consensus; trainNodules.csv holds per-radiologist annotations while trainNodules_gt.csv holds merged agreement-based ground truth (agreement level = number of radiologists who marked the finding).
