name: MosMedData
full_name: MosMedData: Chest CT with COVID-19 Related Findings
segmentation_detail: binary masks of ground-glass opacities and consolidations (~50 studies)
description: 1,110 anonymized Moscow chest CT studies with COVID-19 severity grades and lesion masks for a small subset.

label_schema: Study-level severity class (CT-0 to CT-4), assigned by lung parenchymal involvement; each study belongs to exactly one
CT-0 - normal/not consistent with pneumonia (no COVID-19 findings): 254 studies (22.8%)
CT-1 - mild, ground-glass opacities, <=25% parenchymal involvement: 684 studies (61.6%)
CT-2 - moderate, ground-glass opacities, 25-50% involvement: 125 studies (11.3%)
CT-3 - severe, ground-glass opacities + consolidation, 50-75% involvement: 45 studies (4.1%)
CT-4 - critical, diffuse ground-glass opacities with consolidation and reticular changes, >=75% involvement: 2 studies (0.2%)

Pixel-level binary segmentation mask (n=50 studies): foreground (white=1) pixels mark ground-glass opacifications AND regions of consolidation together; background=0. Both finding types are one positive class, not separated into distinct integer labels
details.file_structure: Each CT study is a single gzipped NIfTI volume (.nii.gz) grouped in per-category folders (CT-0..CT-4), with only every 10th axial slice preserved; the 50 annotated studies have a matching binary mask volume also stored as .nii.gz (created with MedSeg web software).
details.extra: The binary masks do NOT distinguish ground-glass opacity from consolidation - both are merged into a single positive foreground class, so it is single-class (binary) segmentation rather than a 2-class scheme. Dataset licensed CC BY-NC-ND 3.0.

name: COVID-19-CT-Seg
full_name: COVID-19 CT Lung and Infection Segmentation Dataset
segmentation_detail: left lung, right lung and infection regions
description: 20 COVID-19 chest CT scans with radiologist-verified masks for left lung, right lung and infection.

label_schema: Voxel-wise segmentation masks (background = 0)
Lung_and_Infection_Mask (combined): Label 1 - left lung, Label 2 - right lung, Label 3 - infection
Lung_Mask: Label 1 - left lung, Label 2 - right lung
Infection_Mask: binary COVID-19 infection region (foreground = 1)
details.file_structure: 20 chest CT volumes and their masks are stored as 3D NIfTI (.nii.gz) files, distributed as separate zip archives on Zenodo (COVID-19-CT-Seg_20cases.zip for images plus Lung_Mask.zip, Infection_Mask.zip, and Lung_and_Infection_Mask.zip for the masks), with matching per-case filenames.
details.extra: 20 cases = 10 from Coronacases (full 3D volumes) + 10 from Radiopaedia (already lung-windowed to [-1250,250]); left lung, right lung, and infections were labeled by two radiologists and verified by an experienced radiologist. Zenodo provides no official train/test split; the associated COVID-19-CT-Seg-Benchmark repo defines its own splits.

name: NLST
segmentation_detail: core imaging has none; IDC offers some harmonized segmentations
other_labels: screening results and outcomes via CDAS
description: Low-dose chest CT screening exams from the National Lung Screening Trial, with full trial clinical data available via NCI CDAS.

label_schema: Core TCIA imaging carries NO voxel-level or per-image annotations: the ~203,099 low-dose CT DICOM series have no segmentations, bounding boxes, or nodule masks
Labels are participant/exam-level clinical outcomes delivered as separate clinical data files, not image-embedded annotations
Screening result label per annual low-dose CT round: positive vs negative screening test (3 annual rounds; reported 24.2% positive screens for low-dose CT arm)
Lung cancer diagnosis label: confirmed cancer vs no cancer, with cancer diagnosis records, stage, and treatment information
Mortality / follow-up labels: vital status, cause of death, and disease progression tracking
Histopathology labels (subset): tissue category per slide - primary invasive tumor histology, secondary tumor histology, carcinoma in situ, and adjacent normal lung tissue
details.file_structure: Low-dose chest CT stored as DICOM in the standard TCIA Collection/Patient/Study/Series folder hierarchy (26,254 subjects, ~21M images / 203,099 series); clinical/outcome labels are distributed separately as SAS, ZIP, and DOC files (questionnaires, screening results, diagnosis, follow-up, mortality) via the Cancer Data Access System (CDAS), and a 451-subject histopathology subset is provided as SVS whole-slide/tissue-microarray images - there are no DICOM-SEG or RTSTRUCT files in the core release.
details.extra: The core TCIA release ships no segmentations, but downstream projects add them: the NCI Imaging Data Commons (IDC) hosts harmonized/derived segmentations (e.g., AI-generated organ and nodule segmentations in DICOM-SEG) layered on NLST CTs, and the full clinical dataset (beyond the public CC BY 4.0 subset) requires a CDAS data request.

name: Lung-PET-CT-Dx
full_name: Large-Scale CT and PET/CT Dataset for Lung Cancer Diagnosis
multi_sequence_detail: CT and PET/CT (multi-modality)
segmentation_detail: tumor locations as bounding boxes (PASCAL VOC XML)
description: CT and PET/CT DICOM from 355 lung cancer patients with radiologist bounding-box tumor annotations and biopsy-confirmed subtypes.

label_schema: Object detection: axis-aligned bounding boxes marking tumor location on CT/PET-CT slices
Per-patient lung cancer subtype encoded in the subject ID prefix: 'A' = Adenocarcinoma
'B' = Small Cell Carcinoma
'E' = Large Cell Carcinoma
'G' = Squamous Cell Carcinoma
details.file_structure: DICOM images (CT and PET/CT) with tumor bounding-box annotations stored as one XML file per annotated slice in PASCAL VOC format (created with LabelImg); provided Python code overlays the XML boxes onto the DICOM images.
details.extra: Annotations were verified by five thoracic radiologists (one labeled, four reviewed each file); dataset covers 355 subjects, 436 studies, 1,295 series (~251,135 images). Subtype is a patient-level label from the subject ID, not a per-box class.

name: EMPIRE10
full_name: Evaluation of Methods for Pulmonary Image Registration 2010
segmentation_detail: reference lung boundary, fissure and landmark annotations for evaluation
description: Thoracic CT registration challenge providing 30 intra-subject chest CT scan pairs with lung, fissure and landmark references.

label_schema: Reference lung boundary segmentation: binary lung masks provided per scan (used to penalize registrations that align voxels across the lung boundary within 20mm)
Major fissure reference segmentations: points within 20mm of a fissure marked above/below the fissure plate for alignment evaluation
100 distinctive landmark points defined in each fixed image, with corresponding matched points in the moving image (accuracy = Euclidean distance between registered and reference points)
Deformation-field singularities: locations where the Jacobian determinant <= 0 are penalized (metric computed from the submission, not a provided annotation)
details.file_structure: All CT scans and their binary lung masks are distributed in MetaImage format (ASCII .mhd header + binary .raw data); each scan pair is named by subject ID with _Fixed and _Moving suffixes (e.g., 01_Fixed.mhd/.raw, 01_Moving.mhd/.raw), and participants submit three floating-point Meta deformation-field images (defX, defY, defZ) matching the fixed image dimensions.
details.extra: 30 thoracic CT scan pairs total (20 registered offline plus 10 registered live at MICCAI 2010); it is a registration-evaluation challenge with no train/test split. Landmark correspondences were established semi-automatically and verified by multiple independent observers; the lung-boundary and fissure reference segmentations were produced automatically with manual verification.

name: ACDC
multi_sequence_detail: cine bSSFP short-axis
segmentation_detail: LV cavity, RV endocardium, LV myocardium at ED and ES
description: Cine cardiac MRI from 150 patients across 5 diagnostic classes with LV/RV/myocardium segmentations and disease labels.

label_schema: Label 1 — RV cavity
Label 2 — LV myocardium
Label 3 — LV cavity
Per-patient diagnosis: normal, MINF, DCM, HCM, ARV (5 classes)
details.file_structure: patientXXX/ with 4D cine NIfTI + frames at ED and ES, each with a _gt.nii.gz label. Info.cfg holds ED/ES frame numbers and diagnosis.
details.splits: 100 training / 50 test

name: M&Ms
full_name: Multi-Centre, Multi-Vendor & Multi-Disease Cardiac Image Segmentation Challenge
multi_sequence_detail: cine bSSFP short-axis
segmentation_detail: LV/RV blood pool, LV myocardium at ED and ES
description: Generalization challenge with cine CMR from 375 patients scanned on four vendors across Spain, Germany and Canada.

label_schema: Label 1 — LV blood pool
Label 2 — LV myocardium
Label 3 — RV blood pool (annotated at ED and ES)
details.file_structure: Per subject NIfTI cine + label; metadata CSV gives vendor (A–D), centre and pathology.
details.splits: 150 train (2 vendors) / 25 val / 200 test (4 vendors)

name: M&Ms-2
full_name: Multi-Disease, Multi-View & Multi-Center Right Ventricular Segmentation Challenge
multi_sequence_detail: cine bSSFP, short-axis and long-axis
segmentation_detail: LV/RV blood pool, LV myocardium at ED and ES
description: Extension of M&Ms focused on right-ventricle segmentation from multi-view cine CMR of 360 subjects.

label_schema: Label 1 - LV: left ventricle blood pool
Label 2 - MYO: left ventricular myocardium
Label 3 - RV: right ventricle blood pool
Label 0 - background
Ground-truth masks provided only for the ED (end-diastole) and ES (end-systole) cardiac phases
details.file_structure: One folder per patient (e.g. training/001/) containing NIfTI (.nii.gz) volumes for both short-axis (SA) and long-axis 4-chamber (LA) views at ED and ES, with images named {pid}_SA_ED/ES.nii.gz and {pid}_LA_ED/ES.nii.gz and matching segmentation masks suffixed _gt (e.g. {pid}_SA_ED_gt.nii.gz).
details.splits: 160 training (subjects 1-160) + 40 validation (subjects 161-200) publicly released with labels, plus a held-out test set (~160 subjects) for a total of 360; test ground truth is not distributed.
details.extra: Multi-disease, multi-center, multi-vendor cardiac cine MRI focused on the right ventricle; annotations cover only the two extreme phases (ED/ES), not the full temporal cine sequence.

name: EMIDEC
full_name: Evaluation of Myocardial Infarction from Delayed-Enhancement Cardiac MRI
paired_text_detail: per-patient clinical metadata (age, sex, ECG, troponin)
multi_sequence_detail: delayed/late gadolinium enhancement (DE-MRI/LGE) short-axis
segmentation_detail: myocardium, infarct, no-reflow area
description: 150 short-axis DE-MRI exams with myocardium and infarct contours plus clinical data, from University Hospital of Dijon.

label_schema: Segmentation (delayed-enhancement short-axis cardiac MRI), 5 integer classes:
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - cavity (left ventricular blood pool)
Label 2 - normal myocardium
Label 3 - myocardial infarction
Label 4 - no-reflow (persistent microvascular obstruction)
Whole myocardium = union of labels 2, 3 and 4
Case-level classification: Normal vs Pathological (myocardial infarction)
details.file_structure: Per-case folders (named with prefix N for normal, P for pathological); each contains a NIfTI image volume of the left ventricle, a NIfTI ground-truth mask (.nii.gz) with the integer labels, and a text file of clinical/demographic data (sex, age, smoking, BMI, hypertension, diabetes, family history, ECG, troponin, Killip, ejection fraction, NTproBNP).
details.splits: Training: 100 patients (67 pathological, 33 normal); Testing: 50 patients (33 pathological, 17 normal); total 150 exams
details.extra: Two tasks in the EMIDEC challenge: (1) automatic segmentation of the contours and diseased regions, (2) case-level classification (normal vs pathological) that may combine the MRI with the accompanying clinical text file. Data acquired at University Hospital of Dijon on Siemens scanners.

name: MyoPS 2020
full_name: Myocardial Pathology Segmentation Combining Multi-Sequence CMR
multi_sequence_detail: aligned bSSFP cine + LGE + T2-weighted CMR
segmentation_detail: scar, edema, normal myocardium, LV/RV blood pool
description: Aligned three-sequence (bSSFP/LGE/T2) CMR from 45 patients for myocardial scar and edema segmentation.

label_schema: Ground-truth is a single multi-class segmentation label volume per case (one file, suffix _gd)
Label 200 - LV normal myocardium
Label 500 - LV blood pool
Label 600 - RV blood pool
Label 1220 - LV myocardial edema
Label 2221 - LV myocardial scar / infarction
Label 0 - background
Challenge-scored pathology targets: myocardial scar (2221) and edema+scar combined (1220+2221)
details.file_structure: Per patient, three co-registered CMR sequences are stored as separate NIfTI (.nii.gz) volumes named by sequence -- bSSFP/cine (_C0), LGE (_DE), and T2-weighted (_T2) -- alongside one ground-truth label NIfTI (_gd) that encodes the five foreground classes as the integer values above; training cases include the _gd labels while test cases provide images only.
details.splits: 45 patients total: 25 training cases (with gold-standard _gd labels) and 20 test cases (images only, labels withheld).
details.extra: The three CMR sequences were pre-processed/aligned into a common space (via the organizers' MvMM method), so the sequences and the single shared label map are spatially registered; only the pathology classes (scar, and edema+scar) are formally evaluated in the challenge, not the blood pools or normal myocardium.

name: Sunnybrook Cardiac Data
full_name: Sunnybrook Cardiac Data (2009 LV Segmentation Challenge)
multi_sequence_detail: cine short-axis MRI
segmentation_detail: LV endocardium, epicardium, papillary muscles at ED and ES
description: 45 cine cardiac MRI cases (healthy and pathological) with expert LV contours, released public domain.

label_schema: Endocardial contour (inner / i-contour) - LV blood-pool boundary
Epicardial contour (outer / o-contour) - LV myocardium outer boundary
Contours drawn only at the end-diastolic (ED) and end-systolic (ES) cardiac phases
Papillary muscle contours also included (used for LV mass; excluded from blood pool)
45 cases labeled by pathology group: Healthy/normal (N)=9, LV hypertrophy (HYP)=12, heart failure without infarction (HF)=12, heart failure with infarction (HF-I)=12
details.file_structure: Short-axis cine MRI images are provided as DICOM (5 batches), and expert manual annotations are provided as plain-text contour files (one file per contour type per slice, e.g. IM-xxxx-xxxx-icontour/ocontour-manual.txt, each listing x,y pixel coordinates), packaged in a separate 'Manual contours' zip alongside a patient-info CSV and 'Left ventricular models' zip.
details.splits: 45 cases organized as three subsets of 15 each for the MICCAI 2009 LV Segmentation Challenge: training, online (validation), and validation/offline test, each subset balanced across the four pathology groups
details.extra: The official page confirms 45 cases in 4 pathology groups with ED/ES statistics and endocardial/epicardial expert contours; the exact papillary-contour and per-subset case listing were not re-verifiable on the current official landing page, so the 15/15/15 split reflects the original Radau et al. challenge framework.

name: MM-WHS
multi_sequence_detail: cardiac CT/CTA and 3D bSSFP MRI (unpaired)
segmentation_detail: LV/RV/LA/RA cavities, LV myocardium, aorta, pulmonary artery
description: Whole-heart segmentation with 60 cardiac CT/CTA and 60 MRI volumes and labels for seven cardiac substructures.

label_schema: Label 500 - left ventricle blood cavity
Label 600 - right ventricle blood cavity
Label 420 - left atrium blood cavity
Label 550 - right atrium blood cavity
Label 205 - myocardium of the left ventricle
Label 820 - ascending aorta
Label 850 - pulmonary artery
Voxels outside these 7 substructures are background (0); note the non-sequential integer label values used in the ground-truth maps
details.file_structure: Distributed as gzipped NIfTI volumes, one image file and one matching label/ground-truth segmentation file per subject, organized into separate CT and MRI folders (training subjects ship with label maps; test subjects are images only).
details.splits: Training: 20 CT + 20 MRI (with manual labels); Test: 40 CT + 40 MRI (labels withheld). Total 120 volumes (60 CT/CTA + 60 MRI).
details.extra: Same 7-structure label scheme is shared across both modalities, supporting cross-modality / whole-heart segmentation; the peculiar integer label values (e.g. 500, 205) come from the challenge's original annotation convention rather than sequential 1..7 indexing.

name: LAScarQS 2022
full_name: Left Atrial and Scar Quantification & Segmentation Challenge
multi_sequence_detail: late gadolinium enhancement (LGE) MRI
segmentation_detail: left atrium cavity and atrial scar
description: Multi-center LGE-MRI of atrial-fibrillation patients for left-atrium and atrial-scar segmentation.

label_schema: Task 1 LA cavity (atriumSegImgMO.nii.gz): binary mask - 0 background / left atrium blood pool foreground (stored raw as ~420, typically normalized to 1)
Task 1 LA scar (scarSegImgM.nii.gz): binary mask - 0 background / 1 atrial scar (scar lies on the thin LA wall, ~2-5% of LA volume, high class imbalance)
Task 2 LA cavity (atriumSegImgMO.nii.gz): binary mask of the left atrium blood pool only - no scar label provided for Task 2
details.file_structure: Compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz) organized into task1/ and task2/ folders, each case in its own subfolder (e.g. train_data/train_N/) containing enhanced.nii.gz (the LGE-MRI image) plus atriumSegImgMO.nii.gz (LA cavity label) and, for Task 1 only, scarSegImgM.nii.gz (scar label).
details.splits: Task 1: 60 labeled training cases (single-center, Utah/NAMIC-CARMA); Task 2: 130 labeled training cases (multi-center); 194 LGE-MRIs released for training total, with separate validation and test phases (held-out test labels not public; reported ~34 Task 1 / ~64 Task 2 test cases).
details.extra: Scar (scarSegImgM) annotations exist only for Task 1; the two tasks are evaluated separately though joint LA-cavity-plus-scar optimization is encouraged, and validation/test volumes are resampled to 1x1x1 mm.

name: Atria Seg 2018
multi_sequence_detail: 3D late gadolinium enhancement (LGE) MRI
segmentation_detail: left atrium cavity
description: 154 3D LGE-MRI scans of atrial-fibrillation patients with expert left-atrium segmentations.

label_schema: Single-class binary segmentation of the left atrial (LA) cavity from 3D LGE/GE-MRI
Mask value 255 - left atrial cavity (foreground)
Mask value 0 - background
details.file_structure: Each patient has its own folder containing the raw 3D gadolinium-enhanced MRI as lgemri.nrrd and the ground-truth binary LA cavity mask as laendo.nrrd, split across Training Set and Test Set folders.
details.splits: 100 training cases (with masks) and 54 test cases (labels withheld during the challenge test phase)
details.extra: Binary masks are encoded with values {0, 255} rather than {0, 1}; test-set labels were not released to participants during the challenge.

name: MSD Heart (Task02)
multi_sequence_detail: mono-sequence MRI
segmentation_detail: left atrium
description: Task02 of the Decathlon: 30 mono-modal cardiac MRI volumes with left-atrium labels.

label_schema: Voxel-wise segmentation mask, single foreground class
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - left atrium
details.file_structure: Each case is a mono-modal cardiac MRI volume stored as compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz); images in imagesTr/imagesTs folders and matching integer segmentation masks in labelsTr/, with a dataset.json describing modality, labels ({"0":"background","1":"left atrium"}), numTraining and numTest.
details.splits: 20 training cases (with labels) and 10 test cases (images only, labels withheld)
details.extra: Data originates from the Left Atrial Segmentation Challenge (King's College London); only the left atrium appendage/body is annotated, and test labels were held out for the challenge leaderboard.

name: RVSC
multi_sequence_detail: cine short-axis MRI
segmentation_detail: RV endocardium and epicardium at ED and ES
description: Right-ventricle endo/epicardium segmentation from short-axis cine MRI of 48 patients.

label_schema: RV endocardium contour (inner border of the right-ventricle blood pool)
RV epicardium contour (outer/myocardial border of the right ventricle)
Contours annotated on the end-diastole (ED) and end-systole (ES) frames only
Manual expert delineation stored as ordered lists of contour point coordinates (not a labeled voxel/class map)
details.file_structure: Per-patient directories each hold the full cine-MRI examination as DICOM (.dcm) slices (~220 images/patient, e.g. P01-0000.dcm) plus a listing file (e.g. P01list.txt) naming the images to be processed, with the expert manual contours provided as accompanying text files giving the endocardial and epicardial boundary point coordinates for the listed ED/ES images.
details.splits: Three sets of 16 patients each (48 total): Training Set (images + ground-truth contours), Test1 Set, and Test2 Set; ground-truth manual segmentations for Test1 and Test2 were released in July 2019.
details.extra: Contours are single-expert (cardiac radiologist) manual delineations; only ED and ES frames are contoured despite each patient's exam containing ~20 frames/cycle across multiple slices, and image size is typically 256x216 (216x256 for a few patients such as P02 and P08).

name: CMRxRecon
multi_sequence_detail: multi-contrast: cine, T1 mapping, T2 mapping; SAX and 2/3/4-chamber
segmentation_detail: cardiac segmentation labels alongside k-space
description: Multi-contrast, multi-coil raw k-space cardiac MRI from 300 subjects with segmentation labels for accelerated reconstruction.

label_schema: SAX (short-axis) cine labels: Label 1 - left ventricle (LV blood pool)
SAX cine: Label 2 - left ventricular myocardium
SAX cine: Label 3 - right ventricle (RV blood pool)
LAX (long-axis) cine labels use a DIFFERENT scheme (4 chambers): Label 1 - left atrium
LAX cine: Label 2 - right atrium
LAX cine: Label 3 - left ventricle
LAX cine: Label 4 - right ventricle
Manual segmentations by an experienced cardiac radiologist using ITK-SNAP; masks also provided for T1map/T2map mapping images
details.file_structure: Multi-coil k-space stored as MATLAB .mat files (e.g. cine_sax.mat with cine_sax_mask.mat undersampling mask); reconstructed reference images and their manual segmentation masks stored as NIfTI (cine_sax_forlabel.nii.gz = image, cine_sax_label.nii.gz = integer label map; likewise cine_lax_*, T1map_*, T2map_*), organized per-subject.
details.splits: 120 training, 60 validation, 120 test (300 healthy volunteers total); manual segmentations provided for all subjects.
details.extra: This is the CMRxRecon 2023 reconstruction challenge dataset; segmentation label INTEGERS DIFFER between the SAX view (LV=1, myocardium=2, RV=3) and the LAX view (LA=1, RA=2, LV=3, RV=4), so the same integer means different structures across views. Later editions (CMRxRecon2024/2025) expand modalities/views but center on reconstruction.

name: ASOCA
multi_sequence_detail: coronary CT angiography (CTCA)
segmentation_detail: coronary lumen masks, centerlines and vessel radius
description: Coronary CTA from 40 subjects (20 normal, 20 diseased) plus 20 test cases with expert coronary segmentation masks and centerlines.

label_schema: Binary voxel segmentation mask of the coronary artery lumen: label 1 = coronary artery lumen (foreground), 0 = background
One mask per case covering the whole coronary arterial tree as a single lumen class (left and right coronary arteries not separated into distinct label values)
Lumen annotation explicitly excludes calcified plaque regions and other disease (only the contrast-filled lumen is labeled)
Ground truth provided for the 40 training cases only; test-set masks are withheld during the challenge
details.file_structure: Volumetric CCTA images and their matching binary lumen segmentation masks are distributed as NRRD (.nrrd) 3D volumes, organized into separate training and test sets and split by healthy vs. diseased status.
details.splits: 60 CCTA cases total: 40 training (20 healthy + 20 confirmed coronary artery disease) and 20 test (10 healthy + 10 diseased); test-set ground truth not released during the challenge.
details.extra: Each training case was annotated by three expert annotators; evaluation uses Dice score and 95% Hausdorff distance. Note: the official Grand Challenge description documents only lumen segmentation masks — the centerline and vessel-radius annotations mentioned in the prompt could not be verified from official sources and may not be part of the distributed ground truth.

name: ImageCAS
full_name: ImageCAS: Large-Scale Coronary Artery Segmentation Dataset
multi_sequence_detail: coronary CT angiography (CCTA)
segmentation_detail: left and right coronary artery lumen
description: Large-scale coronary artery segmentation benchmark of 1,000 CCTA volumes with radiologist-annotated coronary masks.

label_schema: Binary voxel segmentation mask
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - coronary artery lumen (left and right coronary arteries labeled together as a single foreground class; individual branches/subclasses e.g. LM, LAD, LCX, RCA are NOT separately segmented)
details.file_structure: Each of the 1000 cases is stored as a paired NIfTI volume - a 3D CTA image (512x512x(206-275) voxels, ~0.29-0.43 mm in-plane, 0.25-0.45 mm slice spacing) and its matching binary annotation mask, both as .nii.gz files, one image + one label per patient.
details.splits: No single fixed train/test split; the paper defines a 4-fold cross-validation partition (provided in imageCAS_data_split.xlsx) with ~750 training (50 held out for validation) and 250 test cases per fold over the 1000 volumes.
details.extra: The left and right coronary arteries in each image were independently labeled by two radiologists; the released mask is the binary coronary-artery segmentation (integer label values are not explicitly documented in the paper but the distributed masks are binary background/artery).

name: orCaScore
multi_sequence_detail: paired non-contrast calcium-scoring CT and CTA per patient
segmentation_detail: coronary calcification lesion annotations with vessel assignment
description: Automatic coronary artery calcium scoring on cardiac CT from 72 patients across four scanners/vendors.

label_schema: Voxel-level coronary calcium annotations: voxels with intensity > 130 HU identified as candidate calcium, then confirmed as lesions by manual review
Each calcified lesion is assigned a per-vessel numeric label
Label 1 - LAD (left anterior descending); left main (LM) calcifications are labeled as LAD
Label 2 - LCX (left circumflex)
Label 3 - RCA (right coronary artery)
Derived per-patient targets: total coronary calcium (Agatston-style score) and assignment to a cardiovascular risk category
details.file_structure: Per patient there are two cardiac CT volumes (a non-contrast ECG-triggered calcium-scoring CT and a contrast-enhanced CCTA), each with a paired reference annotation image in which calcified voxels carry the vessel label (1 LAD, 2 LCX, 3 RCA); annotations were made on the non-contrast images.
details.splits: Training: 32 patients; Test: 40 patients (72 patients total)
details.extra: Reference standard built by two independent experts labeling all non-contrast images, with disagreements resolved in consensus; scans come from 4 different CT scanners/vendors across 4 hospitals. The public page does not state the exact on-disk file format of images/annotations.

name: COCA (Stanford)
full_name: COCA — Coronary Calcium and Chest CTs
multi_sequence_detail: gated coronary CT and non-gated chest CT
segmentation_detail: coronary artery calcium masks on gated CT
description: 789 gated coronary CT scans with calcium segmentations plus 214 non-gated chest CTs with calcium scores.

label_schema: Coronary artery calcium (CAC) annotations: per-lesion polygon ROI contours labeled by the coronary artery of origin (segmentation via rasterizable contours, not pre-made masks)
Class 1 - LAD (Left Anterior Descending Artery)
Class 2 - LCX (Left Circumflex Artery)
Class 3 - RCA (Right Coronary Artery)
Class 4 - LCA (Left Coronary Artery / Left Main)
Each ROI carries: pixel-space contour points (Point_px), mm-space contour points (Point_mm), center, area, length, and HU statistics (Min/Max/Mean/Dev)
Companion per-scan coronary calcium / Agatston scores stored in scores.xlsx
details.file_structure: Two subsets: Gated_release_final/ holds gated coronary CT as DICOM under patient/<id>/<series>/*.dcm with matching OsiriX Apple-plist-format XML contour annotations in calcium_xml/<id>.xml, plus a scores.xlsx; deidentified_nongated/<id>/ holds non-gated chest CT DICOMs with calcium scores but no segmentation XML.
details.extra: Only the gated subset has the calcium-segmentation XML annotations; the non-gated subset provides calcium/Agatston scores only (no contour masks). Annotations are polygon contours in OsiriX plist XML that must be rasterized into masks (class values LAD=1, LCX=2, RCA=3, LCA=4); combined gated+non-gated identifiers total ~971.

name: AVT
full_name: Aortic Vessel Tree CTA Dataset Collection
multi_sequence_detail: CT angiography (CTA)
segmentation_detail: aortic vessel tree and branches
description: Multicenter collection of 56 CTA scans covering the full aortic vessel tree with ground-truth segmentation masks.

label_schema: Binary voxel segmentation of the aortic vessel tree (foreground label 1 = vessel, 0 = background)
Single foreground class covers the whole tree as one mask: ascending aorta, aortic arch and its head/neck branches, thoracic aorta, abdominal aorta, and iliac arteries
Branches are NOT split into separate integer classes - it is one merged binary mask, not a per-vessel multi-label map
Manual/expert ground-truth segmentation masks (one per CTA scan)
details.file_structure: Per-case NRRD files with a subset-prefixed ID: a CTA volume (e.g. D1.nrrd) paired with its segmentation mask (e.g. D1.seg.nrrd), organized in folders by source subset (D = Dongyang, K = KiTS, R = Rider).
details.splits: No official train/validation/test split in the base AVT release; the derived SEG.A. Grand Challenge later used the AVT cases as training with a separate hidden fourth-institution test set.
details.extra: 56 total CTA scans across three sources - Dongyang Hospital (18), KiTS19 (20), and RIDER Lung CT (18); cohort is mostly healthy aortas but includes 1 abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) case and 5 aortic dissection cases.

name: MSD Liver (Task03)
segmentation_detail: liver + liver tumor
description: Portal-venous CT volumes with liver and tumor masks (shares imaging with the LiTS challenge).

label_schema: Label 1 — liver
Label 2 — tumor
details.file_structure: Decathlon layout: imagesTr/, labelsTr/, imagesTs/ + dataset.json describing modality and labels.
details.splits: 131 train / 70 test
details.extra: All 10 MSD tasks share this folder convention and NIfTI format.

name: MSD Pancreas (Task07)
segmentation_detail: pancreas + pancreatic tumor
description: Abdominal CT with pancreatic parenchyma and tumor annotations.

label_schema: Voxel-wise segmentation mask, 3 values
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - pancreas (parenchyma)
Label 2 - cancer (pancreatic tumor/mass)
details.file_structure: Single-channel portal-venous-phase abdominal CT volumes and their segmentation masks stored as gzipped NIfTI (.nii.gz); the Task07_Pancreas folder holds imagesTr/ (training volumes) and labelsTr/ (matching integer label masks) named pancreas_XXX.nii.gz, plus imagesTs/ (test volumes), all described by a dataset.json manifest.
details.splits: 281 training cases (with labels) and 139 test cases (labels withheld); 420 total
details.extra: Annotations provided by Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (patients underwent pancreatic mass resection); released CC-BY-SA 4.0. Test-set masks are not publicly distributed (held out for the challenge leaderboard).

name: MSD Spleen (Task09)
segmentation_detail: spleen
description: Portal-venous CT volumes with spleen segmentation masks.

label_schema: Voxel-wise semantic segmentation mask, single foreground class
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - spleen
Modality: CT (portal venous phase); target ROI = spleen
details.file_structure: Images and labels are 3D NIfTI volumes (.nii.gz): imagesTr/ (training scans) with matching per-voxel masks in labelsTr/, plus unlabeled test scans in imagesTs/; a dataset.json describes modality and the label-value-to-name mapping.
details.splits: 61 CT volumes total: 41 training (with labels) / 20 test (labels withheld). No official validation split.
details.extra: Scans are from patients undergoing chemotherapy for liver metastases (Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center); test-set ground-truth labels are not publicly released (evaluation via challenge submission).

name: MSD Colon (Task10)
segmentation_detail: primary colon cancer
description: CT volumes with primary colon cancer segmentation.

label_schema: Segmentation mask, 2 classes (voxel integer values)
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - colon cancer primaries (primary colon cancer tumor)
details.file_structure: 3D CT volumes and their voxel-wise masks as compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz), organized in imagesTr/labelsTr (training image+label pairs) and imagesTs (test images, labels withheld) folders alongside a dataset.json metadata file.
details.splits: 126 training cases (with public labels) and 64 test cases (labels withheld)
details.extra: Single-modality CT; only the tumor is annotated (the colon organ itself is not labeled), making this one of the harder/smaller MSD tasks.

name: MSD Hepatic Vessel (Task08)
full_name: Medical Segmentation Decathlon — Task08 Hepatic Vessel
segmentation_detail: hepatic vessels + liver tumor
description: Liver CT with hepatic vasculature and tumor annotations.

label_schema: Voxel-level semantic segmentation mask (3 classes)
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - Vessel (hepatic vessels)
Label 2 - Tumour (liver tumor)
details.file_structure: Per-case 3D CT volumes and label masks as NIfTI (.nii.gz), organized into imagesTr, labelsTr, and imagesTs folders (e.g. hepaticvessel_001.nii.gz), with a dataset.json holding metadata (modality, labels); a label file is a single integer-valued mask sharing the image filename.
details.splits: 303 training cases (with labels) and 140 test cases (images only; labels withheld for official website evaluation); total 443 CT volumes
details.extra: Single-modality CT (modality 0 = CT), release 1.1, CC-BY-SA 4.0. Caveat: annotation quality has been questioned in later work (Xu et al., MICCAI 2021), reporting ~65.5% of vessel pixels unlabeled and ~8.5% mislabeled as vessel.

name: MSD Prostate (Task05)
multi_sequence_detail: T2-weighted + ADC (2 channels)
segmentation_detail: central gland + peripheral zone
description: Multiparametric prostate MRI (T2W + ADC) with central gland and peripheral zone masks.

label_schema: Segmentation task, 3 label values in the mask NIfTI
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - PZ (peripheral zone of the prostate)
Label 2 - TZ (transition zone / central gland of the prostate)
Input images are 2-channel MRI: channel 0 = T2-weighted, channel 1 = ADC (apparent diffusion coefficient)
details.file_structure: MSD-standard layout: imagesTr/ (4D NIfTI .nii.gz with 2 modality channels: T2 + ADC), labelsTr/ (single-channel integer-label NIfTI masks), imagesTs/ (unlabeled test), plus a dataset.json describing modality, labels, and counts.
details.splits: 32 training cases (labeled) and 16 test cases (labels withheld); no official validation split.
details.extra: Only 32 labeled training volumes make this the smallest MSD task; the two structures (PZ and TZ) together delineate the whole prostate, and the multi-modal T2+ADC input must be co-registered per case.

name: BTCV / Synapse Multi-organ
full_name: Beyond the Cranial Vault Abdomen Multi-Organ Segmentation
segmentation_detail: 13 organs (spleen, kidneys, gallbladder, esophagus, liver, stomach, aorta, IVC, veins, pancreas, adrenals)
description: Widely used benchmark of 30 abdominal CT scans annotated with 13 organs.

label_schema: 13 organs: spleen, R/L kidney, gallbladder, esophagus, liver, stomach, aorta, IVC, portal & splenic vein, pancreas, R/L adrenal gland
details.file_structure: img/ and label/ folders of NIfTI volumes; labels are integer-coded organ maps.
details.splits: 30 labeled training / 20 unlabeled test

name: AMOS22
multi_sequence_detail: multi-center, multi-vendor, multi-phase CT and MRI
segmentation_detail: 15 abdominal organs
description: Large multi-modality (CT + MRI) abdominal benchmark with voxel-level annotations of 15 organs.

label_schema: 15 organs: spleen, R/L kidney, gallbladder, esophagus, liver, stomach, aorta, IVC, pancreas, R/L adrenal gland, duodenum, bladder, prostate/uterus
details.file_structure: imagesTr/labelsTr in NIfTI; filename prefix indicates CT vs MRI. dataset.json lists label indices.
details.splits: 200 CT + 40 MRI train (labels) / 100 CT val / 200 CT + 60 MRI test

name: AMOS-MM
paired_text_detail: radiology reports (findings + impression) and 19,562 VQA pairs
description: Chest/abdomen/pelvis CT volumes paired with radiology reports and VQA pairs for vision-language tasks.

label_schema: AMOS-MM annotations are text-based, covering two tasks over abdominal/chest/pelvis CT: Medical Report Generation (MRG) and Visual Question Answering (VQA) — no segmentation masks are part of AMOS-MM itself
MRG: each CT has a free-text radiology report with two sections stored as labels.report.findings and labels.report.impression
MRG findings are organized by three anatomical regions — chest, abdomen, and pelvis (each a sub-field of the findings object)
VQA: 19,562 multiple-choice question-answer pairs (stored under labels.qa), each item = a question, four options {A, B, C, D}, and one correct answer letter
VQA questions span 6 clinical scopes/regions (per the official page's '6 Clinical Scopes'); the exact six category names are not published on the page
Dataset totals reported officially: 2000+ CT scans with reports and 19,562 VQA pairs
details.file_structure: CT volumes are NIfTI (.nii.gz) files under imagesTr / imagesVa folders; annotations ship as JSON (a report-generation JSON and a VQA JSON), where each record is keyed to an image filename and holds labels.report.findings, labels.report.impression, and labels.qa (list of MCQ dicts with options A-D and answer).
details.splits: Official per-split scan counts are not stated on the dataset page; the released report-generation and VQA JSON files each contain 'training' and 'validation' splits, with the test set held out on the challenge server (CodaBench MICCAI24 AMOS-MM).
details.extra: AMOS-MM builds on the AMOS imaging cohort; the separate AMOS segmentation release provides 15 abdominal-organ masks (500+ CT, 100+ MRI), but AMOS-MM's own labels are text-only (reports + multiple-choice VQA).

name: TotalSegmentator
multi_sequence_detail: real-world clinical CT, various phases/scanners
segmentation_detail: 117 anatomical structures across the whole body
description: Large real-world clinical CT dataset with segmentations of 117 anatomical structures.

label_schema: 117 structures grouped as: 27 organs, 26 vertebrae, 24 ribs, 20+ muscles, cardiovascular (aorta, heart chambers, pulmonary artery), and more
Each structure is a separate binary NIfTI mask (multilabel also provided)
details.file_structure: Per subject: ct.nii.gz + segmentations/ folder with one <structure>.nii.gz per class.
details.splits: 1,228 CT (v2), predefined train/val/test in meta.csv

name: KiTS19
multi_sequence_detail: late arterial phase contrast CT
segmentation_detail: kidney + kidney tumor
description: Arterial-phase abdominal CT of kidney cancer patients with kidney and tumor segmentations.

label_schema: Label 0 - background
Label 1 - kidney (renal parenchyma)
Label 2 - kidney tumor
details.file_structure: One folder per case (case_00000/, case_00001/, ...), each containing imaging.nii.gz (CT volume, float32) and segmentation.nii.gz (voxel-wise mask, uint8) as compressed NIfTI files, with a top-level kits.json metadata file.
details.splits: 300 total cases: 210 training cases with publicly released segmentation masks, 90 test cases with withheld annotations.
details.extra: Voxel spacing varies considerably across cases (multi-center clinical acquisition); an 'interpolated' branch of the official repo provides spatially normalized volumes with consistent affines. Note KiTS21/KiTS23 later expanded classes to include the cyst class, but KiTS19 has only kidney and tumor.

name: KiTS21
full_name: 2021 Kidney and Kidney Tumor Segmentation Challenge
multi_sequence_detail: corticomedullary/late arterial phase CT
segmentation_detail: kidney + tumor + cyst
description: Kidney CT dataset adding cyst annotations, with an external-institution test set.

label_schema: Label 1 - kidney
Label 2 - tumor
Label 3 - cyst
Aggregation/priority order (1, 3, 2) resolves overlaps in the dense masks
Hierarchical Evaluation Classes (HECs) used for scoring: 'Kidney and Masses' (1+2+3), 'Kidney Mass' (2+3), 'Tumor' (2)
details.file_structure: One folder per case (case_00000 ... case_00299), each with imaging.nii.gz (CT) and an aggregated_MAJ_seg.nii.gz (plus OR/AND aggregations); raw per-annotator masks live in a segmentations/ subfolder named like kidney_instance-1_annotation-1.nii.gz; all volumes are NIfTI (.nii.gz).
details.splits: 300 training cases released/frozen (case_00000-case_00299); test set (~100 cases) was held out for the challenge and not publicly labeled.
details.extra: Multi-rater dataset: each anatomical instance was independently delineated by three annotators drawn from three tiers (experts, trainees, laypeople), then combined via union (OR), intersection (AND), and majority-vote (MAJ) aggregations.

name: KiTS23
full_name: 2023 Kidney and Kidney Tumor Segmentation Challenge
multi_sequence_detail: late arterial and nephrogenic contrast phases
segmentation_detail: kidney + tumor + cyst
description: Largest KiTS cohort with kidney, tumor and cyst masks including nephrogenic-phase scans.

label_schema: Label 1 — kidney
Label 2 — tumor
Label 3 — cyst
Hierarchical eval regions: Kidney+Masses, Masses (tumor+cyst), Tumor
details.file_structure: One folder per case (case_00000/) with imaging.nii.gz and segmentation.nii.gz.
details.splits: 489 training (labels public) / 110 hidden test

name: LiTS
multi_sequence_detail: contrast-enhanced abdominal CT
segmentation_detail: liver + liver tumor lesions
description: Contrast-enhanced liver CT with liver and tumor annotations from multiple institutions.

label_schema: Label 1 — liver
Label 2 — tumor/lesion
details.file_structure: volume-*.nii and segmentation-*.nii pairs (NIfTI).
details.splits: 131 training / 70 test

name: CHAOS
multi_sequence_detail: MRI: T1-DUAL in/opposed-phase, T2-SPIR; plus abdominal CT
segmentation_detail: liver (CT); liver + spleen + kidneys (MRI)
description: Healthy-subject abdominal CT and multi-sequence MRI for single and multi-organ segmentation.

label_schema: Task/modality split: CT ground truth = liver only (single foreground organ); MRI ground truth = 4 organs (liver, right kidney, left kidney, spleen)
MRI masks are 8-bit PNG label images where each organ is encoded by a grayscale intensity RANGE (not a single flat value): Liver = 55-70 (nominal 63)
MRI: Right kidney = grayscale 110-135 (nominal 126)
MRI: Left kidney = grayscale 175-200 (nominal 189)
MRI: Spleen = grayscale 240-255 (nominal 252)
Background = 0 in both CT and MRI masks
Common multi-class relabeling used by the community after thresholding the ranges: 1 = liver, 2 = right kidney, 3 = left kidney, 4 = spleen
CT masks are binary (liver vs background), covering the whole liver including vessels/tumors as one class
details.file_structure: Per-patient folders containing a DICOM image series (DICOM_anon) and a matching series of 2D PNG ground-truth masks (Ground); MRI is further organized into T1DUAL (with InPhase/OutPhase DICOMs) and T2SPIR sub-sequences, so labels are stored as slice-wise PNGs rather than a single 3D NIfTI/DICOM-SEG volume.
details.splits: CT: 20 training / 20 testing cases. MRI: 20 training / 20 testing cases, each with T1-DUAL (in-phase + out-phase) and T2-SPIR sequences (120 total DICOM datasets = 40 per sequence). Test-set ground truths are withheld.
details.extra: Ground-truth masks for the test split are never released publicly (reserved for the challenge's online evaluation server); the T1-DUAL in-phase and out-phase images are co-registered and share the same ground-truth mask.

name: WORD
segmentation_detail: 16 organs incl. liver, spleen, kidneys, stomach, pancreas, duodenum, colon, bladder
description: Whole-abdomen CT with dense pixel-level annotations of 16 organs, released for academic research by application.

label_schema: Voxel-level multi-organ segmentation masks; 16 foreground classes plus label 0 = background
Label 1 - liver
Label 2 - spleen
Label 3 - left kidney
Label 4 - right kidney
Label 5 - stomach
Label 6 - gallbladder
Label 7 - esophagus
Label 8 - pancreas
Label 9 - duodenum
Label 10 - colon
Label 11 - intestine
Label 12 - adrenal gland
Label 13 - rectum
Label 14 - bladder
Label 15 - left head of femur
Label 16 - right head of femur
details.file_structure: 3D abdominal CT volumes and their dense label masks are stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz) in nnU-Net-style split folders (imagesTr/labelsTr, imagesVal/labelsVal, imagesTs/labelsTs), each label file a single integer-valued volume matching its image.
details.splits: 150 CT volumes total: 100 training / 20 validation / 30 testing (30,495 slices: 20,115 / 4,103 / 6,277).
details.extra: In addition to the dense pixel-level masks, the authors release scribble-based sparse annotations for weakly-supervised segmentation (each organ scribbled at least once per volume), reducing labeling cost by >96%; distributed zip password is word@uestc.

name: FLARE21
full_name: MICCAI 2021 Fast and Low-resource Abdominal Organ Segmentation
multi_sequence_detail: multi-center abdominal CT
segmentation_detail: liver, kidney, spleen, pancreas
description: Multi-center abdominal CT for efficient four-organ segmentation from 11 medical centers.

label_schema: Label 0 - background
Label 1 - liver
Label 2 - kidney (left and right kidneys share this label)
Label 3 - spleen
Label 4 - pancreas
details.file_structure: Abdominal CT volumes and their voxel-wise segmentation masks are stored as compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz); images use an nnU-Net-style _0000 suffix (e.g. input case test_001_0000.nii.gz) and the matching label/segmentation drops the suffix (test_001.nii.gz), with images and masks kept in separate folders.
details.splits: 361 training + 50 validation + 100 testing = 511 cases from 11 medical centers
details.extra: The training set images come from many centers but the four-organ masks were curated/checked for the challenge; a portion of cases derive from other public datasets (e.g. MSD, KiTS, NIH Pancreas) re-annotated to the unified 4-organ scheme.

name: FLARE22
multi_sequence_detail: multi-center abdominal CT
segmentation_detail: 13 abdominal organs
description: Semi-supervised abdominal CT benchmark with a large unlabeled pool for 13-organ segmentation.

label_schema: Voxel-wise multi-organ segmentation masks: background = 0, 13 abdominal organs labeled with integer values 1-13
Class: liver
Class: spleen
Class: pancreas
Class: right kidney
Class: left kidney
Class: stomach
Class: gallbladder
Class: esophagus
Class: aorta
Class: inferior vena cava (IVC)
Class: right adrenal gland
Class: left adrenal gland
Class: duodenum
details.file_structure: 3D CT volumes and their voxel label masks are stored as compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz), organized in nnU-Net-style folders (image files named like FLARETs_0001_0000.nii.gz with a matching single-channel integer label volume of the same spatial dimensions); the 2000 unlabeled training cases ship as images only.
details.splits: Training: 50 labeled cases + 2000 unlabeled cases; Validation/Tuning: 50 visible cases; Testing: 200 hidden cases (total ~2300 CT scans from 20+ institutions).
details.extra: Semi-supervised challenge: only 50 of the 2050 training cases carry organ masks (labeled cases have pancreas disease; the 2000 unlabeled cases have liver/kidney/spleen/pancreas diseases), so the task centers on exploiting unlabeled data. The exact integer-to-organ index assignment (1-13) could not be confirmed from the official pages fetched; only the class set is verified.

name: FLARE23
multi_sequence_detail: multi-center abdominal CT
segmentation_detail: 13 abdominal organs + pan-cancer (any lesion)
description: Partially-labeled abdominal CT challenge for joint 13-organ and pan-cancer tumor segmentation.

label_schema: Segmentation masks with 14 classes (13 abdominal organs + 1 pan-cancer lesion class)
Label 1 - liver
Label 2 - right kidney
Label 3 - spleen
Label 4 - pancreas
Label 5 - aorta
Label 6 - inferior vena cava (IVC)
Label 7 - right adrenal gland
Label 8 - left adrenal gland
Label 9 - gallbladder
Label 10 - esophagus
Label 11 - stomach
Label 12 - duodenum
Label 13 - left kidney
Label 14 - tumor (pan-cancer / any lesion, single unified class regardless of cancer type)
details.file_structure: 3D CT volumes and their integer-valued segmentation masks are stored as separate NIfTI files (.nii.gz), one file per case, with images and label masks in parallel folders and matching filenames.
details.splits: Training: 4000 CT scans (2200 partially labeled + 1800 unlabeled); Validation/tuning: 100 fully labeled cases; Test: 400 hidden fully labeled cases.
details.extra: Training annotations are largely partial labels (not every case has all 14 classes annotated) produced via a human-in-the-loop pipeline combining expert annotation and model-generated pseudo-labels; scans come from 50+ medical centers.

name: Pancreas-CT (NIH)
multi_sequence_detail: portal-venous phase CT
segmentation_detail: pancreas (manual slice-by-slice)
description: 82 portal-venous abdominal CT scans with manual pancreas segmentations, a classic pancreas benchmark.

label_schema: Binary segmentation of the pancreas (single foreground class)
Label value 1 - pancreas
Label value 0 - background
Ground truth: slice-by-slice manual pancreas segmentation by a medical student, verified/modified by an experienced radiologist
details.file_structure: CT images are provided as DICOM (~18,942 axial 512x512 slices) organized per subject in TCIA folders (PANCREAS_00XX), while the pancreas ground-truth masks are distributed as separate NIfTI files (labelXXXX.nii.gz), one binary volume per subject.
details.extra: Originally 82 subjects, but cases #25 and #70 were found to be duplicate scans of case #2 and removed, leaving 80 usable CT scans; there are no tumor/organ labels beyond the pancreas.

name: CT-ORG
full_name: CT volumes with multiple organ segmentations
multi_sequence_detail: mixed contrast/non-contrast; 9 from PET-CT
segmentation_detail: liver, lungs, bones, kidneys, bladder
description: 140 heterogeneous CT scans with 3D masks of five organs, sourced partly from PET-CT exams.

label_schema: Multi-organ 3D semantic segmentation; masks encode organs as integer codes (stored as 32-bit float) per voxel
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - liver
Label 2 - bladder
Label 3 - lungs
Label 4 - kidneys
Label 5 - bones
Label 6 - brain (only labeled on the minority of scans that show it)
details.file_structure: Each case is a pair of NIfTI-1 files in patient-native space: CT volume 'volume-x.nii.gz' (Hounsfield units) and its segmentation mask 'labels-x.nii.gz', where x is the case number 0-139.
details.splits: 140 CT scans total; the first 21 volumes (case numbers 0-20) are the test split and the remaining 119 (cases 21-139) are the training split.
details.extra: Annotation quality differs by split: training-set lungs and bones were auto-generated by morphological image processing (other organs manual), while the test set was fully manually segmented in ITK-SNAP; the authors warn training masks 'should not be considered reliable for evaluation.'

name: AbdomenCT-1K
multi_sequence_detail: multi-phase, multi-vendor, multi-disease CT
segmentation_detail: liver, kidney, spleen, pancreas
description: Over 1000 multi-center CT scans annotated for liver, kidney, spleen and pancreas.

label_schema: Voxel-wise multi-organ segmentation masks; background = 0
Label 1 - liver
Label 2 - kidney (left and right merged into one class)
Label 3 - spleen
Label 4 - pancreas

Extension subset (50 cases, used in MICCAI FLARE2022): 13 annotated organs - liver, right kidney, spleen, pancreas, aorta, inferior vena cava, right adrenal gland, left adrenal gland, gallbladder, esophagus, stomach, duodenum, left kidney (exact integer index per organ not verified from a fetched source)
Extension subset (773 cases): additional pseudo tumor labels (noisy/weak labels covering all possible tumors)
details.file_structure: Compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz): each CT volume and its integer-valued label mask are stored as separate files with case IDs (e.g. Case_00001 ... Case_01000), organized into four benchmark folders (fully-supervised, semi-supervised, weakly-supervised, continual learning) in an nnU-Net-compatible layout.
details.extra: The 1000+ core scans carry 4-organ masks aggregated/re-annotated from existing single-organ datasets (KiTS19, Medical Segmentation Decathlon, LiTS, NIH Pancreas-CT); companion annotation sets add a 50-case 13-organ subset (Zenodo 7860267) and a 773-case pseudo-tumor subset. There is no single official train/val/test split - each benchmark defines its own (e.g. the semi-supervised task uses 41 labeled + 400 or 800 unlabeled cases).

name: PI-CAI
multi_sequence_detail: bi-parametric MRI: T2W, DWI (high b-value), ADC
segmentation_detail: csPCa lesion delineations + whole-gland annotations
description: Large public bi-parametric prostate MRI cohort with histopathology-linked labels for clinically significant cancer detection.

label_schema: csPCa lesion masks (ISUP ≥2)
Whole-gland masks (AI-derived + subset human)
Case-level label: clinically significant cancer yes/no with ISUP grade
details.file_structure: Per case: T2W, high-b DWI and ADC as .mha + annotations (human + AI) in separate folders; clinical CSV with PSA, ISUP, etc.
details.splits: 1,500 public training/validation / 1,000 hidden test

name: Prostate158
multi_sequence_detail: T2W, ADC, DWI
segmentation_detail: prostate zones + cancer lesions
description: Expert-annotated 3T bi-parametric prostate MRI with zonal anatomy and cancer segmentations.

label_schema: Zonal masks: transition/central zone, peripheral zone
Cancer lesion masks (PI-RADS ≥4)
details.file_structure: Per case NIfTI: T2W, ADC, DWI + t2_anatomy_reader1.nii.gz and adc_tumor_reader1.nii.gz label files.
details.splits: 139 training / 19 test

name: PROMISE12
multi_sequence_detail: transversal T2-weighted MRI
segmentation_detail: whole prostate (capsule)
description: Multi-vendor transversal T2-weighted prostate MRI with whole-gland masks, a classic benchmark.

label_schema: Binary segmentation mask of the whole prostate (prostate capsule) on T2-weighted MRI
Foreground value 1 - prostate; background value 0
Single class only (no zonal/lesion sub-structures)
details.file_structure: Each case is a MetaImage volume (.mhd header + .raw data); the T2W image is CaseXX.mhd and its manually delineated reference mask is CaseXX_segmentation.mhd, grouped in per-set zip archives (TrainingData, TestData, ChallengeData).
details.splits: 50 training cases with reference segmentations; 30 test cases (test-set reference masks withheld for online evaluation); additional 'live challenge' set distributed separately.
details.extra: Multi-center, multi-vendor T2W MRI acquired at 1.5T and 3T, with and without endorectal coil; ground-truth contours were drawn/reviewed by expert readers. Test-set labels are not publicly released - scoring is via the Grand Challenge server.

name: ProstateX
multi_sequence_detail: mpMRI: T2W, PD-W, DCE, DWI (b=50/400/800) + ADC
description: Multiparametric prostate MRI with marked lesions for predicting clinical significance of prostate cancer.

label_schema: PROSTATEx Challenge (primary): binary lesion-level label 'ClinSig' (clinically significant vs not), where clinically significant = biopsy Gleason Score >= 7; PIRADS-2 lesions were not biopsied and are labeled not significant
PROSTATEx-2 Challenge (companion): multi-class Gleason Grade Group of each lesion from multi-parametric MRI biomarkers
Each finding annotated by a point/centroid location (lesion position) rather than a full segmentation mask
No pixel-level tumor segmentation in the core release (labels are lesion-level classification tied to a coordinate)
details.file_structure: MR studies stored as DICOM (T2W, PD-W, DCE, DWI/ADC) plus Ktrans pharmacokinetic maps in MHD/ZRAW format; ground-truth labels provided as separate CSVs: ProstateX-Findings.csv (one row per lesion with ProxID, finding id 'fid', scanner coordinate 'pos', and ClinSig true/false) and ProstateX-Images.csv (maps each finding to DICOM series/UIDs, voxel spacing, and image coordinates 'ijk').
details.splits: PROSTATEx Challenge: 204 training subjects and 140 test subjects (344 total). PROSTATEx-2 Challenge: 112 training findings and 70 test findings.
details.extra: Test-set ClinSig / Gleason labels were withheld for the SPIE-AAPM-NCI grand challenge; companion segmentation collections (PROSTATEx-Seg-HiRes, PROSTATEx-Seg-Zones) provide prostate/zone masks but are separate derived datasets, not part of the original label release.

name: LLD-MMRI
full_name: Liver Lesion Diagnosis on Multi-phase MRI
multi_sequence_detail: non-contrast, arterial, venous, delayed, T2W, DWI, T1 in/out-of-phase
description: Multi-phase liver MRI for focal liver lesion diagnosis with per-lesion class labels and bounding boxes.

label_schema: Classification task: 7 liver lesion categories, integer-labeled 0-6 in the label files (-1 marks unlabeled validation/test cases)
Class - Hepatic hemangioma (benign)
Class - Hepatic abscess (benign)
Class - Hepatic cysts (benign)
Class - Focal nodular hyperplasia / FNH (benign)
Class - Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma / ICC (malignant)
Class - Liver metastases (malignant)
Class - Hepatocellular carcinoma / HCC (malignant)
Each case has 8 co-registered MRI phases: T2WI, DWI, T1 In Phase, T1 Out Phase, C-pre (non-contrast), C+A (arterial), C+V (venous), C+Delay (delayed)
Per-lesion 3D bounding boxes plus lesion category, stored in Annotation.json (also holds true voxel spacing)
March 2025 update adds pixel-wise segmentation masks (available via Hugging Face)
details.file_structure: Per-case folders (e.g. images/MR-a/) each hold the 8 phases as separate compressed NIfTI files (T2WI.nii.gz, In Phase.nii.gz, Out Phase.nii.gz, C-pre.nii.gz, C+A.nii.gz, C+V.nii.gz, C+Delay.nii.gz, DWI.nii.gz); an Annotation.json stores spacing + bounding-box + category info, and plain-text label files map case names to integer class ids.
details.splits: 316 training / 78 validation / 104 test cases (498 total); training labels released, validation/test labels initially withheld for the LLD-MMRI2023 challenge.
details.extra: Distributions include full-volume data, lesion bounding boxes, and pre-cropped lesion patches; released under CC BY-NC 4.0 for noncommercial research only.

name: CT Colonography
multi_sequence_detail: paired prone and supine CT
description: Large screening CT colonography collection (prone/supine) with polyp annotations from the ACRIN 6664 trial.

label_schema: Case-level polyp status grouped into 3 Excel spreadsheets: 35 cases with polyp(s) >=10 mm
69 cases with polyp(s) 6-9 mm (intermediate)
243 cases with no polyps found by both CT colonography and optical colonoscopy
Per-polyp descriptors: polyp size, location within colon segment, morphology, and some CT slice locations
Histological classification of polyps (from optical colonoscopy / biopsy reference standard)
details.file_structure: CT images are DICOM (prone and supine acquisitions per patient, downloaded via TCIA Data Retriever); polyp/finding labels are provided as separate Excel (.xls) spreadsheets keyed by patient case ID rather than as pixel-level segmentation masks.
details.extra: No voxel-level polyp segmentations are provided (labels are tabular case/lesion descriptions from the ACRIN 6664 National CT Colonography Trial); TCIA/NCI notes the spreadsheet data cannot be assured error-free since the original analysis was performed externally.

name: MRNet
multi_sequence_detail: sagittal, coronal and axial series per exam
description: 1,370 knee MRI exams from Stanford with binary labels for abnormality, ACL tears and meniscal tears.

label_schema: 3 binary study-level labels: abnormal, ACL tear, meniscal tear
details.file_structure: Per exam: sagittal.npy, coronal.npy, axial.npy (stack of slices, s×256×256) + train/valid label CSVs.
details.splits: 1,130 train / 120 validation (internal) / 120 hidden test

name: fastMRI knee
full_name: fastMRI Knee Raw k-Space and DICOM Dataset
size: >1,500 fully-sampled knee k-space MRIs + 10,000 clinical DICOM knee MRIs (~1.35 TB)
multi_sequence_detail: coronal proton-density with and without fat suppression
description: Raw k-space and DICOM knee MRI data released by NYU and Meta AI for accelerated MRI reconstruction research.

label_schema: No pathology labels — raw fully-sampled multi-coil k-space + reconstructed images for MRI reconstruction
details.file_structure: HDF5 per volume (kspace, reconstruction_rss); singlecoil and multicoil tracks; DICOMs separately.
details.splits: Official train / val / test / challenge splits

name: OAI
size: 4,796 participants, longitudinal bilateral knee MRI and X-ray
multi_sequence_detail: multiple knee MRI sequences (DESS, T2 mapping, TSE) plus radiographs
description: Longitudinal osteoarthritis cohort of 4,796 participants with serial bilateral knee MRI, radiographs and clinical data.

label_schema: Kellgren-Lawrence (KL) grade 0-4 for tibiofemoral knee OA severity from central radiographic reading: 0 none, 1 doubtful, 2 minimal/mild, 3 moderate, 4 severe
OARSI-atlas semiquantitative individual radiographic features: joint space narrowing (JSN) and osteophytes graded per compartment (medial/lateral, ~0-3)
Quantitative joint space width (JSW/minimum JSW) measurements from fixed-flexion PA knee radiographs
Patient-reported/clinical OA outcomes: WOMAC (pain, stiffness, function) and related symptom/function scores
Labels are tabular clinical + central-read variables keyed to knees/visits, NOT pixel-level image annotations in the base release
details.file_structure: Bilateral knee radiographs and multi-sequence 3T knee MRI (e.g., sagittal 3D DESS, coronal/sagittal intermediate-weighted TSE) are distributed as DICOM per participant and visit; labels/reads and clinical variables are separate tabular datasets (SAS/ASCII/CSV) linked by participant ID, knee side, and visit rather than embedded masks.
details.extra: OAI is a longitudinal observational cohort (~4,796 participants, baseline plus multi-year follow-up visits) with no official train/val/test split; pixel-level segmentations come only from third-party companions (e.g., OAI-ZIB / Ambellan et al., which manually segmented femoral & tibial bone and cartilage on OAI DESS MRIs).

name: MURA
description: Large upper-extremity radiograph dataset covering seven body parts with radiologist normal/abnormal study labels.

label_schema: Binary study-level classification: normal vs abnormal
Each study labeled y in {0,1}: abnormal (positive) = 1, normal (negative) = 0
7 upper-extremity study types: elbow, finger, forearm, hand, humerus, shoulder, wrist
Labels assigned by board-certified radiologists at time of clinical interpretation (train/valid); test set gold standard = majority vote of 3 radiologists (6 total)
details.file_structure: PNG images organized as MURA-v1.1/{train,valid}/XR_<BODYPART>/patientXXXXX/studyN_<positive|negative>/imageN.png, where the folder name suffix (positive=abnormal, negative=normal) encodes the label; accompanying CSV files (train_labeled_studies.csv / valid_labeled_studies.csv map study folder path to 0/1 label, and train_image_paths.csv / valid_image_paths.csv list per-image paths).
details.splits: Train: 13,457 studies (8,280 normal + 5,177 abnormal); Validation: 1,199 studies (661 normal + 538 abnormal); Test: 207 studies (held out, labels not released). Totals: 14,863 studies / 40,561 images from 12,173 patients.
details.extra: Test set has multi-reader annotations: read by 6 board-certified radiologists (3 used to build the majority-vote gold standard, 3 for measuring radiologist performance); labels are per-study, not per-image, and no lesion segmentation or bounding boxes are provided.

name: RSNA Bone Age
description: Pediatric left-hand radiographs labeled with skeletal bone age and sex for bone-age estimation.

label_schema: Regression target: skeletal (bone) age in months, from left-hand radiographs
Sex covariate provided per image: male = True/False (boolean)
Ground truth derived from radiology reports; annotated by multiple expert pediatric radiologist observers
details.file_structure: Left-hand radiographs stored as individual PNG images in train/validation/test image folders, with labels in accompanying CSV files (columns: id, boneage, male) keyed by image filename ID.
details.splits: ~12,611 training images / 1,425 validation images / 200 test images
details.extra: The 200-image held-out test set was reviewed by multiple radiologists, so its ground-truth ages reflect a multi-reader consensus/average rather than a single reader.

name: SKM-TEA
segmentation_detail: 4 tissues (patellar, femoral, tibial cartilage, meniscus)
description: Quantitative double-echo steady-state knee MRI with raw k-space, DICOM, tissue segmentations and pathology bounding boxes.

label_schema: Dense tissue segmentation: manual masks for 4 tissues, stored one-hot in the HDF5 'seg' key as 6 channels: Patellar Cartilage, Femoral Cartilage, Tibial Cartilage - Medial, Tibial Cartilage - Lateral, Meniscus - Medial, Meniscus - Lateral (the 4 clinical tissues, with tibial cartilage and meniscus split into medial/lateral compartments)

Pathology detection: 2D bounding-box annotations for 16 clinically relevant pathology categories spanning joint effusion and meniscal, ligament, and cartilage lesions; each category is a pathology type + subtype (e.g. 'Meniscal Tear - Myxoid')
Quantitative MRI (qMRI): raw dual-echo (qDESS) k-space enabling T2 relaxation-time / biomarker estimation as a downstream quantitative target
details.file_structure: One HDF5 file per scan: the image/DICOM track holds keys echo1, echo2 (image volumes), seg (segmentation), and stats; the raw-data track holds kspace, maps, and target (complex-valued); scanner DICOMs and NIfTI segmentation masks are also provided, and detection labels are COCO-style JSON annotation files (train.json/val.json/test.json).
details.splits: 155 total scans: 86 train, 33 validation, 36 test (test drawn from patients who underwent arthroscopic surgery)
details.extra: ~25,000 slices from 155 patients (~1.6 TB); segmentations and pathology bounding boxes were manually annotated, and the DICOM-track and raw-data (reconstruction) tracks share the same underlying scans.

name: KneeMRI
description: 917 sagittal PD-weighted knee MRI volumes from Rijeka, Croatia, labeled for anterior cruciate ligament condition.

label_schema: Task: volume-level classification of anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) condition, target column `aclDiagnosis`
aclDiagnosis 0 - healthy / non-injured ACL
aclDiagnosis 1 - partially injured (partially ruptured) ACL
aclDiagnosis 2 - completely ruptured ACL
Per-volume ROI annotation: a manually extracted wider rectangular region of interest around the ligament, given as position (roiX, roiY, roiZ) and size (roiWidth, roiHeight, roiDepth)
Other metadata columns: volumeFilename, kneeLR (left/right knee), examId (and volumeId)
details.file_structure: 917 12-bit grayscale sagittal knee MRI volumes stored as Python pickle (.pck) files (each a single 3D NumPy array, one file per volume), plus one comma-delimited metadata.csv holding the per-volume aclDiagnosis label and ROI coordinates; an example.py loader is shipped with the dataset.
details.extra: No official train/test split (the source paper, Stajduhar et al., CMPB 2017, evaluated with cross-validation); labels are single-reader radiologist diagnoses and the class distribution is strongly imbalanced (reported ~690 healthy, 172 partially injured, 55 completely ruptured of the 917 volumes). Note: the dataset's descriptive text lists conditions as (1) healthy, (2) partially injured, (3) completely ruptured, but the actual aclDiagnosis column in metadata.csv is 0/1/2-coded.

name: Digital Hand Atlas
full_name: Digital Hand Atlas for Skeletal Development
description: Reference collection of ~1,400 normal pediatric hand radiographs across ages, sexes and ethnic groups for bone-age assessment.

label_schema: Primary label: bone age assessment ground truth - each left-hand radiograph carries two independent radiologist bone-age readings (fields 'Reading1' and 'Reading2')
Chronological age and date of birth recorded per subject (basis for bone-age comparison)
Gender: Female / Male
Race/ethnicity: ASI (Asian), BLK (African American), CAU (Caucasian), HIS (Hispanic)
Age group index 0-18 (19 groups spanning ages 0 to 18 years)
Additional per-subject metadata: height, weight, trunk height, Tanner stage, exam date
details.file_structure: Collection of pediatric left-hand radiograph images with per-subject tabular metadata (readings/demographics) held in a relational database; browsable/queryable by race, gender, and age group, with the full atlas available as a ~7GB download.
details.extra: Multi-reader annotation: two independent bone-age readings are provided per image; the atlas consists of normal-development pediatric hand radiographs intended as a reference for computer-aided bone age assessment. Exact total image count and on-disk image file format are not stated on the official page and were not verified.

name: LERA
description: Lower-extremity radiographs (foot, knee, ankle, hip) from 182 patients with radiologist normal/abnormal labels.

label_schema: Binary patient-level classification: normal (y=0) vs abnormal (y=1)
'Abnormal' is a catch-all covering varied findings including degeneration, hardware, arthritis, and fractures, among others
Radiograph type / body-part field per study: foot, knee, ankle, or hip
No fine-grained finding categories or segmentation/bounding-box annotations - abnormality is a single binary label
Note: one label is assigned per patient and applies to ALL of that patient's images (patient-level, not image-level)
details.file_structure: A single .csv file maps patient ID numbers to their binary diagnosis label (normal/abnormal) and radiograph type (body part); the radiograph image files are grouped per patient. Images are highly variable in size, resolution, and color (collected 2003-2014) and may include duplicates.
details.extra: Multi-reader ground truth: the original attending radiologist's read was re-adjudicated by two additional board-certified radiologists (each ~6 years post-graduate experience) who independently labeled the images, with final labels by majority-vote consensus among the two radiologists and the prospective exam report. LERA (182 patients) was released specifically as a held-out TEST set for generalized lower-extremity abnormality detection, so no train/validation split is provided.

name: RSNA Screening Mammography
full_name: RSNA Screening Mammography Breast Cancer Detection (2023)
description: De-identified screening mammograms (~54,700 DICOM images) with a cancer label and BI-RADS/density metadata for breast-cancer detection.

label_schema: Task: image/breast-level binary classification - predict presence of breast cancer
cancer - target label, 0/1, whether malignancy was found in that breast
biopsy - 0/1, whether a follow-up biopsy was performed on the breast
invasive - 0/1, if the breast is positive for cancer, whether the cancer is invasive
BIRADS - 0 (needs follow-up), 1 (negative), or 2 (normal)
density - breast density rating A, B, C, or D (A=mostly fatty ... D=extremely dense)
implant - 0/1, whether the patient has breast implants
laterality - L or R (which breast the image is of)
view - mammography view orientation (e.g. CC, MLO, ML, LM, AT)
age - patient age in years
difficult_negative_case - True/False, flags hard negatives
Metadata IDs: site_id, patient_id, image_id, machine_id; prediction_id (patient_id + laterality) used for test submission
details.file_structure: Images are DICOM files laid out as train_images/{patient_id}/{image_id}.dcm (test_images/ for test); labels/metadata are in train.csv with one row per image keyed by patient_id and image_id, plus a sample_submission.csv keyed by prediction_id.
details.splits: Training set: ~54,706 images from ~11,913 patients across 2 sites; official test set is hidden (held-out for Kaggle scoring), so no released validation count.
details.extra: Highly imbalanced - only ~2% of images are cancer-positive; ground truth combines radiologists' assessments with follow-up pathology results, and biopsy/invasive/BIRADS/density fields are provided for train only (not available at inference). Dataset contributed by mammography screening programs in Australia and the U.S.

name: CBIS-DDSM
full_name: Curated Breast Imaging Subset of DDSM
segmentation_detail: ROI masks and bounding boxes for masses and calcifications
description: Curated DICOM-converted subset of DDSM screening mammograms with pathology labels, BI-RADS and ROI segmentations.

label_schema: Lesion type: mass or calcification
Pathology: BENIGN / BENIGN_WITHOUT_CALLBACK / MALIGNANT; BI-RADS assessment 0–5; breast density 1–4
ROI segmentation mask + cropped patch per lesion
details.file_structure: DICOM full images + ROI mask + cropped images; CSV descriptors for mass/calc train and test separately.
details.splits: ~2,458 train / ~645 test lesions (predefined)

name: VinDr-Mammo
segmentation_detail: bounding-box annotations for findings (BI-RADS 3-5)
description: Full-field digital mammography benchmark of 5,000 four-view exams with breast-level BI-RADS/density and bounding-box finding annotations.

label_schema: Breast-level BI-RADS assessment: category 1-5 (per breast/image)
Breast density: BI-RADS density A, B, C, or D (per breast/image)

Bounding-box findings (10 categories): Mass, Suspicious Calcification, Asymmetry, Focal Asymmetry, Global Asymmetry, Architectural Distortion, Suspicious Lymph Node, Skin Thickening, Skin Retraction, Nipple Retraction
Each bounding-box finding carries its own local BI-RADS assessment (only findings graded BI-RADS 3, 4, or 5 are annotated)
Bounding boxes given as pixel coordinates xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax
details.file_structure: Full-field digital mammograms stored as DICOM under images/ (one folder per study, 4 files each: L/R x CC/MLO views); labels in CSV files (breast-level_annotations.csv, finding_annotations.csv, metadata.csv) with bounding boxes as xmin/ymin/xmax/ymax columns.
details.splits: 5,000 studies (20,000 images) split into 4,000 training and 1,000 test studies, stratified to preserve BI-RADS and finding distributions.
details.extra: Each exam was independently double-read by two radiologists with disagreements resolved by a third arbitrating radiologist; findings graded BI-RADS 1-2 (benign) were not given bounding boxes.

name: CMMD
description: Mammography database of 1,775 Chinese patients with biopsy-confirmed benign/malignant labels and molecular subtype for a subset.

label_schema: Pathology classification (image/breast-level): Benign or Malignant, biopsy-confirmed
Abnormality type: calcification or mass
Molecular subtype (subset of 749 patients / 1,498 mammographies): Luminal A, Luminal B, HER2-positive (HER2-enriched), Triple-negative
Breast laterality: L or R (LeftRight column)
Patient age (Age column)
details.file_structure: Images are DICOM (converted from original TIFF), organized in the standard TCIA per-patient folder hierarchy; labels are in a single Excel clinical-data spreadsheet (e.g., CMMD_clinicaldata_revision.xlsx) keyed by patient ID (ID1/ID2), with columns for LeftRight, Age, number of images, abnormality, classification, and subtype. No segmentation masks or ROI/RTSTRUCT files are provided.
details.extra: This is a classification-only dataset with no pixel-level annotations; molecular subtype labels exist only for a 749-patient subset, while benign/malignant classification and abnormality (calcification/mass) cover the full cohort (1,775 patients, 3,728 studies, 5,202 images).

name: INbreast
segmentation_detail: expert pixel-level lesion contours (XML)
description: Full-field digital mammography database (410 images) from Porto with BI-RADS, density and expert XML lesion contours; access by request.

label_schema: Pixel-level ROI contours (expert-drawn) per finding, each ROI tagged by a 'Name' lesion type
ROI finding types: Mass
ROI finding types: Calcification
ROI finding types: Cluster (microcalcification cluster)
ROI finding types: Spiculated Region
ROI finding types: Distortion (architectural distortion)
ROI finding types: Asymmetry
ROI finding types: Point / pectoral muscle annotation
Per-image metadata (spreadsheet): BI-RADS assessment category (0, 1, 2, 3, 4a, 4b, 4c, 5, 6)
Per-image metadata: ACR breast density category (1-4)
Per-image metadata: laterality (L/R), view (CC / MLO)
Per-image binary finding flags: mass, micros (calcifications), distortion, asymmetry; plus free-text findings notes (in Portuguese)
details.file_structure: Images are full-field digital mammograms stored as DICOM (.dcm) files; per-image expert lesion contours are stored as separate XML files in Apple plist / OsiriX format (ROIs with a 'Name' type and 'Point_px' polygon vertices in pixel coordinates); global case/image metadata (BI-RADS, ACR density, laterality, view, finding flags) is provided in an accompanying spreadsheet (CSV/XLS).
details.extra: 115 cases / 410 images total (90 cases with both breasts = 4 views each, 25 mastectomy cases = 2 views each); acquired on a Siemens MammoNovation FFDM system. Not every image is annotated with contours for every finding type, and contour segmentation may be incomplete for some cases; masks must be rasterized from the XML polygon points (e.g. via cv2.fillPoly).

name: Duke Breast Cancer MRI
full_name: Dynamic Contrast-Enhanced MRI of Breast Cancer Patients (Duke)
multi_sequence_detail: DCE-MRI: non-fat-sat T1, fat-sat pre-contrast and 3-4 post-contrast sequences
segmentation_detail: radiologist 3D bounding boxes around lesions
description: DCE-MRI of 922 invasive breast cancer patients with 3D tumor bounding boxes plus extensive clinical, genomic and radiomic data.

label_schema: Radiologist-drawn 3D bounding box around the primary tumor per patient (one box per case), given as pixel/slice index ranges: Start Row, End Row, Start Column, End Column, Start Slice, End Slice

Clinical/pathology features per patient: demographics (age, race/ethnicity, menopausal status), tumor receptor status (ER, PR, HER2), molecular subtype, tumor grade and histologic type, staging, treatment (surgery, chemo, radiation), and outcomes (recurrence, survival)
