922 computer-extracted / imaging feature values and MRI acquisition + mammography/ultrasound descriptors provided as tabular columns
details.file_structure: Images are DICOM MRI series (pre-contrast and multiple post-contrast dynamic T1 sequences) organized per patient/study; annotations are supplied as separate XLSX spreadsheets ('Annotation Boxes', 'Clinical and Other Features', 'Imaging Features', and a filepath-mapping file) keyed by Patient ID (Breast_MRI_XXX), not as per-image mask files.
details.extra: The primary lesion annotation is a single axis-aligned 3D bounding box per patient (row/column/slice index ranges) rather than a voxel-level segmentation mask; the box is drawn on the first post-contrast sequence.

name: Breast-Diagnosis
multi_sequence_detail: 3+ MR pulse sequences (T2, STIR, BLISS) with contrast; plus PET, CT, mammography
description: Multi-modality breast imaging (multi-sequence MR, PET, CT, mammography) for 88 subjects with lesion-type and biomarker data.

label_schema: Case/diagnosis categories: high-risk normals, DCIS, fibroids, and lobular carcinomas
Pathology molecular markers per patient: ER, PR, HER2 status and Oncotype DX recurrence scores
BI-RADS MRI descriptive features extracted from imaging reports
Approximate lesion localization: key image slice number with approximate X-Y center position when a mass was found (point/location annotation, not a pixel segmentation)
Demographic/clinical data per subject
details.file_structure: Radiology images are stored as DICOM (MR, CT, PET/PT, and MG modalities) organized per subject/study/series in the standard TCIA layout; the labels/annotations are supplied as a separate XLSX spreadsheet containing clinical, pathology, molecular-marker, BI-RADS, and lesion-location fields.
details.extra: No tumor segmentations, RTSTRUCT, or DICOM-SEG objects are provided; lesion 'annotation' is limited to an approximate key-slice X-Y center point, and molecular/pathology labels come only from the companion spreadsheet. Collection covers 88 subjects (148 studies, 429 series).

name: BCS-DBT
full_name: Breast Cancer Screening — Digital Breast Tomosynthesis
segmentation_detail: bounding boxes for lesions
description: Large digital breast tomosynthesis screening dataset of 5,060 patients with normal/actionable/benign/cancer labels and lesion bounding boxes.

label_schema: Per-volume classification into 4 mutually exclusive study labels: Normal, Actionable (recall requested, not biopsied), Benign (biopsy-proven), Cancer (biopsy-proven)
Lesion bounding boxes provided ONLY for biopsy-proven volumes (benign and cancer cases)
Box class field: 'benign' or 'cancer'
Architectural distortion flagged via a binary 'AD' column
details.file_structure: Images are DICOM reconstructed DBT volumes (one 3D volume per view, up to 4 views RCC/LCC/RMLO/LMLO per exam); labels/annotations are separate CSV spreadsheets - a file-paths/label CSV mapping PatientID + StudyUID + View to Normal/Actionable/Benign/Cancer, and a boxes CSV with columns PatientID, StudyUID, View, Slice (central slice of lesion), X, Y, Width, Height (0-indexed box), Class, AD, and VolumeSlices.
details.splits: Total 5,060 patients / 22,032 DBT volumes. Train: 4,362 patients (19,148 volumes); Validation: 280 patients (1,163 volumes); Test: 418 patients (1,721 volumes).
details.extra: Bounding boxes mark only the biopsied lesion on its central slice (single-slice annotation with a listed VolumeSlices for evaluation tolerance); Normal and Actionable volumes have no box annotations. Companion CSVs are distributed alongside the DICOM collection on TCIA.

name: ISPY1 (ACRIN 6657)
full_name: Breast DCE-MRI from the I-SPY 1 / ACRIN 6657 trial
multi_sequence_detail: DCE-MRI across 4 neoadjuvant-chemo timepoints; T2 + pre/post-contrast T1
segmentation_detail: functional tumor volume segmentations
description: Multi-center serial DCE-MRI (222 patients, 4 timepoints) with functional tumor volume segmentations for predicting chemotherapy response.

label_schema: Functional Tumor Volume (FTV): binary segmentation mask of enhancing tumor voxels, defined on DCE-MRI by peak-enhancement / signal-enhancement-ratio (SER) thresholding criteria (the trial's primary imaging biomarker)
Tumor Volume of Interest (VOI): a 3D rectangular bounding box drawn to enclose the enhancing tumor region plus surrounding peritumoral tissue, used as the input region for FTV/SER computation
Structural Tumor Volume (STV): full 3D voxel-wise delineation of the entire primary lesion, including both enhancing and non-enhancing tumor regions (companion segmentation analysis result)
Note: masks are single-lesion binary tumor labels (foreground = tumor), not multi-class organ/tissue segmentations
details.file_structure: Main TCIA collection is DICOM: breast DCE-MRI series plus derived SEG (DICOM-SEG) functional-tumor-volume analysis maps generated at UCSF, organized by TCIA patient/study/series folders with clinical & outcome variables in accompanying Excel spreadsheets; the separate STV/FTV/VOI tumor-segmentation analysis result is distributed as NIfTI images/masks (~6.1 GB) with radiomic features in XLSX and a CaPTk parameter CSV.
details.extra: No ML train/val/test split; the collection is instead tiered into quality-control Levels 0-3 (Level 0 = 222 subjects/847 studies down to Level 3 = 162 subjects). The Structural Tumor Volume (STV) segmentations come from a separate TCIA analysis result covering 163 subjects / 1,467 studies and were reviewed and approved by a board-certified fellowship-trained breast radiologist; FTV segmentations were produced centrally by the UCSF Breast Imaging Research Program.

name: ISPY2
multi_sequence_detail: DCE-MRI + T2 across 4 treatment timepoints; derived parametric maps
segmentation_detail: functional tumor volume masks (DICOM SEG)
description: I-SPY 2 trial serial breast DCE-MRI (719 patients, 4 timepoints) with parametric maps and DICOM-SEG functional tumor volume segmentations.

label_schema: Functional Tumor Volume (FTV) analysis mask - a DICOM SEG segmentation of the tumor region derived from DCE-MRI (series description 'ISPY2: VOLSER: uni-lateral cropped: Analysis Mask')

Mask is bit-encoded, capturing the FTV computation steps: pre-contrast background threshold, minimum percent-enhancement threshold (nominally 70%), a manually defined rectangular VOI, and manually defined 'OMIT' regions excluding non-tumor enhancement
Accompanying parametric maps (derived DICOM objects): PEearly (percent enhancement ~120-150 s), PElate (percent enhancement ~450 s), SER (signal enhancement ratio, scaled x1000 for integer storage)
Clinical/outcome labels in supplementary spreadsheets: demographics, molecular/receptor status, treatment arm, follow-up, and pathologic complete response (pCR)
details.file_structure: DICOM DCE-MRI image series with the FTV segmentation stored as a separate DICOM SEG series (series number = original DCE series root + 1900) plus derived parametric-map objects; clinical outcomes and radiomic features provided as supplementary Excel spreadsheets.
details.extra: Collection covers ~719 patients from the I-SPY2 TRIAL (neoadjuvant breast cancer); supplementary clinical/radiomic spreadsheets are available only for a ~384-patient subset, and the maps/segmentations are stated to be 'believed identical' to those in the primary trial analyses (not independently re-verified).

name: BreastDM
full_name: BreastDM: DCE-MRI dataset for breast tumor segmentation and classification
multi_sequence_detail: DCE-MRI with pre-contrast, post-contrast and subtraction sequences
segmentation_detail: tumor segmentation masks (PNG)
description: Breast DCE-MRI dataset of 232 cases with tumor segmentation masks and benign/malignant labels, distributed via GitHub.

label_schema: Two annotated tasks: binary classification and tumor segmentation (from breast DCE-MRI)
Classification: 2 classes (num_class=2) - 'B' = benign, 'M' = malignant, encoded by folder name
Segmentation: single-class binary tumor mask (num-classes=1) - foreground pixels = breast tumor region, background = 0
No sub-typing, BI-RADS, or multi-class tissue labels - malignancy label is patient/tumor-level benign-vs-malignant only
details.file_structure: Processed 2D slices are distributed via Google Drive: for classification, .jpg images sorted into B/ and M/ folders; for segmentation, paired .png files in images/ and masks/ directories matched by identical filenames (001.png, 002.png, ...) where masks are single-channel binary PNGs; the repo also hosts raw sample cases as DICOM (.dcm) organized as Benign|Malignant / BreaDM-Be|Ma-<id> / <series> with DCE-MRI series (VIBRANT pre-contrast, VIBRANT+C1..C8 post-contrast, and SUB1..SUB8 subtraction series).
details.extra: No fixed official train/val/test split is provided; the classification training code defaults to a configurable 80/20 split (--split_train_ratio 0.8). The repo separately links a 'RoI image' Google Drive archive of tumor region-of-interest crops.

name: EMBED
description: Large, racially diverse Emory mammography dataset (2D, C-view and DBT) with 60,000 lesions linked to BI-RADS descriptors and pathologic outcomes.

label_schema: BI-RADS assessment (`asses` field): 0=Additional evaluation needed, 1=Negative, 2=Benign, 3=Probably benign, 4=Suspicious, 5=Highly suggestive of malignancy, 6=Known biopsy-proven malignancy
Breast density (`tissueden`, BI-RADS A-D): 1=Almost entirely fat (A), 2=Scattered fibroglandular (B), 3=Heterogeneously dense (C), 4=Extremely dense (D)
Pathologic severity (`path_severity`): 0=Invasive cancer, 1=Non-invasive cancer, 2=High-risk lesion, 3=Borderline lesion, 4=Benign, 5=Negative/normal, 6=Non-breast cancer
Detailed pathology diagnoses in text columns path1-path10
BI-RADS imaging descriptors: mass shape (`massshape`), mass margin (`massmargin`), mass density (`massdens`), calcification type (`calcfind`), calcification distribution (`calcdistri`)
Lesion ROI bounding boxes (`ROI_coords`) as (ymin, xmin, ymax, xmax) with a `match_level` reliability score (1=primary, 2=secondary)
Image type (`FinalImageType`): 2D, 3D (DBT tomosynthesis), C-view synthetic; laterality (L/R) and view position (CC, MLO, etc.)
details.file_structure: Images are DICOM files organized on disk as ./cohort/patientID/studyID/SOPinstanceUID.dcm; labels come as CSV files — EMBED_OpenData_metadata.csv (one row per image file, with DICOM tags, ROI_coords, and file paths), a reduced metadata variant, and EMBED_OpenData_clinical.csv (one row per finding, with BI-RADS assessment, density, and pathology labels).
details.extra: No official train/test split is provided; clinical and metadata CSVs must be joined on patient ID (empi_anon), exam ID (acc_anon), and laterality (clinical `side` vs metadata `ImageLateralityFinal`) to link labels/ROIs to the correct images. The full release contains ~3.4M+ images with roughly 60,000 annotated lesions across 110,000+ patients.

name: HECKTOR
full_name: HEad and neCK TumOR segmentation and outcome prediction challenge
paired_text_detail: clinical data (age, sex, HPV, treatment) and recurrence-free survival endpoints
segmentation_detail: primary tumor (GTVp) and lymph node (GTVn) contours
description: MICCAI challenge for automatic segmentation of head and neck tumors and lymph nodes and outcome prediction in FDG-PET/CT.

label_schema: Label 1 — primary tumor GTVp
Label 2 — metastatic lymph nodes GTVn
Endpoint: recurrence-free survival (time + event)
details.file_structure: Per patient: CT.nii.gz, PT.nii.gz (PET) and a GTV label NIfTI; clinical CSV with HPV, treatment and survival.
details.splits: ~524 train / ~359 test (2022, 9 centers)

name: HNSCC
full_name: Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma (MD Anderson)
paired_text_detail: demographics, risk factors, stage, recurrence, survival and treatment data
segmentation_detail: primary and nodal gross tumor volume contours (RTSTRUCT)
description: TCIA collection of imaging, radiation therapy and clinical data from 627 head and neck squamous cell carcinoma patients at MD Anderson.

label_schema: GTVp - primary gross tumor volume (RTSTRUCT contour)
GTVn - nodal gross tumor volume (RTSTRUCT contour)
ROIs named per AAPM TG-263 nomenclature; manual segmentations by radiation oncologists
details.file_structure: DICOM images (CT, PET/PT, MR) with radiotherapy DICOM objects (RTSTRUCT for tumor contours, plus RTPLAN and RTDOSE) organized per-patient on TCIA.
details.extra: Manual GTV contours are provided for the oropharyngeal-cancer subset (~412 patients), not for all 627 patients in the full collection.

name: HN1
paired_text_detail: demographic, follow-up, chemotherapy and survival data
segmentation_detail: pre-treatment gross tumor volume (DICOM SEG and RTSTRUCT)
description: TCIA radiomics collection of pre-treatment CT and clinical/outcome data for 137 head and neck cancer patients from MAASTRO.

label_schema: Primary gross tumor volume (GTV) of the primary neoplasm, manually delineated on pre-treatment CT by an experienced radiation oncologist
Single structure of interest (primary-tumor GTV); no organs-at-risk or nodal GTV schema documented on the collection page
details.file_structure: DICOM-based collection: CT (and some PT) image series with tumor delineations provided as DICOM RTSTRUCT (from v1) and DICOM SEG (added in v2, Sept 2019); accompanying clinical/demographic data provided as tabular files with a data dictionary.
details.extra: 137 head-and-neck squamous cell carcinoma patients; images fall under the NIH Controlled Data Access Policy due to facial-reconstruction re-identification risk. Clinical data includes demographics, treatment, and follow-up/survival fields (a 'chemotherapy_given' field added in v3, July 2020).

name: TCGA-HNSC
full_name: TCGA Head-Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma Collection
paired_text_detail: matched clinical, pathological and genomic data via TCGA/GDC
segmentation_detail: radiation therapy structures (RTSTRUCT) for a subset
license: CC BY 3.0; some imaging controlled access
description: TCIA imaging collection for 227 head and neck cancer patients matched to genomic and clinical data in The Cancer Genome Atlas.

label_schema: Annotations are original clinical radiation-therapy DICOM objects (RTSTRUCT structure sets, plus RTDOSE and RTPLAN), available only for a subset of patients

RTSTRUCT files contain clinician-drawn ROI contours from treatment planning (target volumes and organs at risk); the collection does NOT publish a curated or standardized list of structure/organ names, so exact contour labels vary by institution and are not enumerated
No image-level classification labels; matched clinical/genomic/pathology metadata is available separately via the NCI Genomic Data Commons (GDC), not as imaging annotations
details.file_structure: All data is stored as DICOM under a per-patient TCIA folder hierarchy (patient > study > series); imaging series are CT/PT/MR DICOM, and the radiation-therapy annotations are separate DICOM RTSTRUCT (contours), RTDOSE, and RTPLAN series rather than DICOM-SEG or NIfTI/CSV.
details.extra: This is a TCIA archival collection (227 subjects; CT, PT, MR, RTDOSE, RTSTRUCT, RTPLAN, OT), not a segmentation challenge — RT structures are original clinical contours available only for a subset of patients, so there is no consistent, verified per-class segmentation schema across the cohort.

name: Head-Neck-PET-CT
paired_text_detail: histopathology, tumor grade and follow-up outcomes
segmentation_detail: radiotherapy GTV contours (RTSTRUCT)
description: TCIA collection of FDG-PET/CT and radiotherapy planning CT with GTV contours and outcome data for 298 head and neck cancer patients.

label_schema: Radiotherapy GTV contours stored as named ROIs in DICOM-RTSTRUCT (no integer label indices)
GTV primary - gross tumor volume at the primary tumor site
GTV lymph nodes - gross tumor volume of involved lymph nodes
A companion spreadsheet lists the exact ROI/structure names used for 'GTV primary' and 'GTV lymph nodes' across the RTSTRUCT files (names are inconsistent and comma-separated per patient)
Clinical/outcome labels per patient: age, sex, TNM/tumor stage, histopathological type, tumor grade, and follow-up outcomes (metastases, survival)
details.file_structure: Per-patient DICOM studies containing CT, PET, and RTSTRUCT (plus RTDOSE, RTPLAN, and REG) series; GTV contours are stored as named ROIs inside the DICOM-RTSTRUCT files rather than pixel masks, and clinical/structure-name metadata is provided in accompanying Excel spreadsheets.
details.extra: 298 patients from 4 Quebec institutions (2 of the original 300 excluded); RTSTRUCT ROI naming is not standardized, so the provided structure-name spreadsheet is needed to map ROIs to GTV-primary vs GTV-lymph-node classes.

name: RADCURE
full_name: CT Images from a Large Head and Neck Cohort (RADCURE)
paired_text_detail: demographic, diagnosis, treatment and follow-up data with survival status
segmentation_detail: primary GTV, nodal GTV and 19 organs-at-risk contours
description: TCIA head and neck CT dataset of 3,346 patients treated with definitive radiotherapy, with tumor and organ-at-risk contours and outcomes.

label_schema: Annotations are DICOM-RTSTRUCT region-of-interest contours (polygon ROIs), NOT integer-indexed voxel label maps
GTVp - primary gross tumor volume
GTVn - nodal gross tumor volume
19 organs-at-risk (OAR) contours per applicable patient, with TG263-standardized ROI names (individual OAR names not enumerated on the TCIA page; 'parotid' is one explicitly referenced example)
Contour ROI names standardized via a provided mapping spreadsheet from original contour names to TG263 nomenclature
Companion tabular clinical/outcome labels (demographics, diagnosis, treatment, follow-up/survival status) provided alongside the imaging annotations

name: QIN-HeadNeck
paired_text_detail: clinical data (demographics, treatment, follow-up) in XLSX
segmentation_detail: DICOM SEG segmentations plus DICOM SR quantitative PET/CT results
license: CC BY 3.0; some imaging controlled access
description: TCIA collection of serial pre/post-therapy FDG-PET/CT with DICOM SEG/SR quantitative analysis objects for head and neck cancer.

label_schema: DICOM SEG: binary segmentations of PET-avid tumor/lesion regions of interest delineated on the PET/CT scans (one segment per ROI; no fixed multi-class integer palette — each SEG encodes a region)
DICOM SR (TID 1500 measurement report): quantitative SUVbw (standardized uptake value, body-weight normalized) statistics computed over each segmented region, i.e. structured PET/CT analysis results
DICOM RWV (Real World Value Mapping): objects that map stored PET pixel values to SUVbw units
DICOM SR 'Clinical Data' (series description = 'Clinical Data'): structured demographic, exposure, treatment, diagnosis, follow-up, and family-history records
details.file_structure: All data are DICOM, organized on TCIA by patient/study/series — images as CT and PT (PET) series; labels/annotations as DICOM SEG (segmentation), DICOM SR (quantitative measurement reports and clinical-data reports), and DICOM RWV (real-world value mapping) objects; a supplementary clinical-data spreadsheet is also provided in XLSX format.
details.extra: Collection = 279 subjects (1,032 studies) of head-and-neck cancer PET/CT. The DICOM SEG, SR, and RWV analysis objects were added in Version 2 (2017) as a standards-based re-encoding of quantitative PET/CT results (Fedorov et al.); exact SUV sub-metric names and per-lesion counts could not be fully enumerated from the public pages.

name: DENTEX
full_name: Dental Enumeration and Diagnosis on Panoramic X-rays (MICCAI 2023)
description: Panoramic dental X-rays hierarchically annotated (FDI) for abnormal-tooth detection with quadrant, enumeration and diagnosis.

label_schema: Hierarchical FDI-system annotation with 3 levels: quadrant, tooth enumeration, diagnosis (bounding-box detection)
Quadrant (4 classes): 1 = top-right, 2 = top-left, 3 = bottom-left, 4 = bottom-right
Enumeration: tooth position 1-8 within a quadrant (FDI numbering, e.g. tooth 48 = quadrant 4 position 8); combined with quadrant gives 32 tooth classes
Diagnosis (4 classes): Caries, Deep Caries, Periapical Lesion, Impacted (labeled per abnormal tooth)
Three progressively-labeled sets: Set 1 quadrant-only, Set 2 quadrant+enumeration, Set 3 fully labeled quadrant-enumeration-diagnosis (the benchmarked task)
details.file_structure: Panoramic dental X-ray images (PNG/JPG) stored in per-set image folders, with object-detection annotations (bounding boxes + hierarchical category labels) provided as COCO-style JSON files; distributed via Grand Challenge / Hugging Face.
details.splits: Fully-annotated diagnosis set (1,005 X-rays): 705 train (labels released), 50 validation (labels withheld), 250 test (hidden). Partially-labeled sets: 693 quadrant X-rays and 634 quadrant-enumeration X-rays. Plus 1,571 unlabeled X-rays for optional pre-training.
details.extra: Annotations are hierarchical: only Set 3 carries diagnosis labels, and diagnoses are assigned only to abnormal teeth; the paper/benchmark is HierarchicalDet (arXiv:2305.19112).

name: Tufts Dental Database
full_name: Tufts Dental Database (TDD): multimodal panoramic X-ray dataset
paired_text_detail: radiologist think-aloud text descriptions plus eye-tracking gaze maps
segmentation_detail: teeth masks, maxillomandibular masks and abnormality masks
description: 1,000 expert-annotated panoramic dental radiographs with tooth/abnormality masks plus radiologist eye-tracking and text descriptions.

label_schema: 1000 panoramic dental radiographs, each with expert labeling of abnormalities and teeth
Segmentation: teeth masks (tooth region segmentation)
Segmentation: maxillomandibular region masks (jaw/mandible-maxilla region)
Segmentation: abnormality masks (pixel regions marking the pathological finding)
Per-image abnormality annotation across 5 categories: (1) anatomical location, (2) peripheral characteristics, (3) radiodensity, (4) effect on the surrounding structure, (5) abnormality category
Radiologist's expertise captured as eye-tracking gaze maps + think-aloud (text) protocol per image
details.file_structure: Data is organized into separate folders for the radiographs and for each annotation type (teeth masks, maxillomandibular masks, abnormality masks, plus eye-tracking maps and label text); radiographs and segmentation masks are stored as image files and the 5-category abnormality labels as accompanying text/label files.
details.extra: Multimodal, multi-reader dataset: it captures a radiologist's diagnostic process via eye-tracking and a think-aloud protocol alongside the standard masks and labels. Exact on-disk file formats (JPG/PNG/JSON) and any expert-vs-student annotation split could not be confirmed from the public page without downloading the data.

name: ToothFairy2
segmentation_detail: 42-class 3D masks: teeth (FDI), mandible, maxilla, alveolar canals, sinuses, implants
description: First fully-annotated public CBCT dataset with 42-class 3D expert segmentations of dental and maxillofacial structures at 0.3mm voxels.

label_schema: Multi-class 3D semantic segmentation of CBCT; 42 foreground classes; each voxel has one integer label
Label 0 - Background
Label 1 - Lower Jawbone (Mandible)
Label 2 - Upper Jawbone (Maxilla)
Label 3 - Left Inferior Alveolar Canal
Label 4 - Right Inferior Alveolar Canal
Label 5 - Left Maxillary Sinus
Label 6 - Right Maxillary Sinus
Label 7 - Pharynx
Label 8 - Bridge (dental prosthesis)
Label 9 - Crown
Label 10 - Implant
Teeth use FDI World Dental Federation codes AS the integer label value (not contiguous)
Labels 11-18 - Upper-right teeth: 11 central incisor, 12 lateral incisor, 13 canine, 14 first premolar, 15 second premolar, 16 first molar, 17 second molar, 18 third molar (wisdom)
Labels 21-28 - Upper-left teeth (FDI 21-28, central incisor through third molar)
Labels 31-38 - Lower-left teeth (FDI 31-38, central incisor through third molar)
Labels 41-48 - Lower-right teeth (FDI 41-48, central incisor through third molar)
details.file_structure: CBCT volumes and their single multi-class label mask are stored as .mha files in the nnU-Net dataset layout (imagesTr / labelsTr with dataset.json), all at 0.3 mm isotropic voxel spacing.
details.splits: Public training set: 480 volumes total, split into Set A (417 volumes, overlapping the original ToothFairy v1 data) and Set B (63 new wider field-of-view volumes showing full upper teeth); the test set is held private and its count is not disclosed.
details.extra: Label integers are intentionally non-contiguous: tooth values mirror FDI codes so 19, 20, 29, 30, 39, 40 are never used; the '42 classes' count refers to foreground structures (10 anatomy/appliance labels + 32 teeth), excluding background. Released under CC BY-SA.

name: CTooth
full_name: CTooth: 3D Tooth Volume Segmentation on CBCT
segmentation_detail: 3D tooth-volume voxel masks
description: Fully annotated CBCT tooth-volume segmentation benchmark of 22 volumes, extended by CTooth+ with 146 additional unlabeled volumes.

label_schema: Binary tooth-region segmentation (single foreground class, not per-tooth)
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - tooth (whole-tooth foreground; all teeth merged into one class, no individual tooth numbering/classes)
Voxel masks manually delineated by 4 dental trainees (~4 years experience) in ITK-SNAP, marked slice-by-slice in axial view then refined in coronal/sagittal views
details.file_structure: 22 CBCT volumes totaling 7,363 axial slices from 22 patients, of which 4,243 slices contain tooth annotations; each scan is paired with a binary 3D tooth-volume voxel mask (images resized to 256x256 in the benchmark); the exact on-disk container format (e.g. NIfTI/NRRD) is not stated in the paper.
details.extra: Annotations took ~10 months to collect/label/review (~6 h to annotate a volume plus ~1 h to check/refine); in-plane resolution ~0.25x0.25 mm and slice thickness 0.25-0.3 mm. Caveat: this is whole-tooth (foreground vs background) segmentation only - it does not provide per-tooth instance labels, tooth numbering (e.g. FDI), or pulp/root sub-structures.

name: autoPET I
full_name: Automated Lesion Segmentation in Whole-Body FDG-PET/CT (autoPET 2022)
segmentation_detail: manual whole-body FDG-avid tumor lesion masks
description: MICCAI 2022 challenge for automated tumor lesion segmentation on whole-body FDG-PET/CT (900 patients, melanoma/lymphoma/NSCLC and controls).

label_schema: Binary whole-body mask of FDG-avid malignant lesions (0 = background/physiologic uptake, 1 = tumor lesion)
details.file_structure: Per study: CT.nii.gz, SUV.nii.gz (PET SUV), CTres.nii.gz and SEG.nii.gz; also DICOM on TCIA (FDG-PET-CT-Lesions).
details.splits: 1,014 studies / 900 patients (501 positive, 513 negative controls)

name: autoPET III
full_name: Automated Lesion Segmentation in Multitracer PET/CT (autoPET III 2024)
multi_sequence_detail: multi-tracer: FDG and PSMA PET; PET + CT
segmentation_detail: manual whole-body tumor lesion masks for FDG- and PSMA-avid lesions
license: FDG: TCIA Restricted; PSMA: CC BY 4.0
description: Third autoPET edition extending the task to multi-tracer whole-body PET/CT by combining FDG and PSMA collections (1,611 training studies).

label_schema: Binary 3D tumor-lesion segmentation mask (single foreground class)
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - metabolically active (avid) malignant tumor lesion; FDG-avid lesions in the FDG cohort and PSMA-avid lesions (primary prostate tumor and/or metastases) in the PSMA cohort
All lesions manually segmented slice-by-slice on the PET image by trained specialists (no per-lesion or per-organ class labels; masks in negative-control studies are empty)
details.file_structure: On TCIA the data is DICOM - anonymized original PET and CT plus the lesion mask as DICOM-SEG (per study); the autoPET challenge redistributes NIfTI conversions where each study folder holds the CT, PET, resampled CT (CTres), PET SUV volume, and the binary SEG mask as .nii.gz (input channels = PET SUV + resampled CT), with conversion scripts (DICOM/NIfTI/MHA/HDF5) provided on the lab-midas/autoPET GitHub.
details.splits: Training data = 1611 whole-body studies pooled from two cohorts: FDG-PET-CT-Lesions = 1014 studies / 900 patients (501 with lesions across lymphoma, melanoma, NSCLC; 513 negative controls) and PSMA-PET-CT-Lesions = 597 studies / 378 male prostate-cancer patients (539 with lesions, 58 without). Official test set is private/held-out on Grand Challenge (count not publicly disclosed).
details.extra: The two tracer cohorts are multi-center (FDG from University Hospital Tuebingen, PSMA from LMU Munich); the PSMA cohort has lower image resolution and greater heterogeneity than the FDG cohort, and the segmentation task is a single unified binary tumor class despite the differing tracers.

name: FDG-PET-CT-Lesions
full_name: Whole-body FDG-PET/CT dataset with manually annotated tumor lesions
segmentation_detail: manual 3D segmentation of FDG-avid malignant lesions (501 positive, 513 controls)
license: TCIA Restricted (DICOM); NIfTI mirror CC BY-NC 4.0
description: TCIA collection of 1,014 whole-body FDG-PET/CT studies with manually annotated tumor lesions, the FDG training data behind autoPET.

label_schema: Binary segmentation, single foreground class: FDG-avid malignant lesion (tumor). Voxels labeled 1 = lesion, 0 = background
Lesions were all FDG-avid findings identified as malignant based on patient history and prior exams, manually segmented slice-by-slice on the PET images by a single reader
Underlying malignancies among positive cases: lymphoma, melanoma, and non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC)
Negative/control studies contain no PET-positive malignant lesion (empty/all-background mask)
Clinical metadata labels per study (CSV): diagnosis (lymphoma / melanoma / lung cancer / negative), age, sex
details.file_structure: Original whole-body FDG-PET/CT stored as DICOM (paired PET and CT series) with lesion masks as DICOM-SEG; TCIA/challenge organizers also provide conversion scripts to NIfTI, HDF5, and MHA (each subject as a study folder), plus a clinical metadata CSV.
details.extra: This is the autoPET (MICCAI autoPET challenge) database: 1,014 studies from 900 patients (501 positive with lesions, 513 negative controls); segmentations by a single reader (no multi-reader annotations), best reviewed as a PET overlay in 3D Slicer or MITK.

name: PSMA-PET-CT-Lesions
full_name: Whole-body PSMA-PET/CT dataset with manually annotated tumor lesions
segmentation_detail: manual 3D segmentation of PSMA-avid tumor lesions (DICOM SEG)
description: TCIA collection of 597 whole-body PSMA-PET/CT studies from 378 prostate-cancer patients with manually annotated lesions.

label_schema: Binary segmentation: single foreground class = PSMA-avid tumor lesion (voxel value 1) vs background (0)
A lesion segmentation includes the primary tumor and/or all metastases annotated together as one lesion class (not separated by organ or lesion type)
597 whole-body PSMA-PET/CT studies: 539 contain >=1 lesion, 58 contain no lesions (empty/negative masks)
details.file_structure: Per-study DICOM PET and CT image series with a corresponding 3D binary tumor-lesion mask stored as a DICOM SEG file (created with the highdicom package), plus a single TSV metadata table (age, scanner manufacturer/model, PET radionuclide, CT contrast use).
details.extra: Masks were made semi-automatically (threshold-based pre-segmentation inside drawn circular VOIs, then manual slice-by-slice correction); this collection was used for the autoPET III/IV Grand Challenges.

name: ROCO
paired_text_detail: captions + UMLS concepts (CUIs), keywords
description: Multimodal radiology image-caption dataset mined from PubMed Central Open Access articles, spanning multiple modalities and body regions.

label_schema: Free-text caption per image (captions.txt): the original figure caption from the source PubMed Central article
Keywords per image (keywords.txt): terms extracted from the image caption
UMLS Semantic Types per image (semtypes.txt): UMLS SemType labels mapped from the caption
UMLS Concept Unique Identifiers per image (cuis.txt): UMLS CUIs mapped from the caption (used for the ImageCLEF 2019 Concept Detection Task)
Binary image-class organization: each image belongs to either 'radiology' or 'non-radiology' (out-of-class) via folder placement
No pixel-level segmentation, bounding boxes, or per-region annotations — labels are image-level text/concept tags only
details.file_structure: Images are distributed as download links (not pixel data in the repo); layout is data/{train,validation,test}/{radiology,non-radiology}/ with plain-text annotation files per split-and-class: dlinks.txt (PMC download URLs + image IDs), captions.txt, keywords.txt, semtypes.txt, cuis.txt, and licences.txt, and the fetch.py script downloads the JPGs into an images/ subfolder.
details.splits: Three official splits (train, validation, test), each further divided into radiology and non-radiology subsets; exact per-split image counts not verified from the fetched sources.
details.extra: Images come from PubMed Central Open Access articles automatically filtered to non-compound figures; labels are auto-generated (captions from source articles, CUIs/SemTypes via UMLS mapping), not expert manual annotations. Actual image pixels must be fetched via the provided download links using scripts/fetch.py.

name: ROCOv2
size: 79,789 images with captions and UMLS concepts
paired_text_detail: captions + manually curated UMLS CUIs, keywords
description: Updated multimodal radiology image dataset from PMC Open Access with manually curated modality and anatomy concepts, used in ImageCLEFmedical Caption.

label_schema: Free-text caption per image (radiological description mined from PubMed Central open-access articles)
Multi-label UMLS concepts per image as CUI codes (1,947 unique CUIs across the dataset)
Manually curated modality concept for every image, 7 classes: Angiography (DRAN), Combined/e.g. PET-CT (DRCO), CT (DRCT), MRI (DRMR), PET (DRPE), Ultrasound (DRUS), X-ray (DRXR)
Manually curated body region for X-rays only (IRMA-based), 8 classes: Abdomen, Breast, Chest, Cranium, Lower extremity, Pelvis, Spine, Upper extremity
Manually curated directionality for X-rays only, 4 classes: Coronal anteroposterior, Coronal posteroanterior, Sagittal, Transversal
details.file_structure: JPG images bundled per split (train_images.zip, valid_images.zip, test_images.zip) named ROCOv2_2023_<id>.jpg, with parallel CSV label files per split: *_captions.csv (image ID + caption), *_concepts.csv (image ID + CUI list), and *_concepts_manual.csv (image ID + manual modality/region/directionality CUIs), plus a shared cui_mapping.csv (CUI to human-readable name) and license_information.csv.
details.splits: Train 59,958 / Validation 9,904 / Test 9,927 images (79,789 total).
details.extra: Successor to the original ROCO dataset, adding 35,705 new PMC images since 2018; the manual modality/region/direction concepts were validated by a radiologist for quality assurance, unlike the automatically extracted UMLS concepts.

name: MedICaT
full_name: Medical Images, Captions, and Textual References
paired_text_detail: captions, inline textual references and subfigure-subcaption annotations
description: Medical figures with captions, inline references and compound-figure/subcaption annotations extracted from PubMed Central and S2ORC.

label_schema: Figure captions per image (fields: s2_caption and s2orc_caption)
Inline textual references linking a figure to passages in the source paper (s2orc_references)
Subfigure and subcaption annotations for compound figures: 7,507 subcaptions/subfigures across 2,069 compound figures
Per-figure metadata flags: radiology (whether figure is radiological), scope, predicted_type (predicted figure/modality type)
~25,000 figures carry inline references sourced from the ROCO dataset
details.file_structure: JSON-based: each figure is a JSON record with fields such as pdf_hash, fig_key, fig_uri, s2_caption, s2orc_caption, s2orc_references, radiology, scope, predicted_type, and oa_info; image files are named {pdf_hash}_{fig_uri}; subcaption task splits are provided in subcaption_split_keys.json.
details.splits: No explicit train/val/test counts published; official split keys are provided only for the subcaption task via subcaption_split_keys.json.
details.extra: Large-scale multimodal figure-caption dataset (217,060 figures from 131,410 open-access PubMed Central papers); contains NO segmentation, bounding-box, or ROI annotations. Access to the full release requires agreeing to terms/contacting the authors.

name: PMC-OA
paired_text_detail: captions aligned at subfigure/subcaption granularity
description: Large biomedical image-caption archive from PubMed Central Open Access used to pretrain the PMC-CLIP vision-language model.

label_schema: Each example is an image-caption pair with two fields: 'image' (figure/subfigure filename) and 'caption' (free-text description)
Captions are free-text biomedical figure descriptions extracted from PubMed Central Open Access articles - there is NO fixed categorical class set / no segmentation or detection label taxonomy
'pmc_oa' config: <subfigure, subcaption> pairs - subfigures detected by a DETR model, and compound captions split into subcaptions by ChatGPT then aligned to the corresponding subfigure
'pmc_oa_beta' config: <subfigure, caption> pairs - subfigures detected by DETR but retaining the full un-split compound caption
Images span diverse biomedical modalities (e.g. X-ray, MRI, CT, microscopy, plots) rather than a single anatomy/modality
details.file_structure: Images are stored as JPGs inside images.zip with descriptive filenames (e.g. PMC212319_Fig3_4.jpg); labels are line-delimited JSONL metadata files (pmc_oa.jsonl, pmc_oa_beta.jsonl, plus train.jsonl/valid.jsonl/test.jsonl) where each line is an object with 'image' and 'caption' string fields.
details.extra: The repo ships separate train.jsonl / valid.jsonl / test.jsonl metadata files, but exact row counts are not published on the dataset card and the official HF loading script (pmc_oa.py) exposes only a single 'train' split per config; the repo also bundles companion PMC-CLIP model checkpoints (ResNet50 image encoder, text encoder, projection layer).

name: VQA-RAD
paired_text_detail: clinician-generated QA pairs (open- and closed-ended)
description: Radiology VQA dataset of head CT/MRI, chest X-rays and abdominal CTs with clinician-authored question-answer pairs.

label_schema: Visual question answering (VQA) over radiology images
Each record is an (image, question, answer) triple
answer: free-text ground-truth string, ranging from single words (e.g. 'yes', 'no', 'mri') to short phrases (e.g. 'bilateral frontal lobes')
Questions are of two kinds: closed-ended (yes/no or limited-choice) and open-ended (free response); scored separately as closed vs open accuracy
Images span three anatomical regions: head (brain), chest, and abdomen (CT, MRI, and X-ray modalities)
details.file_structure: Distributed as HuggingFace Parquet files with three columns per row - image (PIL JPEG), question (string), answer (string); no separate mask/annotation files, labels are the plain-text answer strings inline.
details.splits: train: 1,793 QA pairs; test: 451 QA pairs (total 2,244 QA pairs over ~314 unique radiology images)
details.extra: This HF release stores only image/question/answer; the richer per-question metadata from the original VQA-RAD paper (e.g. 11 question categories such as modality, plane, organ system, abnormality, and rephrasing type) is NOT included in these Parquet columns.

name: SLAKE
paired_text_detail: bilingual (English-Chinese) QA pairs, vision- and knowledge-based
segmentation_detail: semantic segmentation masks and object-detection boxes per image
description: Bilingual radiology VQA dataset covering 39 organs and 12 diseases, annotated with segmentation masks, bounding boxes and a medical knowledge graph.

label_schema: Primary task = medical Visual Question Answering: 14,028 question-answer pairs over 642 radiology images
Bilingual QA: English and Chinese questions (q_lang field)
answer_type field: OPEN (free-form) or CLOSED (e.g. yes/no) questions
content_type field spans 10 categories: Plane, Quality, Modality, Position, Organ, KG (knowledge-graph-based), Abnormality, Color, Shape, Size
Semantic segmentation masks (pixel-level) annotated with ITK-SNAP covering 39 organs and 12 diseases across the whole body
Object-detection bounding boxes provided per image for the annotated organs/structures
Structural medical knowledge graph: 2,603 English + 2,629 Chinese triples (organ function, disease symptom/cause/treatment, etc.), linked via the QA 'triple' field
Metadata per QA: modality (CT/MRI/X-Ray) and location (anatomical region, e.g. Head, Neck, Chest, Abdomen, Pelvic Cavity)
details.file_structure: QA pairs are stored as JSON (train.json / validate.json / test.json) whose entries reference images by path (e.g. 'xmlab1/source.jpg'); images live in an imgs/ directory with one folder per image (xmlab0 ... xmlab641) holding the JPG plus its segmentation mask (PNG) and detection/bounding-box annotation file.
details.splits: QA-pair splits: train 9,840, validation 2,100, test 2,090 (14,028 total over 642 images)
details.extra: Imaging composition: 282 CT, 181 MRI, 179 X-Ray images across 5 body regions (head, neck, chest, abdomen, pelvic cavity); annotations were made by experienced physicians. Note counts refer to QA pairs, not images (each image has multiple questions).

name: MS-CXR
full_name: MS-CXR: Making the Most of Text Semantics
paired_text_detail: radiologist-verified phrases describing findings, paired to bounding boxes
description: Phrase-grounding benchmark over MIMIC-CXR images with radiologist-annotated bounding boxes paired to real report sentences across eight findings.

label_schema: Locally-aligned phrase-grounding annotations: each item is a bounding box paired with a phrase/sentence from the radiology report describing that finding
8 finding categories: Atelectasis, Cardiomegaly, Consolidation, Edema, Lung Opacity, Pleural Effusion, Pneumonia, Pneumothorax
1,162 image-sentence (bounding-box + text) pairs across 851 unique subjects
1,026 pairs reviewed/edited from existing annotations + 136 newly annotated from scratch
details.file_structure: Chest X-ray images are the public MIMIC-CXR-JPG/v2 images (referenced by MIMIC dicom/study IDs, not redistributed); annotations are shipped as an MS-COCO-style JSON (categories, image metadata, bounding boxes + sentences) plus a convenience CSV with per-annotation rows and a 'split' column (added in v1.1.0).
details.splits: Recommended patient-level 70:15:15 split stratified by finding category and gender: train 817 pairs (595 subjects), validation 169 pairs (128 subjects), test 176 pairs (128 subjects).
details.extra: Derived from MIMIC-CXR v2 (images) with demographics from MIMIC-IV v1.0.0; a credentialed PhysioNet dataset intended as a benchmark for phrase grounding / vision-language alignment (used with the BioViL / MS-CXR work), not a general segmentation or classification set.

name: Chest ImaGenome
paired_text_detail: scene-graph relations linking anatomy boxes to report-derived attributes
description: Scene-graph dataset auto-derived from MIMIC-CXR with 29 anatomy bounding boxes, attributes and longitudinal comparison relations per image.

label_schema: Scene graphs (one per frontal CXR): objects + attributes + relationships derived automatically from MIMIC-CXR reports

29 anatomical object classes with bounding boxes (e.g. left/right lung, left/right lower/mid/upper lung zone, left/right apical zone, left/right costophrenic angle, left/right hilar structures, left/right hemidiaphragm, cardiac silhouette, mediastinum, upper mediastinum, trachea, carina, aortic arch, SVC, cavoatrial junction, right atrium, left/right clavicle, spine, abdomen)
Attributes attached to objects, grouped into categories: anatomicalfinding, disease, nlp (normal/abnormal), technicalassessment, tubesandlines, devices, texture
Attribute relations encoded as <categoryID | relation | label_name> where relation is 'yes' (present/affirmed) or 'no' (negated)
1,256 object-attribute relation combinations between the 29 locations and their attributes
Over 670,000 localized comparison relations (improved / worsened / no change) between anatomical locations across sequential exams
details.file_structure: Two top-level folders: silver_dataset/ (automatically generated) with scene_graph.zip of per-image JSON scene graphs, processed report sentences (.txt), RDF attribute graphs (.json) and patient-level split CSVs; and gold_dataset/ (manually validated 500-patient subset) with tab/text files for object-attribute and comparison relations plus bbox_coordinate_annotations CSVs; bounding boxes are given as CSV coordinates for 224x224 and original image dimensions, and the images themselves come from MIMIC-CXR.
details.splits: Patient-level train/validation/test CSVs are provided in silver_dataset/splits/, but no fixed official counts are stated on the page (gold set = 500 patients / 1000 studies, listed in images_to_avoid.csv and excluded from silver train/val)
details.extra: Labels are largely auto-generated by a rule-based NLP + atlas/Faster-RCNN bounding-box pipeline over MIMIC-CXR reports (silver ~242k images); the 500-patient gold_dataset is manually validated (dual-annotator corrected boxes and sentence-level relations) and should be used for evaluation, not training.

name: REFLACX
full_name: Reports and Eye-Tracking Data for Localization of Abnormalities in Chest X-rays
size: 3,032 eye-tracking + report sessions for 2,616 chest X-rays
paired_text_detail: timestamped dictated report transcriptions synced with gaze data
description: Radiologist eye-tracking and timestamped report transcriptions over MIMIC-CXR images, with bounding boxes and abnormality localizations.

label_schema: Multimodal reader annotations per chest X-ray reading: eye-tracking gaze/fixations, timestamped speech transcription (dictated report), abnormality-localization ellipses, and chest bounding boxes (lungs + heart)
Each ellipse tags a finding label with a certainty rating on a 0-5 scale (0 not selected, 1 Unlikely <10%, 2 Less Likely ~25%, 3 Possibly ~50%, 4 Suspicious/Probably ~75%, 5 Consistent with >90%)

Phase 1 finding set (15): Airway wall thickening, Atelectasis, Consolidation, Emphysema, Enlarged cardiac silhouette, Fibrosis, Fracture, Groundglass opacity, Mass, Nodule, Pleural effusion, Pleural thickening, Pneumothorax, Pulmonary edema, Wide mediastinum

Phases 2-3 finding set (14): Abnormal mediastinal contour, Acute fracture, Atelectasis, Consolidation, Enlarged cardiac silhouette, Enlarged hilum, Groundglass opacity, Hiatal hernia, High lung volume/emphysema, Interstitial lung disease, Lung nodule or mass, Pleural abnormality, Pneumothorax, Pulmonary edema
Chest bounding boxes delineate lungs and heart for anatomical normalization (not disease localization)
details.file_structure: CSV/TXT files per reading laid out under main_data/<id>/ (fixations.csv, anomaly_location_ellipses.csv, chest_bounding_box.csv, timestamps_transcription.csv, transcription.txt plus metadata tables) with a separate gaze_data/ folder holding full 1000 Hz eye-tracking recordings; images are the referenced MIMIC-CXR DICOM/JPGs (not redistributed here).
details.splits: No official train/val/test split; images were sampled so ~20% come from the MIMIC-CXR test set and ~80% from the rest, and original MIMIC-CXR split labels are preserved in the metadata.
details.extra: 3,052 readings (3,032 complete) over 2,616 unique chest X-rays from 2,199 subjects, annotated by 5 radiologists; 109 cases were read by all 5 for inter-reader variability analysis.

name: Medical-Diff-VQA
paired_text_detail: difference-oriented question-answer pairs comparing paired studies
description: Large difference-VQA dataset derived from MIMIC-CXR for answering questions about changes between a patient's main and reference chest X-rays.

label_schema: Visual-question-answering pairs (700,703 total) over MIMIC-CXR chest X-ray image pairs (main image vs. prior reference image of same patient)
7 question types: Abnormality, Presence, View, Location, Type, Level, Difference
Abnormality Q: 'what abnormalities are seen in the image?' (~145,421)
Presence Q: 'is there any evidence of <abnormality>?' (~155,726)
View Q: 'which view is this image taken?' (~56,265)
Location Q: 'where in the image is the <abnormality> located?' (~84,193)
Type Q: 'what type is the <abnormality>?' (~27,478)
Level Q: severity level of the abnormality (~67,296)
Difference Q: 'what has changed compared to the reference image?' - compares main vs. reference image (~164,324)
Answers are free-text (open-ended), not fixed multiple-choice classes
Companion mimic_all.csv carries MIMIC-CXR-JPG metadata incl. 14 disease-category labels and imaging view
details.file_structure: CSV/JSON label files (no images shipped; images pulled from MIMIC-CXR-JPG): mimic_pair_questions.csv holds the QA pairs with columns study_id, subject_id, ref_id (reference study), question_type, question, answer, split; mimic_all.csv holds per-study metadata/disease labels; all_diseases.json holds extracted KeyInfo (abnormalities, locations, types, levels).
details.splits: Each QA row carries a train/val/test 'split' field; the 700,703 pairs derive from 164,324 study pairs, but official per-split counts are not published on the page.
details.extra: Built automatically from MIMIC-CXR radiology reports (credentialed access required); human validation of 1,700 samples reported 97.4% correctness.

name: RadGenome-ChestCT
size: 25,692 CT volumes with 665K grounded report regions + 1.3M VQA
paired_text_detail: multi-granularity grounded reports and grounded QA pairs linked to regions
segmentation_detail: 197 organ-level segmentation masks per volume linked to report sentences
description: Grounded vision-language extension of CT-RATE adding organ segmentation masks, region-linked reports and grounded VQA pairs for 3D chest CT.

label_schema: Organ-level segmentation masks covering 197 anatomical categories, one mask set per CT volume
Grounded (region-guided) reports: each report sentence is linked to the anatomical region it describes (columns include Volumename, Anatomy, and Sentence)
Grounded VQA pairs, organized into 4 question types: abnormality, location, presence, and size
Case-level VQA on disorders (case_disorders)
Each report sentence and each VQA question/answer is tied to a reference segmentation mask (grounding)
details.file_structure: CT volumes and 197-category segmentation masks are stored as 3D NIfTI (.nii.gz) files (imaging built on the CT-RATE volumes); the grounded annotations live under dataset/radgenome_files/ as CSV files split by task and split, e.g. train_region_report.csv / validation_region_report.csv for reports and train_vqa_{abnormality,location,presence,size}.csv (plus case_disorders CSVs) for VQA.
details.splits: Train and validation only (no separate test split): ~665K grounded reports (~625K train / ~40.3K validation rows) and ~1.3M grounded VQA pairs, built over 25,692 non-contrast chest CT volumes from 20,000 patients (CT-RATE).
details.extra: All grounded reports and VQA pairs in the validation set were manually verified for quality; the dataset extends CT-RATE and there is no dedicated test split released.

name: INSPECT
full_name: Integrating Numerous Sources for Prognostic Evaluation of Clinical Timelines
size: 23,248 CTPA studies / 19,402 patients with reports and EHR
paired_text_detail: radiology report impressions plus structured longitudinal EHR data
description: Multimodal dataset linking CTPA images, radiology reports and structured EHR at scale for pulmonary embolism diagnosis and prognosis.

label_schema: PE diagnosis (3 binary labels derived from radiology reports): PE positive, Acute PE, Subsegmental PE
12-month prognosis / classification outcomes: mortality, readmission, and pulmonary hypertension (PH)
Mortality and readmission also provided at 1-month and 6-month horizons (1/6/12-month each)
Label values are True / False / Censored (censored for time-to-event outcomes without complete follow-up)
No pixel-level segmentation or bounding-box annotations; labels are study-level classification/outcome targets
details.file_structure: CTPA scans distributed as anonymized DICOM; a master cohort CSV holds demographics and all study-level labels; radiology report impression sections in CSV; and linked structured EHR (OMOP/FEMR) as gzipped CSV files, all linked by patient/study identifiers.
details.splits: Train 18,945 studies (15,789 patients), Validation 1,089 studies (913 patients), Test 3,214 studies (2,700 patients); total ~23,248 CTPA studies from 19,402 patients.
details.extra: Multimodal dataset (CT + radiology reports + longitudinal structured EHR with 225M+ events); PE diagnosis labels are automatically extracted from reports rather than expert-annotated per-image, and prognosis labels use a Censored value for incomplete follow-up.

name: MAIDA
size: multi-institution chest X-rays across 22+ hospitals in 12 countries
paired_text_detail: DICOM metadata and annotations (endotracheal tube placement, pneumonia)
description: Global data-sharing initiative providing multi-institution chest X-rays for ICU/NICU/ED tasks such as endotracheal-tube and pneumonia assessment.

label_schema: Landmark/keypoint detection on chest X-rays: 2 target landmarks
Endotracheal tube (ETT) tip location (COCO category_id 1 = 'tip')
Carina location (COCO category_id 2 = 'carina')
Downstream clinical use: ETT-tip-to-carina distance to triage tube placement (normal vs. malpositioned)
Accompanying DICOM-derived metadata: reason for the chest X-ray, available radiology reports, and demographics (age, race, sex)
details.file_structure: Chest X-ray images (original DICOM; preprocessed to 640x640 PNG for the models) with annotations stored as COCO-format JSON (images[], annotations[] with image_id/category_id/bbox, categories[]); model demo outputs predicted ETT-tip and carina (x,y) coordinates as a predictions.csv.
details.extra: Multi-center crowdsourced platform: the neonatal (NICU) continual-learning study spans 30 hospitals across 20 countries/territories; annotations are supplied per hospital as separate train/test COCO JSON files rather than one fixed global split. Also covers ICU/NICU/ED settings and other tube types in related work.

name: TCIA
description: NCI-funded public archive of de-identified cancer medical images (mostly DICOM) organized into collections by disease, modality or research focus.

name: PhysioNet
paired_text_detail: many datasets pair images with reports/clinical data (e.g. MIMIC-CXR)
description: MIT-hosted repository of physiological and clinical research data, including major credentialed radiology datasets such as MIMIC-CXR and its derivatives.

name: OpenNeuro
multi_sequence_detail: BIDS-organized MRI/fMRI/EEG/MEG/PET incl. multi-sequence MRI
description: Free BIDS-compliant repository for openly sharing neuroimaging datasets (MRI, fMRI, EEG, MEG, PET), operated by the Stanford Center for Reproducible Neuroscience.

name: Grand Challenge
segmentation_detail: many hosted challenges provide expert segmentation annotations
description: Open platform for hosting biomedical imaging challenges, expert annotation collection and deployment of deep-learning algorithms.

name: Synapse
description: Sage Bionetworks collaborative research platform hosting biomedical datasets, challenges (incl. BraTS) and imaging benchmarks with governance controls.

name: Zenodo
description: CERN-operated general-purpose open research repository with DOI minting, widely used to publish radiology datasets such as ROCOv2 and TotalSegmentator.

name: Kaggle Datasets
size: hundreds of thousands of public datasets
description: Community data-science platform hosting many public medical-imaging datasets and competitions (e.g. RSNA, chest X-ray challenges).

name: Stanford AIMI
paired_text_detail: several datasets include reports/annotations (e.g. CheXpert, INSPECT)
segmentation_detail: some datasets include expert segmentation/annotation
description: Stanford Center for AI in Medicine and Imaging portal of de-identified, annotated clinical imaging datasets across modalities for non-commercial research.

name: IDC
segmentation_detail: includes image-derived annotations, segmentations and measurements
description: Cloud-based NCI node collocating public cancer imaging collections with Google Cloud analysis tools; no registration required to browse and download.

name: UK Biobank Imaging
multi_sequence_detail: brain multi-sequence MRI (T1, FLAIR, SWI, fMRI, dMRI), cardiac/abdominal MRI, DXA, carotid US
description: World's largest multi-modal imaging study, scanning 100,000 volunteers with brain/cardiac/abdominal MRI, DXA and carotid ultrasound linked to genetic and phenotypic data.

name: Medical Segmentation Decathlon
multi_sequence_detail: includes multi-parametric MRI tasks (brain, prostate) and CT tasks
segmentation_detail: expert voxel-level labels for all 10 tasks
description: Benchmark suite of ten 3D CT/MRI segmentation tasks with expert labels spanning diverse organs and lesions (individual tasks also listed separately).

name: AWS Open Data
full_name: Registry of Open Data on AWS
description: Registry of openly available datasets hosted on AWS S3, including medical imaging resources such as the Medical Segmentation Decathlon and EMBED.

name: Hugging Face
paired_text_detail: hosts many image-text/report/VQA datasets (CT-RATE, RadGenome, VQA-RAD, PMC-OA)
description: Machine-learning dataset hub hosting a large and growing collection of medical imaging and multimodal radiology datasets, some gated behind usage agreements.

name: Radiopaedia
paired_text_detail: case descriptions, findings and teaching text per case
multi_sequence_detail: cases include multi-sequence MRI and multi-modality studies
description: Collaborative open-edit radiology reference and teaching archive with tens of thousands of annotated cases spanning all modalities and body regions.

name: CT-RATE
full_name: Chest CT with Radiology Text and Extended labels
size: 25,692 CT volumes (50,188 with reconstructions) from 21,304 patients; ~21.3 TB
paired_text_detail: Free-text radiology reports for every volume, plus 18-class multi-abnormality labels and metadata
multi_sequence_detail: Non-contrast chest CT; multiple reconstruction matrices but single phase
description: A large-scale 3D chest CT dataset of 25,692 non-contrast volumes from 21,304 patients paired with free-text radiology reports and 18-class multi-abnormality labels, used to train the CT-CLIP foundation model.

label_schema: Multi-label classification: 18 binary abnormality labels per CT volume, extracted from radiology reports
1 - Medical material
2 - Arterial wall calcification
3 - Cardiomegaly
4 - Pericardial effusion
5 - Coronary artery wall calcification
6 - Hiatal hernia
7 - Lymphadenopathy
8 - Emphysema
9 - Atelectasis
10 - Lung nodule
11 - Lung opacity
12 - Pulmonary fibrotic sequela
13 - Pleural effusion
14 - Mosaic attenuation pattern
15 - Peribronchial thickening
16 - Consolidation
17 - Bronchiectasis
18 - Interlobular septal thickening
Also includes free-text radiology reports (sections: Clinical Information, Technique, Findings, Impression)
details.file_structure: CT volumes are stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz) files organized in folders named split_patientID_scanID_reconstructionID (e.g. valid_53_a_1); the 18-class multi-abnormality labels, radiology text reports, and scan metadata are each provided as separate CSV files (e.g. train/valid_predicted_labels.csv) with one row per volume.
details.splits: Train: 20,000 patients; Validation: 1,304 patients (25,692 CT volumes total, expanded to 50,188 via multiple reconstructions, from 21,304 unique patients)
details.extra: Labels are not manually annotated per volume; the 18 abnormality labels were automatically extracted from the corresponding radiology reports using a fine-tuned RadBERT text classifier (files named *_predicted_labels.csv).

name: MR-RATE
full_name: MRI with Radiology Text and Extended labels
size: 705,254 MRI volumes from 98,334 studies of 83,425 patients (~8-38 TB across repositories)
paired_text_detail: Anonymized, LLM-structured radiology reports (clinical information, technique, findings, impression) per study, plus pathology labels across 37 brain/spine categories grounded in SNOMED CT/RadLex
multi_sequence_detail: T1-weighted, T2-weighted, FLAIR, SWI, and MRA sequences; non-contrast and contrast-enhanced
segmentation_detail: Brain masks and defacing masks accompany volumes; multi-label anatomical (brain/body) segmentations, co-registered within-study volumes, and MNI152 atlas-registered volumes are also provided
description: A large-scale brain and spine MRI dataset of over 705,000 multi-sequence volumes from ~98,000 studies paired with structured radiology reports and segmentation masks.

label_schema: Structured radiology reports split into 4 sections: clinical_information, technique, findings, impression (plus full report text)

37 brain and spine MRI pathology categories (multi-label classification), each grounded to SNOMED CT / RadLex; classified from the report findings section (exact category names not enumerated on the public card - given in the pathology-labels CSV header)

Anatomical multi-label voxel-wise segmentations from the NV-Segment-CTMR model: a brain segmentation (center-modality volumes) and a whole-body segmentation (all modalities), following the NV-Segment-CTMR label_dict.json (integer class values not listed on the public card)
Per-series brain masks (binary) and defacing masks (binary)
details.file_structure: Per-study folders (<study_uid>/) with NIfTI volumes under img/ (<study_uid>_<series_id>.nii.gz) and masks/segmentations under seg/ (e.g. _brain-mask.nii.gz, _defacing-mask.nii.gz, _nvseg-ctmr-brain.nii.gz, _nvseg-ctmr-wb.nii.gz); reports, metadata, and pathology labels are stored as CSV; co-registration and atlas-registration derivatives add coreg_img/atlas_img NIfTIs plus .mat transform matrices, distributed across separate HuggingFace repos.
details.splits: Patient-level splits: Train 75,000 patients / 88,985 studies / 638,345 series; Validation 3,425 / 3,781 / 27,003; Test 5,000 / 5,568 / 39,906 (total 83,425 patients / 98,334 studies / 705,254 series).
details.extra: Companion derivative repos provide within-study co-registered volumes (MR-RATE-coreg) and MNI152 atlas-registered volumes (MR-RATE-atlas) with .mat transforms, plus NV-Segment-CTMR segmentations (MR-RATE-nvseg-ctmr); reports were anonymized and LLM-structured/translated to English.

name: Merlin
full_name: Merlin: A Computed Tomography Vision-Language Foundation Model and Dataset
size: 25,494 abdominal/pelvic CT scans from 18,317 patients (>6M images)
paired_text_detail: Paired free-text radiology reports plus structured EHR data (>1.8M ICD diagnosis codes)
license: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement
description: A native-3D abdominal/pelvic CT dataset of 25,494 scans from 18,317 patients paired with radiology reports and ICD/EHR diagnosis codes, released to train the Merlin CT vision-language foundation model.

label_schema: Free-text radiology reports paired with each abdominal CT (findings + impression sections; ~10.05M tokens in the findings sections used for training)
EHR-derived diagnosis codes per patient: 2,995,293 assigned codes / 16,553 unique, comprising 954,013 ICD-9 codes (5,686 unique) and 2,041,280 ICD-10 codes (10,867 unique)

Phenotype labels: 1,692 hierarchical phenotypes obtained by mapping the ICD-9/ICD-10 codes through the PheWAS Phecode mapping (692 phenotypes with adequate prevalence used for classification evaluation)

30 radiologist-selected abdominal findings for zero-shot classification, e.g. ascites, pleural effusion, splenomegaly, appendicitis, lymphadenopathy, free air, metastatic disease, abdominal aortic aneurysm, prostatomegaly, anasarca

20-organ 3D segmentation label set (evaluated via TotalSegmentator, not a core Merlin release): stomach, liver, gallbladder, left kidney, right kidney, spleen, prostate, T12/L1/L2/L3/L4/L5/S1 vertebrae, sacrum, urinary bladder, colon, duodenum, small bowel, pancreas
details.file_structure: Each abdominal CT study is paired with its free-text radiology report and with tabular EHR labels (ICD-9/ICD-10 diagnosis codes and derived Phecode phenotypes) for the patient; the paper does not specify the exact on-disk image/label file formats (e.g. NIfTI/DICOM/CSV) for the public release.
details.splits: Main cohort of ~25,528 CTs split 60/20/20 by patient: 15,331 train / 5,060 validation / 5,137 test CTs, plus a separate ~7,000-CT external validation set from another institution.
details.extra: Phenotype labels are not independent annotations but are algorithmically derived from the ICD codes via the standard PheWAS Phecode mapping; the 20-organ segmentation results rely on the external TotalSegmentator dataset rather than released Merlin masks (consistent with segmentation = n/a).

name: ReXGradient-160K
full_name: ReXGradient-160K: A Large-Scale Publicly Available Dataset of Chest Radiographs with Free-text Reports
size: 273,004 images from 160,000 studies of 109,487 patients across 79 sites (3 U.S. health systems)
paired_text_detail: Structured free-text radiology reports per study with four sections: indication, comparison, findings, and impression
license: Custom non-commercial research data use agreement (accepted on Hugging Face)
description: A large multi-site chest X-ray dataset of 273,004 PNG images from 160,000 studies across 79 U.S. sites, each paired with structured free-text radiology reports.

label_schema: Free-text radiology report per study, structured into 4 sections: Indication, Comparison, Findings, Impression
Primary task is chest X-ray report generation (image-to-text); no fixed finding-label taxonomy is provided
Companion bounding-box annotations: radiologist-drawn boxes marking interstitial patterns for ~400 examples (Label Studio coordinate format)
details.file_structure: Chest X-ray images are provided as downsampled PNGs packaged in split tar part-files (e.g. concatenate deid_png.part* into deid_png.tar, then extract); reports/metadata and the interstitial-pattern bounding boxes are provided as separate metadata files (metadata/interstitial_pattern_bbox.json).
details.splits: Train: 140,000 studies (238,968 images, 95,716 patients); Validation: 10,000 studies (17,007 images, 6,964 patients); Public Test: 10,000 studies (17,029 images, 6,807 patients); Private Test: 10,000 studies (held out for ReXrank benchmark). Total 160,000 studies / 273,004 images.
details.extra: The ~400 interstitial-pattern bounding boxes are a small companion annotation set; the vast majority of the dataset has only paired free-text reports, not structured finding labels or per-study multi-reader annotations.

name: PadChest-GR
full_name: PadChest-GR: A Bilingual Chest X-ray Dataset for Grounded Radiology Report Generation
size: 4,555 CXR studies (3,099 abnormal, 1,456 normal); 7,037 positive and 3,422 negative finding sentences; ~46 GB
paired_text_detail: Sentence-level grounded reports in English and Spanish describing present (positive) and absent (negative) findings
other_labels: bounding boxes per positive finding (up to two independent reader sets)
license: PADCHEST Dataset Research Use Agreement (free for research)
description: A manually curated bilingual (English/Spanish) chest X-ray dataset of 4,555 studies with sentence-level grounded radiology reports and radiologist-annotated bounding boxes for grounded report generation.

label_schema: Grounded radiology reports: each study's report is decomposed into individual sentences, each describing at most one finding, provided bilingually in English and Spanish (orig/translation)
7,037 positive-finding sentences and 3,422 negative-finding sentences across the dataset

Per positive finding: a 'finding category' label (e.g. aortic atheromatosis, apical pleural thickening, laminar atelectasis, aortic elongation, cardiomegaly, nodule, pleural effusion, scoliosis) drawn from PadChest's taxonomy; Fig.4 shows the 24 most common finding categories plus 'Other' and 'negative'
Per finding: anatomical 'locations' labels from a hierarchical taxonomy (e.g. aortic, pleural, basal, left, right, lung field / upper / middle / lower lung field, column)
Per finding: a 'progression' label classifying temporal change vs a prior study - one of new, worsening, stable, improving, resolved, or N/A/None

Bounding boxes localizing each positive finding on the image; a finding may have multiple boxes (e.g. bilateral), and each positive finding has up to two independent sets of boxes drawn by two different radiologist readers
Normal studies contribute negative findings only (1,819 negative findings from 1,456 normal studies)
details.file_structure: Chest X-ray images are single-view (mostly PA) PNG files inherited from PadChest, paired with grounded-report annotations (per-sentence finding category, locations, progression, EN/ES text, and bounding-box coordinates); distributed as a gated dataset on Hugging Face (microsoft/padchest-gr) and via the BIMCV request form (~46 GB).
details.splits: 3,185 training studies / 455 validation studies / 915 test studies (total 4,555), split ~70/10/20 at random, stratified by finding category and keeping all studies of a patient within one partition
details.extra: Multi-reader: every positive finding sentence carries up to two independent bounding-box annotation sets from different radiologists (a 14-radiologist team reviewed/annotated); note ~6,217 positive findings have no manual box while 820 do (per Fig.4C), so not all findings are box-grounded, and images are reduced-grayscale PNG rather than DICOM.

name: MedTrinity-25M
full_name: MedTrinity-25M: A Large-scale Multimodal Dataset with Multigranular Annotations for Medicine
anatomy: Whole body (multiple regions, e.g. chest, brain, abdomen)
size: ~25M images (18M accessible image-text pairs) across 10 modalities
paired_text_detail: Multigranular text annotations: disease/lesion type, modality, region-specific descriptions, and inter-region relationships
segmentation_detail: Automatically generated region-of-interest segmentation masks and bounding boxes included in the multigranular annotations
license: Mixed (CC BY 4.0 for some sources; some constituent datasets proprietary/credentialed, e.g. CheXpert, MIMIC-CXR-JPG)
description: A large-scale multimodal medical dataset of ~25M images across 10 modalities (including CT, MRI, and X-ray) with automatically generated multigranular text annotations, bounding boxes, and segmentation masks.

label_schema: Each sample is an image paired with a single multigranular free-text 'caption' (no separate class-index label columns)
Caption encodes GLOBAL attributes: disease/lesion type, imaging modality, and region/organ identity
Caption encodes LOCAL attributes: region-of-interest (ROI) descriptions plus inter-regional spatial relationships
ROI bounding boxes and segmentation masks are auto-generated (via SAT/BA-Transformer-style tools) and expressed textually within the caption rather than as pixel-mask files
Coverage: 10 imaging modalities and 65+ diseases/conditions across whole-body regions (e.g., chest, brain, abdomen)
Per-sample metadata fields: file_name, id (UUID), caption, source (originating dataset)
details.file_structure: Images plus a metadata.jsonl file are grouped into numbered shards (to avoid filesystem limits); each JSONL line holds file_name, id, caption, and source, with the annotations living inside the caption text (no per-image NIfTI/DICOM-SEG mask files).
details.extra: No official train/val/test split; three released versions exist — a ~100k demo, the full 25M (text/metadata), and an ~18M 'accessible' subset with paired images. Annotations are automatically generated by an MLLM pipeline, not human-verified.

name: CheXpert Plus
full_name: CheXpert Plus: Augmenting a Large Chest X-ray Dataset with Text Radiology Reports, Patient Demographics and Additional Image Formats
size: 223,228 chest radiographs paired with 187,711 radiology reports from 64,725 patients
paired_text_detail: De-identified free-text radiology reports (36M text tokens, ~1M PHI spans anonymized) paired with images, plus CheXbert structured labels and RadGraph-XL annotations
license: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement
description: An augmentation of the CheXpert dataset pairing 223,228 chest radiographs (in DICOM and PNG) with 187,711 de-identified free-text radiology reports, patient demographics, and structured labels for 64,725 patients.

label_schema: 14 CheXbert pathology labels per report/image (multi-label): No Finding, Enlarged Cardiomediastinum, Cardiomegaly, Lung Opacity, Lung Lesion, Edema, Consolidation, Pneumonia, Atelectasis, Pneumothorax, Pleural Effusion, Pleural Other, Fracture, Support Devices
Label values follow CheXpert/CheXbert convention: 1 = positive, 0 = negative, -1 = uncertain, blank/NaN = not mentioned

Full free-text radiology reports parsed into up to 11 subsections: Narrative, Clinical History, History, Comparison, Technique, Findings, Impression, End of Impression, Summary, Accession Number (and related header/exam fields)
RadGraph (RadGraph-XL) structured entity/relation annotations extracted separately from the Findings and Impression sections
Patient demographics and clinical/socio-economic metadata plus DICOM image metadata attributes
details.file_structure: A master CSV (df_chexpert_plus_240401.csv) maps each row to an image path (e.g. train/patientXXXXX/studyY/viewZ_frontal.jpg) and the parsed report section text plus demographics; images are provided in both DICOM and PNG format; CheXbert labels are stored as JSON/JSONL (e.g. chexbert_labels/findings_fixed.json) and RadGraph annotations as JSON (e.g. radgraph-XL-annotations/section_findings.json).
details.extra: Contains 223,228 unique image-report pairs from 187,711 studies and 64,725 patients (~36M text tokens, ~1M PHI spans anonymized); pathology labels are machine-generated by CheXbert, and a held-out 1000-sample test set is annotated by 2 board-certified radiologists for evaluation.

name: DRR-RATE
full_name: DRR-RATE: Digitally Reconstructed Radiographs from CT-RATE
paired_text_detail: Radiology reports inherited from CT-RATE, plus 18-class binary pathology labels
description: A dataset of 50,188 digitally reconstructed chest radiographs generated from CT-RATE CT volumes via Siddon-Jacobs ray tracing (frontal and lateral views), paired with inherited radiology reports and 18-class binary pathology labels.

label_schema: Multi-label classification: 18 binary abnormality labels per DRR image, inherited from CT-RATE
1 - Medical material
2 - Arterial wall calcification
3 - Cardiomegaly
4 - Pericardial effusion
5 - Coronary artery wall calcification
6 - Hiatal hernia
7 - Lymphadenopathy
8 - Emphysema
9 - Atelectasis
10 - Lung nodule
11 - Lung opacity
12 - Pulmonary fibrotic sequela
13 - Pleural effusion
14 - Mosaic attenuation pattern
15 - Peribronchial thickening
16 - Consolidation
17 - Bronchiectasis
18 - Interlobular septal thickening
Companion label: paired free-text radiology report (impression/findings) per study
details.file_structure: DRR images are 2D PNGs (frontal/lateral projections rendered from NIfTI CT volumes via Siddon-Jacobs ray tracing) organized under train/ and valid/ folders following the CT-RATE patient/study hierarchy; the 18 binary labels and text reports are provided as CSV files (train_predicted_labels.csv, valid_predicted_labels.csv, train_reports.csv, validation_reports.csv) plus metadata CSVs, downloaded from the gated CT-RATE HuggingFace repo via the included download_metadata_label.py script per license.
details.splits: Split into train/ and valid/ folders following CT-RATE; ~50,188 DRR volumes generated from 25,692 chest CT volumes (exact per-split image counts not confirmed).
details.extra: Labels are machine-generated ('predicted') multi-abnormality labels inherited verbatim from CT-RATE (not re-annotated for the DRR projections); labels and reports are not stored in the DRR-RATE repo and must be pulled from the gated CT-RATE dataset to comply with the CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 license.

name: BIMCV
full_name: Medical Imaging Databank of the Valencia Region (BIMCV: PadChest, BIMCV-COVID19+)
size: PadChest ~160,000 CXR images from ~67,000 patients; BIMCV-COVID19+ tens of thousands of CXR/CT studies
paired_text_detail: PadChest includes Spanish radiology reports and UMLS-coded labels; BIMCV-COVID19+ includes radiographic findings, RT-PCR/antibody results and DICOM metadata
description: A regional medical imaging repository hosting large open chest imaging datasets including PadChest (chest X-rays with Spanish reports/labels) and BIMCV-COVID19+ (chest X-ray and CT studies with reports and clinical results).

name: AbdomenAtlas 1.1
anatomy: abdominal organs, thorax organs, vasculature, skeletal structures
multi_sequence_detail: CT volumes span multiple contrast phases (pre-contrast, arterial, portal-venous, delayed).
segmentation_detail: Per-voxel annotations for 25 anatomical structures (organs, vascular and skeletal structures) plus pseudo-labels for 7 tumor types.
host: GitHub / Hugging Face (MrGiovanni/AbdomenAtlas, AbdomenAtlas HF org)
description: An expanded release of AbdomenAtlas with 9,262 multi-phase CT volumes annotated per-voxel for 25 anatomical structures plus pseudo-labels for seven tumor types.

label_schema: Voxel-wise multi-organ segmentation with 25 classes (class map, integer index - structure):
Label 1 - aorta
Label 2 - gall_bladder
Label 3 - kidney_left
Label 4 - kidney_right
Label 5 - liver
Label 6 - pancreas
Label 7 - postcava (inferior vena cava)
Label 8 - spleen
Label 9 - stomach
Label 10 - adrenal_gland_left
Label 11 - adrenal_gland_right
Label 12 - bladder
Label 13 - celiac_trunk
Label 14 - colon
Label 15 - duodenum
Label 16 - esophagus
Label 17 - femur_left
Label 18 - femur_right
Label 19 - hepatic_vessel
Label 20 - intestine
Label 21 - lung_left
Label 22 - lung_right
Label 23 - portal_vein_and_splenic_vein
Label 24 - prostate
Label 25 - rectum
Separate tumor pseudo-labels stored in pseudo_label.nii.gz (README states 'six types of tumors'; exact tumor class names not enumerated on the official page, so not listed here)
details.file_structure: Per-case folders (e.g. BDMAP_00000001/) each containing ct.nii.gz plus a combined label mask and a segmentations/ subfolder of one NIfTI file per structure (spleen.nii.gz, liver.nii.gz, ...), all in compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz) format.
details.extra: Organ masks are real annotations but tumor labels are AI-generated pseudo-labels (README notes only ~8 organs are 'pretty accurate'); the README says 'six types of tumors' rather than seven, and the tumor class names are not enumerated on the official GitHub page.

name: AbdomenAtlas 3.0
anatomy: abdominal organs, tumors (liver, kidney, pancreas), pancreas sub-segments, peripancreatic vessels
size: 9,262 CT volumes (3,220 with a contrast-enhanced phase) + reports
paired_text_detail: First public abdominal CT dataset with paired radiology reports (>9,000 CT scans with structured/'superhuman' reports generated by RadGPT).
multi_sequence_detail: Includes contrast-enhanced phases; 3,220 scans have at least one contrast-enhanced phase.
