segmentation_detail: Per-voxel segmentations of liver, kidney and pancreatic tumors, nine core organs, plus pancreas sub-segments and peripancreatic blood vessels.
description: An ICCV 2025 abdominal CT dataset pairing over 9,000 CT volumes with radiology reports and per-voxel tumor, organ, pancreas sub-segment and vessel annotations in NIfTI format.

label_schema: Per-voxel segmentation masks (NIfTI) plus paired free-text radiology reports for each CT
9 core organ classes: liver, kidney, pancreas, spleen, adrenal glands, stomach, duodenum, aorta, postcava (inferior vena cava)
3 pancreas sub-segments: pancreas head, pancreas body, pancreas tail
5 peripancreatic vessels: superior mesenteric artery (SMA), superior mesenteric vein (SMV), celiac axis (CA), common hepatic artery (CHA), portal vein
3 tumor classes: liver tumor, kidney tumor, pancreatic tumor
Reports come in 3 styles: Structured (template with findings/measurements/staging), Narrative (prose), and Human-AI Fusion / Enhanced Human reports (240 reports)

Reports encode tumor measurements, organ volumes, Hounsfield-unit values, and pancreatic cancer T-stage (T1-T4) for 260 reports; note: exact integer index per mask class not documented on the public page, so indices are omitted
details.file_structure: Downloaded as NIfTI (.nii.gz) with two parallel directory trees, data/image_only (CT volumes) and data/mask_only (per-voxel masks), plus data/AbdomenAtlas3.0MiniWithMeta.csv metadata and a data/TrainTestIDS folder of split CSVs.
details.splits: No fixed train/val/test; two official split schemes provided as CSVs (IID_train.csv/IID_test.csv = random 90%/10%; OOD_train.csv/OOD_test.csv = cross-hospital, ~50% train). Total 9,262 CT-mask-report triplets.
details.extra: Of 9,262 scans, ~2,947 reports document tumors (929 liver / 1,674 kidney / 344 pancreatic) and ~6,061 report no tumor; aggregated from 17 public datasets across 88 institutions in 19 countries, so annotation provenance is mixed.

name: TotalSegmentator-MRI
anatomy: whole-body anatomy (organs, bones, muscles, vessels, tissues)
multi_sequence_detail: Sequence-independent dataset spanning diverse clinical MRI sequences, scanners and institutions; some images sourced from the Imaging Data Commons.
segmentation_detail: Voxel segmentations of 50 anatomical structures across organs, bones, muscles, vessels and tissues.
description: An open real-world clinical MRI dataset of 616 scans annotated with voxel segmentations of 50 anatomical structures, released with the sequence-independent TotalSegmentator MRI model.

label_schema: Voxel-level multi-class segmentation: 50 anatomical structures, one integer index per class (values 1-50; 0 = background)
Label 1 - spleen
Label 2 - kidney_right
Label 3 - kidney_left
Label 4 - gallbladder
Label 5 - liver
Label 6 - stomach
Label 7 - pancreas
Label 8 - adrenal_gland_right
Label 9 - adrenal_gland_left
Label 10 - lung_left
Label 11 - lung_right
Label 12 - esophagus
Label 13 - small_bowel
Label 14 - duodenum
Label 15 - colon
Label 16 - urinary_bladder
Label 17 - prostate
Label 18 - sacrum
Label 19 - vertebrae
Label 20 - intervertebral_discs
Label 21 - spinal_cord
Label 22 - heart
Label 23 - aorta
Label 24 - inferior_vena_cava
Label 25 - portal_vein_and_splenic_vein
Label 26 - iliac_artery_left
Label 27 - iliac_artery_right
Label 28 - iliac_vena_left
Label 29 - iliac_vena_right
Label 30 - humerus_left
Label 31 - humerus_right
Label 32 - scapula_left
Label 33 - scapula_right
Label 34 - clavicula_left
Label 35 - clavicula_right
Label 36 - femur_left
Label 37 - femur_right
Label 38 - hip_left
Label 39 - hip_right
Label 40 - gluteus_maximus_left
Label 41 - gluteus_maximus_right
Label 42 - gluteus_medius_left
Label 43 - gluteus_medius_right
Label 44 - gluteus_minimus_left
Label 45 - gluteus_minimus_right
Label 46 - autochthon_left
Label 47 - autochthon_right
Label 48 - iliopsoas_left
Label 49 - iliopsoas_right
Label 50 - brain
details.file_structure: One folder per subject; each contains the MRI volume and a 'segmentations/' subfolder holding the label masks, all as NIfTI (.nii.gz) - either individual binary masks per structure or a single multi-label file, with class names/indices per the total_mr class map.
details.extra: Version 2.0.0 contains 616 MRI examinations (expanded from 298 in the original release). Class names/indices verified against the official TotalSegmentator 'total_mr' class_map (map_to_binary.py); the accompanying model and paper (arXiv:2405.19492) also cover CT, but this Zenodo record is the MRI subset.

name: ULS23
anatomy: thorax-abdomen (universal lesions: bone, pancreas, lymph node, liver, kidney, lung, etc.)
size: ~38,000 lesion VOIs across multi-part release; e.g. Part 1 = 1,618 lesions, 15.9 GB
segmentation_detail: 3D voxel-level segmentation masks of lesions across many organ types; novel fully-annotated Radboudumc/Jeroen Bosch data plus curated public sources (DeepLesion, KiTS21, LIDC-IDRI, LiTS, MSD, NIH-LN, CCC18)
description: A CT benchmark for universal 3D lesion segmentation aggregating novel fully-annotated data and curated public annotated lesion datasets across the thorax and abdomen, released as NIfTI lesion VOIs with 3D segmentation masks.

label_schema: Binary voxel-level segmentation mask per VOI (single foreground class)
Label 1 - lesion (universal; covers lung, mediastinal, abdominal, bone, pancreas, kidney, liver lesions)
Label 0 - background
No per-organ / per-lesion-type class index: all lesion types share value 1 (a 'universal' lesion segmentation target)
details.file_structure: 3D CT volumes-of-interest and their masks are stored as NIfTI files (one label file per lesion VOI, cropped/centered on the lesion with preserved voxel spacing); the GitHub repo (DIAGNijmegen/ULS23) is organized into annotations/ULS23/ (training labels), baseline_model/ (nnU-Netv2), data_processing/ (source-dataset prep), evaluation/, and GC_algorithm/, with the imaging data distributed via Zenodo releases.
details.splits: Novel ULS23_DeepLesion3D contribution = 750 fully-annotated lesions (200 abdominal, 100 bone, 50 kidney, 50 liver, 100 lung, 100 mediastinal, 150 assorted); overall train/val/test counts not confirmed from official pages (test set is a held-out multi-centre 'type II challenge' set on Grand Challenge)
details.extra: Mix of fully-annotated data (novel Radboudumc/Jeroen Bosch and DeepLesion3D lesions each segmented in triplicate with majority-vote consensus, plus hard-negative mining) and weakly/semi-supervised labels generated with GrabCut; training data curated from 7 public sources (DeepLesion, KiTS21, LIDC-IDRI, LiTS, MSD, NIH-LN, CCC18) alongside the new Radboudumc Bone and Pancreas sets.

name: ATLAS
full_name: A Tumour and Liver Automatic Segmentation (ATLAS) MICCAI 2023 Challenge
size: 90 T1 contrast-enhanced MRI scans (60 train / 30 test) from 90 unresectable HCC patients
multi_sequence_detail: T1 contrast-enhanced MRI with variation in image resolution, contrast phase, sequence and MRI manufacturer.
segmentation_detail: Binary segmentation masks for two structures: liver (parenchyma) and tumor(s).
description: A MICCAI 2023 challenge dataset of 90 T1 contrast-enhanced liver MRI scans from unresectable HCC patients with liver and tumor segmentation labels.

label_schema: Voxel-wise semantic segmentation mask, 3 classes
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - liver (parenchyma)
Label 2 - liver tumor (hepatocellular carcinoma)
details.file_structure: 3D volumetric NIfTI (.nii.gz): one contrast-enhanced T1 MRI volume per patient plus one matching segmentation label volume encoding liver and tumor, split into train and test folders, with per-patient acquisition metadata in patient_info_train.json / patient_info_test.json.
details.splits: 60 training patients / 30 test patients (90 total); test-set labels withheld during the challenge.
details.extra: Modality is contrast-enhanced T1 MRI (CE-MRI), not CT; masks were manually segmented and reviewed by radiologists, and dataset spans varied resolutions, contrast phases, sequences, and scanner manufacturers.

name: SegRap2023
full_name: Segmentation of Organs-at-Risk and Gross Tumor Volume of NPC (SegRap 2023)
anatomy: head and neck organs-at-risk and nasopharyngeal carcinoma tumor volumes
size: 400 CT scans (200 patients, paired contrast-enhanced + non-contrast); 120 train, 20 validation, 60 test
multi_sequence_detail: Paired non-contrast and contrast-enhanced CT per patient.
segmentation_detail: Voxel annotations of 45 organs-at-risk plus 2 gross tumor volumes (GTVnx primary, GTVnd nodal).
description: A MICCAI 2023 head-and-neck CT dataset of 200 nasopharyngeal carcinoma patients with paired contrast/non-contrast scans, annotated for 45 organs-at-risk and two gross tumor volumes.

label_schema: Task 1 (OAR segmentation): 45 head-and-neck organs-at-risk, voxel masks

OARs: Brain, Brainstem, Chiasm, Cochlea left, Cochlea right, Esophagus, Eustachian tube left, Eustachian tube right, Eye left, Eye right, Hippocampus left, Hippocampus right, Internal auditory canal left, Internal auditory canal right, Larynx, Larynx glottic, Larynx supraglottic, Lens left, Lens right, Mandible left, Mandible right, Mastoid left, Mastoid right, Middle ear left, Middle ear right, Optic nerve left, Optic nerve right, Oral cavity, Parotid left, Parotid right, Pharynx, Pituitary, Spinal cord, Submandibular left, Submandibular right, Temporal lobe left, Temporal lobe right, Thyroid, Temporomandibular joint left, Temporomandibular joint right, Trachea, Tympanic cavity left, Tympanic cavity right, Vestibular semicircular canal left, Vestibular semicircular canal right
Task 2 (GTV segmentation): 2 gross tumor volume classes
GTVnx - primary gross target volume of nasopharynx (nasopharyngeal carcinoma tumor)
GTVnd - gross target volume of involved lymph node(s)
details.file_structure: Each of the 200 patients has two co-registered CT volumes (a non-contrast and a contrast-enhanced scan) with corresponding voxel-level annotations; challenge submissions require 4D .mha masks (shape [45, *image_shape] for OARs, [2, *image_shape] for GTVs), with inputs organized under image folders (e.g. head-neck-ct and head-neck-contrast-enhanced-ct).
details.splits: 120 training / 20 validation / 60 testing patients (200 total)
details.extra: Two separate tasks: Task 1 = 45 OARs, Task 2 = 2 nasopharyngeal carcinoma GTVs; training and validation data were publicly released after MICCAI 2023 via Google Drive and Baidu Cloud.

name: PANORAMA
full_name: Pancreatic Cancer Diagnosis - Radiologists Meet AI (PANORAMA)
size: ~2,238 CECT training scans (two centers) plus 194 MSD and 80 NIH external cases; ~197 GB
segmentation_detail: Segmentation labels provided in companion repo DIAGNijmegen/panorama_labels: PDAC lesion (manual), plus pancreas parenchyma, veins, arteries, pancreatic duct, and common bile duct (automatic). Manual lesion segmentations for PDAC cases.
description: A large multi-center contrast-enhanced abdominal CT dataset of ~2,200+ scans enriched for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with pancreas and tumor segmentation annotations.

label_schema: Multi-class voxel segmentation, integer-valued masks
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - PDAC lesion (manual for annotated PDAC cases; automatic otherwise)
Label 2 - veins
Label 3 - arteries
Label 4 - pancreas parenchyma
Label 5 - pancreatic duct
Label 6 - common bile duct
Non-lesion structures (labels 2-6) generated automatically via the Alves et al. 2021 algorithm
details.file_structure: Segmentation masks are single-file multi-label NIfTI volumes (.nii.gz) named by case (e.g. 100002_00001.nii.gz), stored in the companion GitHub repo DIAGNijmegen/panorama_labels under manual_labels/ and automatic_labels/, with a clinical_information.xlsx reference-standard spreadsheet; contrast-enhanced CT images are distributed separately on the Grand Challenge / Zenodo data pages.
details.splits: Public training/development set of 2,238 CECT studies; hidden test cohort of ~400 unseen scans used for challenge evaluation (no official validation split published).
details.extra: PDAC lesion labels come in two flavors: 482 manually segmented cases (ITK-SNAP v3.80, by two trained investigators supervised by an expert radiologist) and the remainder automatically generated; includes non-PDAC/healthy-pancreas cases plus cases derived from the NIH pancreas set and Medical Segmentation Decathlon.

name: TopCoW 2024
full_name: Topology-Aware Anatomical Segmentation of the Circle of Willis
size: 125 training patients (250 paired CTA+MRA images), plus 10 validation and 70 test pairs
multi_sequence_detail: Paired CTA and MRA of the same patients (two modalities, not multi-sequence)
segmentation_detail: Multi-class voxel annotations of up to 13 Circle of Willis vessel components
license: Open use with attribution; commercial use requires data-owner permission
description: The first public dataset with paired CTA and MRA from the same patients and voxel-level multi-class annotations of the Circle of Willis vessels, released via the TopCoW 2024 MICCAI challenge.

label_schema: Multi-class voxel segmentation of Circle of Willis vessels (Task 1)
Label 0 - Background
Label 1 - BA (Basilar Artery)
Label 2 - R-PCA (Right Posterior Cerebral Artery)
Label 3 - L-PCA (Left Posterior Cerebral Artery)
Label 4 - R-ICA (Right Internal Carotid Artery)
Label 5 - R-MCA (Right Middle Cerebral Artery)
Label 6 - L-ICA (Left Internal Carotid Artery)
Label 7 - L-MCA (Left Middle Cerebral Artery)
Label 8 - R-Pcom (Right Posterior Communicating Artery)
Label 9 - L-Pcom (Left Posterior Communicating Artery)
Label 10 - Acom (Anterior Communicating Artery)
Label 11 - R-ACA (Right Anterior Cerebral Artery)
Label 12 - L-ACA (Left Anterior Cerebral Artery)
Label 15 - 3rd-A2 (anatomical variant)
13 labeled vessel components total (labels 1-12 plus 15)
Companion Task 2 labels: 3D bounding box of the CoW region of interest (detection)
Companion Task 3 labels: anterior/posterior CoW edge-list annotations for graph classification
details.file_structure: Angiographic scans and masks are stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz, 16-bit signed, LPS+ orientation), organized in per-annotation-type folders (imagesTr, cow_seg_labelsTr, roi_loc_labelsTr, antpos_edges_labelsTr), with paired CTA and MRA acquired per patient.
details.splits: Training: 125 patients (250 CTA+MRA scans); Validation: 5 patients (10 scans, images only); Test: 70 patients (labels not publicly released).
details.extra: Voxel-level annotations were created using virtual-reality tooling with expert verification; each patient has paired CTA and MRA of the same anatomy.

name: CURVAS
full_name: Calibration and Uncertainty for multiRater Volume Assessment in multiorgan Segmentation
segmentation_detail: Multi-rater segmentations by three independent experts of pancreas, kidney and liver on contrast-enhanced (portal venous phase) abdominal CT
other_labels: multi-rater annotations for inter-rater variability, uncertainty and calibration
description: Ninety contrast-enhanced abdominal CT scans, each segmented independently by three experts for pancreas, kidney and liver, to study inter-rater variability, uncertainty and model calibration in multi-organ segmentation.

label_schema: Label 0 - background
Label 1 - pancreas
Label 2 - kidney
Label 3 - liver
Each case carries 3 independent full-organ segmentations, one per expert rater (annotation_1, annotation_2, annotation_3)
details.file_structure: Contrast-enhanced (portal venous phase) abdominal CT volumes in NIfTI (.nii.gz) format, one folder per case containing the CT image plus three separate multi-label expert annotation volumes (one per rater) using the shared 0/1/2/3 organ label map.
details.splits: 90 CT scans total: 20 training + 5 validation + 65 test (validation and test labels withheld during the challenge).
details.extra: Multi-rater dataset (CURVAS = Calibration and Uncertainty for multiRater Volume Assessment in multiorgan Segmentation); the 3 per-case annotations are meant for inter-rater variability/uncertainty modeling, not a single consensus ground truth. Cases are stratified by cyst count/pathology into hidden groups A/B/C. License CC BY-NC.

name: MAMA-MIA
full_name: MAMA-MIA: A Large-Scale Multi-Center Breast Cancer DCE-MRI Benchmark Dataset
size: 1,506 annotated training scans plus 574 external validation cases (multi-institutional)
paired_text_detail: Structured clinical and demographic variables per case, including pathologic complete response (pCR) to neoadjuvant chemotherapy
multi_sequence_detail: Dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI with a pre-contrast phase and multiple post-contrast phases
segmentation_detail: Expert manual and automated primary-tumor segmentation masks
description: A large multi-institutional homogenized breast dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI dataset with expert primary-tumor segmentations and clinical variables for tumor segmentation and treatment-response prediction.

label_schema: Primary-tumor segmentation mask (single foreground class): experts segmented the primary breast tumor including non-mass-enhancement (NME) areas; background elsewhere
Two segmentation versions provided per case: expert-corrected gold-standard masks and the automatic (uncorrected) nnU-Net baseline masks
49 harmonized tabular variables per case: 21 clinical, 6 demographic, and 22 imaging/acquisition-parameter fields
Secondary clinical prediction target available: pathologic complete response (pCR) to neoadjuvant chemotherapy (NAC)
details.file_structure: Images and masks are stored as compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz); each case is keyed by collection acronym + patient ID (e.g. ISPY1_1221), with DCE-MRI phases named CASE_0000 (pre-contrast), CASE_0001/_0002/... (post-contrast) in an images folder, and separate folders for expert vs. automatic tumor-segmentation masks; accompanying metadata/clinical data and automatic-segmentation QA scores and the train/test split are provided as CSV/Excel tables (hosted on Synapse, DOI 10.7303/syn60868042).
details.extra: 1,506 pre-treatment DCE-MRI cases aggregated from four TCIA collections (ISPY1, Breast-MRI-NACT-Pilot, ISPY2, Duke-Breast-Cancer-MRI); only 163 cases had pre-existing expert segmentations, the rest were auto-segmented by a deep-learning model then corrected/verified by 16 breast-cancer experts, yielding 1,506 expert + 1,506 automatic masks. A train/test split table is provided for reproducibility but exact counts were not confirmed from the sources reviewed.

name: HNTS-MRG 2024
full_name: Head and Neck Tumor Segmentation for MR-Guided Radiotherapy 2024
anatomy: Head and neck (primary tumor GTVp and nodal GTVn volumes)
size: ~150 training patients (pre-RT and mid-RT), ~50 test patients
multi_sequence_detail: Primarily T2-weighted MRI at each timepoint
segmentation_detail: Expert GTVp (primary tumor) and GTVn (nodal) segmentation masks on pre-RT and mid-RT MRI
description: An MRI dataset of head-and-neck cancer patients imaged pre- and mid-radiotherapy with expert primary and nodal tumor segmentations for MR-guided adaptive radiotherapy.

label_schema: Segmentation mask, single label volume with integer values
0 - background
1 - GTVp (primary gross tumor volume)
2 - GTVn (metastatic/nodal gross tumor volume; all nodes merged into this single label)
details.file_structure: NIfTI compressed (.nii.gz) files organized as HNTSMRG24_train/<patientID>/{preRT,midRT}/, where preRT holds the pre-RT T2w image (<id>_preRT_T2.nii.gz) and its mask (<id>_preRT_mask.nii.gz), and midRT holds the mid-RT T2w image (<id>_midRT_T2.nii.gz), mid-RT mask (<id>_midRT_mask.nii.gz), plus the pre-RT image and mask registered to mid-RT space (<id>_preRT_T2_registered.nii.gz, <id>_preRT_mask_registered.nii.gz).
details.splits: Training: 150 patient cases. Test set size not stated on the official dataset page.
details.extra: Two challenge tasks share the same patients: Task 1 = pre-RT segmentation, Task 2 = mid-RT segmentation. Masks are expert reference (ground-truth) GTVp/GTVn contours on T2-weighted MRI.

name: AortaSeg24
full_name: Multi-Class Segmentation of Aortic Branches and Zones in CTA
segmentation_detail: Multi-class segmentation of the aorta, individual branch vessels, and clinically relevant SVS/STS aortic zones (23 classes)
description: A MICCAI 2024 challenge providing 100 annotated CT angiography volumes with multi-class labels of aortic branches and SVS/STS zones for automated aorta segmentation and surgical planning in type B aortic dissection.

label_schema: Multi-class 3D segmentation: 23 foreground classes (integer labels 1-23) plus background=0, stored in a single mask volume per subject
10 aortic SVS/STS zones: Zone 0, Zone 1, Zone 2, Zone 3, Zone 4, Zone 5, Zone 6, Zone 7, Zone 8, Zone 9

13 aortic branch vessels: Innominate (brachiocephalic) artery, Left common carotid artery, Left subclavian artery, Celiac artery, Superior mesenteric artery (SMA), Right renal artery, Left renal artery, Right common iliac artery, Left common iliac artery, Right internal iliac artery, Left internal iliac artery, Right external iliac artery, Left external iliac artery
details.file_structure: Data in .mha (MetaImage) format, organized as an images/ folder (e.g. subject001_CTA.mha) and a masks/ folder with a matching single multi-class label volume per subject (e.g. subject001_label.mha); CTA volumes resampled to isotropic 1 mm voxels.
details.splits: 100 total CTA volumes from 100 uncomplicated Type-B Aortic Dissection (uTBAD) patients, split into 50 training/development (released with labels), 10 validation (hidden), and 40 test (hidden).
details.extra: The exact integer-to-anatomy assignment for the 23 classes was not confirmed from the accessible sources (paper/challenge page list the class names but not a numbered mapping table); zones follow the SVS/STS aortic reporting standards. Contact weishao@ufl.edu for the official label dictionary.

name: LNQ2023
segmentation_detail: Segmentation masks of mediastinal lymph nodes >1 cm from cancer clinical-trial patients; partially annotated training (subset of nodes), fully annotated validation/test
description: A contrast-enhanced chest CT benchmark for weakly-supervised segmentation of enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes from oncology clinical-trial imaging, with data hosted on TCIA (MEDIASTINAL-LYMPH-NODE-SEG, 513 subjects).

label_schema: Binary segmentation mask: foreground = clinically relevant mediastinal lymph node(s) (short-axis > 1 cm), background = everything else
Single target class (lymph node); no multi-class/per-node instance labels
Training masks are PARTIAL: typically only one node per case (the trial-tracked lesion) is segmented, not all abnormal nodes
Validation/test masks are FULL: every abnormal mediastinal node with estimated short axis > 1 cm is segmented (total mediastinal nodal disease burden)
details.file_structure: Chest CT volumes and their segmentation masks are distributed as paired NRRD files (.nrrd), one image + one label mask per case.
details.splits: 383 training (partially annotated), 20 validation (fully annotated), 100 test (fully annotated)
details.extra: Data comes from oncology clinical-trial patients (2007-2020) via the Tumor Imaging Metrics Core (TIMC) at MGH/Dana-Farber/Brigham & Women's, Boston; the intended setup is weakly-supervised learning (train on partial labels, evaluate against full labels) scored by Dice and Average Symmetric Surface Distance (ASSD).

name: ISLES 2024
multi_sequence_detail: Acute multimodal CT (NCCT, CTA, CT perfusion source and derived perfusion maps); final infarct ground truth derived from follow-up MRI (DWI)
segmentation_detail: Binary segmentation mask of the final post-treatment ischemic stroke infarct
description: A stroke imaging challenge providing acute multimodal CT (NCCT, CTA, CT perfusion) with follow-up-MRI-derived infarct masks and tabular clinical data for final ischemic lesion segmentation.

label_schema: Binary segmentation mask of the final ischemic stroke infarct
Value 0 - background (non-infarcted tissue)
Value 1 - final post-treatment infarct lesion (delineated on follow-up MRI)
Companion label: vessel-occlusion masks derived from acute CT angiography (CTA)
details.file_structure: BIDS-organized directories of compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz) files: rawdata/ holds per-subject acute CT (NCCT, CTA, CTP) plus follow-up DWI/ADC, derivatives/ holds registered CTP perfusion maps (Tmax, MTT, CBF, CBV) and the binary infarct segmentation named like sub-strokecaseXXXX_ses-02_lesion-msk.nii.gz, and phenotype/ holds clinical tabular data.
details.splits: Full multicenter dataset described in the paper comprises 245 cases; the challenge released training data in batches with a separate hidden test set, but exact public-train vs. test counts are not confirmed from the sources reviewed.
details.extra: Ground-truth infarct masks were delineated on follow-up MRI (DWI/ADC) acquired 2-9 days after the acute CT; the task is to predict this final infarct from acute imaging alone.

name: PENGWIN
full_name: Pelvic Bone Fragments with Injuries Segmentation Challenge
anatomy: Pelvic bones (sacrum and hipbone fracture fragments)
size: 150 patient CT scans plus synthetically generated 2D X-ray images
segmentation_detail: Semi-automatically annotated and medical-expert-validated segmentations of sacrum and hipbone fracture fragments in 3D CT and in 2D X-ray
description: A MICCAI 2024 challenge dataset of 150 pelvic fracture CT scans and synthetically generated 2D X-rays with expert-validated fragment-level segmentation annotations of the sacrum and hipbone.

label_schema: Task 1 (CT), voxel-wise integer labels: 0 = background
Labels 1-10 = sacrum (SA) fracture fragments (one integer per fragment, up to 10)
Labels 11-20 = left hipbone/ilium (LI) fracture fragments (up to 10)
Labels 21-30 = right hipbone/ilium (RI) fracture fragments (up to 10)
An unfractured bone is represented as a single fragment (the whole bone)

Task 2 (X-ray): multi-label uint32 mask where each pixel is a binary bit-vector (bits allow overlapping fragments due to projection): bits 1-10 = SA fragments, bits 11-20 = LI fragments, bits 21-30 = RI fragments
details.file_structure: Task 1 CT images and label masks are stored as .mha volumes (images split across PENGWIN_CT_train_images_part1/2.zip, masks in PENGWIN_CT_train_labels.zip); Task 2 X-ray inputs and masks are TIFF files organized as train/input/images/x-ray/[ID].tif and train/output/images/x-ray/[ID].tif, filenames using a 3-digit CT/patient ID (001-100) plus a 4-digit image index (0000-0499).
details.splits: Training released publicly: Task 1 = 100 CT scans; Task 2 = 50,000 synthetic X-rays (500 projections per each of the 100 CTs). Test set counts are not confirmed from the sources reviewed.
details.extra: Ground-truth segmentations were semi-automatically annotated and then validated by medical experts; the X-ray masks are uint32 bit-encoded and cannot be viewed with standard image viewers, and about half the synthetic X-rays include K-wires and orthopedic screws.

name: CMRxRecon2024
multi_sequence_detail: Multi-contrast raw k-space: cine, T1/T2 mapping, tagging, phase-contrast (flow2d) and dark-blood imaging across long-axis, short-axis, outflow-tract and aortic views, with undersampling masks/trajectories and autocalibration signals
description: A multi-contrast, multi-view raw cardiac MRI k-space dataset from 330 volunteers on a Siemens 3T scanner for developing accelerated undersampled reconstruction methods.

label_schema: No segmentation/classification/detection annotations - this is an MRI reconstruction challenge
Ground-truth 'label' = fully-sampled (reference) k-space, provided for training only
Reconstruction targets span contrasts: cardiac Cine, Aorta, T1/T2 Mapping, Tagging (train), plus unseen Flow2d and BlackBlood in val/test
Task 1 target: reconstruct from uniform undersampling at 4x, 8x, 10x acceleration
Task 2 target: reconstruct from uniform/Gaussian/pseudo-radial trajectories at 4x-24x acceleration
Companion arrays: undersampled k-space, sampling/undersampling masks, and autocalibration signal (ACS: 16 lines for Task 1, 16x16 region for Task 2)
details.file_structure: Data stored as MATLAB .mat files per subject/contrast; training files hold fully-sampled k-space (ground truth), while validation/test hold undersampled k-space plus sampling trajectories and ACS - arrays are 5D (nx,ny,nc,nz,nt) for Cine/Aorta/Mapping/Tagging, 4D for BlackBlood, with 2D masks (Task 1) or 3D masks (Task 2).
details.splits: 330 healthy-volunteer scans (3.0T Siemens MAGNETOM Vida): 200 training / 60 validation / 70 test
details.extra: No manual annotations or reader labels exist; evaluation is image-quality/reconstruction fidelity against the fully-sampled reference, and validation/test add two contrasts (Flow2d, BlackBlood) not present in training to test generalization.

name: CMRxMotion
full_name: Extreme Cardiac MRI Analysis Challenge under Respiratory Motion
anatomy: Heart (left ventricle, left ventricle myocardium, right ventricle)
size: ~40 healthy volunteers imaged under 4 respiratory-motion levels
segmentation_detail: Left ventricle, left ventricle myocardium and right ventricle segmentation on cine MRI (diagnostically adequate images)
description: Cine cardiac MRI from ~40 volunteers acquired under four respiratory-motion conditions with radiologist image-quality grades and LV/myocardium/RV segmentations for motion-robustness benchmarking (MICCAI 2022 STACOM).

label_schema: Task 2 segmentation masks (NIfTI, integer-valued): 0 - background
Label 1 - left ventricle (LV) blood pool
Label 2 - left ventricular myocardium (MYO)
Label 3 - right ventricle (RV)
Task 1 image-quality assessment: 3 ordinal motion-artifact classes - Label 1 mild motion (radiologist score 4-5), Label 2 intermediate motion (score 3), Label 3 severe motion (score 1-2)
details.file_structure: Cine CMR volumes exported from DICOM to NIfTI (.nii.gz); per subject/respiratory-condition, 3D image volumes at ED and ES frames are provided, with a matching integer-labeled segmentation .nii.gz for diagnostically adequate images plus a per-image quality label for the IQA task.
details.splits: 40 healthy volunteers total: 20 training (images + quality labels + segmentation released), 5 validation (images only), 15 test (held out for offline evaluation).
details.extra: Each volunteer was imaged under 4 respiratory conditions (normal breath-hold, halved breath-hold, free breathing, intensive breathing) to induce varying motion artifacts; segmentation (Task 2) is only provided/evaluated on diagnostically adequate images (mild/intermediate motion), while severe-motion images are excluded from segmentation.

name: LUMIERE
size: 91 patients, 638 study dates, 2487 MRI images (~32.5 GB imaging ZIP)
paired_text_detail: Expert RANO tumor-response ratings plus demographics, pathology and survival/clinical data provided as separate tables
multi_sequence_detail: Multiparametric MRI: pre-contrast T1, post-contrast T1, T2 and FLAIR at each timepoint (599 of 638 studies have all four)
segmentation_detail: Automated tumor segmentations from DeepBraTumIA and HD-GLIO-AUTO for timepoints with all four sequences
description: A longitudinal multiparametric MRI dataset of 91 glioblastoma patients across 638 study dates with automated tumor segmentations and expert RANO response ratings.

label_schema: Two automated tumor segmentation label maps per timepoint (only for study dates with all four MR sequences), from two different tools
DeepBraTumIA (seg_mask.nii.gz): multi-class tumor label map with 3 tumor tissue classes - necrosis, contrast-enhancing tumor, and edema
HD-GLIO-AUTO (segmentation.nii.gz): 2 tumor classes - label 1 = contrast-enhancing tumor, label 2 = non-enhancing T2/FLAIR signal abnormality (per HD-GLIO convention)

Expert RANO rating (LUMIERE-ExpertRating.csv): neuroradiologist response category per selected follow-up timepoint - Post-op (Op), Complete Response (CR), Partial Response (PR), Stable Disease (SD), Progressive Disease (PD)
RANO rating also records rationale/comments, presence of non-measurable/measurable/target/non-target lesions, and bi-dimensional (Macdonald) measurements for measurable lesions
Per-tumor-label radiomic features included: PyRadiomics features and CoLlAGe features for each segmentation label and MRI sequence
Pathology/demographics CSV: MGMT promoter methylation status, IDH1 status, sex, age at diagnosis, and overall survival time (subset of patients)
details.file_structure: On disk it is organized as Patient-XXX/week-00N folders holding skull-stripped NIfTI sequences (CT1, T1, T2, FLAIR .nii.gz) plus DeepBraTumIA-segmentation and HD-GLIO-AUTO-segmentation subfolders with label-map .nii.gz files in both atlas/registered and native/back-transformed spaces (with transform .tfm/.mat files); ratings, MRI acquisition info, demographics/pathology, and radiomic features are separate CSV files.
details.extra: No official train/val/test split; the two automated segmentations (DeepBraTumIA and HD-GLIO-AUTO) are provided deliberately to benchmark algorithm/segmentation variability, and both are automated (not manual ground truth); some RANO-rated timepoints have no segmentation because rating was possible with only a subset of the four sequences. Cohort: 91 GBM patients, 638 study dates, 2487 images.

name: BraTS 2024 Post-Treatment Glioma (BraTS-GLI)
full_name: 2024 Brain Tumor Segmentation Challenge: Adult Glioma Post-Treatment
multi_sequence_detail: Co-registered, skull-stripped native T1, post-contrast T1 (T1c/T1Gd), T2, and T2-FLAIR sequences
segmentation_detail: Expert annotations of four sub-regions: enhancing tissue (ET), non-enhancing tumor core (NETC), surrounding non-enhancing FLAIR hyperintensity (SNFH), and resection cavity (RC)
description: The BraTS 2024 post-treatment adult glioma challenge provides multi-sequence brain MRI (T1, T1c, T2, T2-FLAIR) with expert segmentations of enhancing tissue, non-enhancing tumor core, FLAIR hyperintensity, and resection cavity for post-therapy glioma monitoring.

label_schema: Voxel-wise multi-class segmentation: single -seg NIfTI volume with 4 tumor sub-region classes over background
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - NETC (non-enhancing tumor core): necrosis/cysts, dark on both T1 and T1-Gd
Label 2 - SNFH (surrounding non-enhancing FLAIR hyperintensity): edema, infiltrating/non-enhancing tumor, radiation-related hyperintensity and gliosis
Label 3 - ET (enhancing tissue): thick/nodular T1-Gd enhancement, excluding vessels and thin linear treatment-related enhancement
Label 4 - RC (resection cavity): recent and chronic post-surgical resection cavities
details.file_structure: One folder per case (named BraTS-GLI-XXXXX-XXX) containing four co-registered, skull-stripped MRI channels as NIfTI .nii.gz (-t1n = pre-contrast T1, -t1c = post-contrast T1/T1-Gd, -t2w = T2, -t2f = T2-FLAIR) plus one multi-label ground-truth mask (-seg.nii.gz).
details.splits: Cases split ~70% training / 10% validation / 20% testing out of ~2,200 total cases from 7 institutions; public ground-truth segmentation labels released only for the training set, with validation/test labels withheld (exact per-split case counts not confirmed).
details.extra: Ground truth was generated by STAPLE-fusing five nnU-Net pre-segmentations then manual radiologist refinement and board-certified neuroradiologist approval; the held-out test set was independently annotated by two annotator/approver teams to assess inter-rater reliability. Evaluation uses lesion-wise Dice and Hausdorff-95.

name: BraTS-MEN-RT 2024
full_name: 2024 Brain Tumor Segmentation Challenge: Meningioma Radiotherapy
size: 750 radiotherapy-planning brain MRIs (multi-institutional) with expert GTV annotations on the training/validation partitions
multi_sequence_detail: Single 3D post-contrast T1-weighted radiotherapy-planning MRI in native acquisition space (defaced); not a multi-sequence set
segmentation_detail: Single-label expert-annotated gross tumor volume (GTV) / at-risk post-operative site for radiotherapy planning, approved by neuroradiologists and radiation oncologists
description: BraTS-MEN-RT is the largest multi-institutional collection of 3D post-contrast T1-weighted radiotherapy-planning MRIs of intracranial meningiomas with expert gross tumor volume (GTV) annotations for automated radiotherapy target delineation.

label_schema: Single foreground class - radiotherapy target volume (binary mask): foreground = the GTV plus any at-risk post-operative site; background = 0
In the intact setting the label is the tumor portion visible on the post-contrast T1w (T1c) MRI; in the postoperative setting it covers the resection cavity / at-risk site per RT planning protocols
Annotations approved by expert neuroradiologists and radiation oncologists; derived from the treating institution's DICOM-RT structure sets
details.file_structure: One defaced 3D post-contrast T1-weighted (T1c) MRI and one single-label GTV segmentation mask per case, stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz) in per-case folders under the BraTS-MEN-RT-<ID> naming convention; converted from source DICOM / DICOM-RT with dcmrtstruct2nii.
details.splits: 750 total cases from 7 US/UK academic centers: Training 500 (images + reference labels released), Validation 70 (images only released), Testing 180 (not publicly released).
details.extra: Unlike earlier BraTS tasks, images are defaced (extracranial tissue preserved, not skull-stripped) and kept in native acquisition space rather than atlas-registered; only a single T1c sequence is provided (no multiparametric MRI). Non-target/non-segmented meningiomas may be present in scans.

name: BraTS-GoAT 2024
size: Pooled multi-tumor BraTS cohort (thousands of cases across glioma, meningioma, metastases, pediatric)
multi_sequence_detail: Co-registered, skull-stripped multiparametric MRI: T1, T1-contrast-enhanced (T1c), T2, and T2-FLAIR
segmentation_detail: Expert-annotated tumor sub-region masks (enhancing tumor, tumor core, whole tumor) pooled across glioma, meningioma, brain metastases and pediatric tumors
description: The BraTS-GoAT 2024 task benchmarks the generalizability of brain-tumor segmentation methods by pooling co-registered, skull-stripped multiparametric MRI across diverse tumor types with expert sub-region masks.

label_schema: Voxel-wise multi-class tumor sub-region segmentation mask, values consistent across all tumor types
Label 0 - background / everything else
Label 1 - NCR (necrotic tumor core / necrosis)
Label 2 - ED (peritumoral edema / invaded tissue)
Label 3 - ET (enhancing tumor)
Evaluated hierarchical regions derived from labels: Enhancing Tumor (ET = label 3), Tumor Core (TC = NCR + ET = labels 1+3), Whole Tumor (WT = NCR + ED + ET = labels 1+2+3)
Presence of each sub-region varies by case and tumor type (some lesions lack necrosis, edema, or enhancement), but label values never change
details.file_structure: Standard BraTS layout: one folder per subject containing four co-registered skull-stripped multiparametric MRI volumes (T1 native, T1-Gd/post-contrast, T2, T2-FLAIR) plus a ground-truth segmentation volume, all as NIfTI (.nii.gz) files with per-region integer labels.
details.extra: Data is pooled from five BraTS 2023 populations (adult glioma, sub-Saharan Africa adult glioma, intracranial meningioma, brain metastases, pediatric tumors); ground-truth annotations are created/approved by expert neuroradiologists, but for meningioma and brain-metastasis training subsets ground truth is released for only a subset, and the private test-set composition is undisclosed. Challenge site is now archived (data no longer downloadable there; continued in BraTS 2025 Lighthouse-GoAT). Evaluation uses Dice and 95% Hausdorff distance.

name: UCSF-PDGM
full_name: University of California San Francisco Preoperative Diffuse Glioma MRI
multi_sequence_detail: Standardized 3T single-scanner protocol: 3D T1 pre/post-contrast, T2, T2/FLAIR, SWI, DWI, HARDI (high angular resolution diffusion), and ASL perfusion
segmentation_detail: Manual tumor segmentation masks with three compartments: enhancing tumor, non-enhancing/necrotic core, and surrounding FLAIR abnormality
description: UCSF-PDGM contains standardized preoperative multi-sequence 3T brain MRI of 495 histopathologically proven diffuse gliomas with manual tumor segmentations and molecular/clinical labels, hosted on TCIA in NIfTI format.

label_schema: Multicompartment tumor segmentation mask, 3 tumor classes: enhancing tumor
non-enhancing / necrotic tumor core
surrounding FLAIR abnormality (peritumoral edema)
Per-patient clinical/molecular labels: WHO 2021 grade (II-IV)
IDH mutation status
MGMT promoter methylation status
1p/19q codeletion status
details.file_structure: Preprocessed, skull-stripped, co-registered NIfTI (.nii.gz) files at 1mm isotropic resolution organized in per-patient folders (e.g., UCSF-PDGM-0004), with one segmentation file per case named like *_tumor_segmentation.nii.gz and clinical/molecular labels in an accompanying spreadsheet/CSV.
details.extra: Tumor masks were first produced by an automated deep-learning ensemble model, then manually corrected by trained radiologists and approved by two expert reviewers; a deep-learning brain mask (skull-strip) is included but no full anatomic parcellation. Exact integer label values for the three compartments are not stated on the official TCIA page. Collection covers ~495 unique patients (originally reported as 501, six being follow-up scans).

name: UCSF-ALPTDG
full_name: University of California San Francisco Adult Longitudinal Post-Treatment Diffuse Glioma MRI
anatomy: Brain (adult diffuse glioma, post-treatment longitudinal follow-up)
size: 298 patients; 596 post-treatment MRI exams (two consecutive posttreatment timepoints per patient)
multi_sequence_detail: Multimodal 3D brain MRI: T1 pre-contrast, T1 post-contrast, T2, and T2-FLAIR, acquired on a standardized 3.0-T protocol across two longitudinal timepoints
segmentation_detail: Expert voxelwise segmentations of four tumor subregions (enhancing tissue, nonenhancing FLAIR hyperintensity, nonenhancing tumor core, resection cavity) plus longitudinal change annotations tracking ET and SNFH between timepoints
license: Noncommercial research use; data use agreement required
description: UCSF-ALPTDG is a longitudinal post-treatment adult diffuse glioma dataset of multimodal 3D brain MRI (T1 pre/post-contrast, T2, FLAIR) from 298 patients scanned at two consecutive posttreatment timepoints, with expert voxelwise tumor subregion and longitudinal-change segmentations, hosted by the UCSF Center for Intelligent Imaging under a data use agreement.

label_schema: Voxelwise tumor segmentation (4 foreground classes + background), from the official nnUNet dataset.json:
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - Tumor_Necrosis (nonenhancing / necrotic tumor core)
Label 2 - Edema (surrounding nonenhancing FLAIR hyperintensity, SNFH)
Label 3 - Enhancing Tumor (enhancing tissue, ET)
Label 4 - Resection Cavity

Longitudinal change annotations: separate masks tracking interval change in enhancing tissue (ET) and in SNFH between the two post-treatment timepoints, benchmarked via FLAIR-change and T1CE-change segmentation models using timepoint-1, timepoint-2, and subtraction images
details.file_structure: 3D MRI in NIfTI (.nii.gz), one folder per patient/timepoint with four co-registered, skull-stripped sequences (FLAIR, T1, T2, T1CE/post-contrast); expert segmentation masks are single-file multi-label NIfTI volumes (nnUNet-style imagesTr/labelsTr layout in the released benchmark).
details.splits: 298 patients total (each imaged at two consecutive post-treatment timepoints); official benchmark split 248 training / 50 test
details.extra: The four subregion class names/integers above come from the official rachitsaluja/UCSF-ALPTDG-benchmarks nnUNet dataset.json; the paper (Radiology: AI, ryai.230182) describes the same four compartments plus longitudinal ET and SNFH change annotations. Follow-up intervals between the two timepoints vary across patients, which may limit cross-patient comparisons.

name: SPIDER
full_name: Lumbar Spine Segmentation in MR Images: A Dataset and Public Benchmark
size: 218 studies, 447 sagittal T1/T2 MRI series (~3.8 GB)
paired_text_detail: CSV/metadata with patient demographics, scanner parameters, and per-IVD radiological degenerative gradings (not free-text reports)
multi_sequence_detail: Sagittal T1- and T2-weighted lumbar MRI series
segmentation_detail: Reference masks for vertebrae, intervertebral discs, and spinal canal
description: SPIDER is a multi-vendor lumbar spine MRI dataset of 218 studies (447 sagittal T1/T2 series from four hospitals) with reference segmentations of vertebrae, discs, and spinal canal plus per-level degenerative radiological gradings.

label_schema: Multi-class 3D segmentation mask, one per MRI series; three anatomical structures labeled: vertebrae, intervertebral discs (IVDs), and spinal canal
Vertebrae: integer labels numbered sequentially from the most caudal (lowest) vertebra upward, i.e. 1, 2, 3, ... (label 1 = lowest lumbar vertebra, typically L5)
Spinal canal (dural sac): fixed label value 100
Intervertebral discs (IVDs): label values 201, 202, 203, ... numbered from the lowest disc upward, each disc identified by the vertebra directly above it

Companion radiological_gradings.csv scores each disc level for: Modic changes (I-III), upper/lower endplate defects (Schmorl nodes), spondylolisthesis, disc herniation, disc narrowing, disc bulging, and Pfirrmann grade (1-5)
details.file_structure: Distributed on Zenodo as images.zip and masks.zip containing one MetaImage (.mha) volume per sagittal MRI series, with matching image/mask filenames per patient; plus overview.csv (patient/scanner metadata and set assignment) and radiological_gradings.csv.
details.splits: 218 patients / 447 sagittal T1 and T2 MRI series total, with training and validation assignments provided in overview.csv; an additional hidden test set (not publicly released) is used for the accompanying SPIDER Grand Challenge.
details.extra: Annotations were created/corrected in 3D Slicer (v5.0.3). Beyond the voxel segmentations, the dataset uniquely ships per-disc-level radiological degeneration gradings as a separate CSV, enabling both segmentation and classification/grading tasks.

name: RSNA 2024 Lumbar Spine Degenerative Classification
multi_sequence_detail: Sagittal T1, sagittal T2/STIR, and axial T2 lumbar MRI series per study
segmentation_detail: Coordinate (point) labels at disc levels rather than pixel masks
other_labels: 3 severity grades (Normal/Mild, Moderate, Severe) across 5 disc levels (L1/L2-L5/S1)
description: The RSNA 2024 challenge provides multi-sequence lumbar spine MRI studies in DICOM with per-level severity labels for five degenerative conditions across five disc levels plus coordinate keypoint annotations.

label_schema: Task: per-study ordinal severity classification of 5 degenerative conditions at each of 5 disc levels = 25 targets per study
5 conditions: Left Neural Foraminal Narrowing, Right Neural Foraminal Narrowing, Left Subarticular Stenosis, Right Subarticular Stenosis, Spinal Canal Stenosis
3 severity grades per target: Normal/Mild, Moderate, Severe (metric sample weights 1 / 2 / 4)
5 disc levels: L1/L2, L2/L3, L3/L4, L4/L5, L5/S1 (columns named e.g. spinal_canal_stenosis_l1_l2)
Coordinate (point) labels: train_label_coordinates.csv gives an (x, y) center point for a condition at a level, tied to a specific series_id + instance_number (no pixel masks)
Series/plane labels: train_series_descriptions.csv tags each series as Sagittal T1, Sagittal T2/STIR, or Axial T2
details.file_structure: MRI images are DICOM files laid out as train_images/{study_id}/{series_id}/{instance_number}.dcm; study-level severity labels in train.csv (study_id + 25 [condition]_[level] columns), point annotations in train_label_coordinates.csv (study_id, series_id, instance_number, condition, level, x, y), and series metadata in train_series_descriptions.csv.
details.splits: ~1,975 training studies with public labels; test set is hidden (used for leaderboard scoring), so no public test counts. No official train/validation split (competitors made their own k-folds).
details.extra: Coordinate points mark only the single most-relevant instance per condition/level rather than exhaustive masks; some train.csv severity cells are missing, and evaluation uses mean column-wise weighted log loss plus a derived any_severe_spinal target.

name: MRSpineSeg
full_name: MRSpineSeg Challenge (MICCAI CSI 2019): Lumbar Spine MRI Vertebrae and Intervertebral Disc Segmentation
size: 172 training + 20 (round-1) + 23 (round-2) sagittal T2-weighted lumbar MRI volumes (test labels withheld)
multi_sequence_detail: Sagittal T2-weighted lumbar MRI only
segmentation_detail: Multi-class voxel masks of individual vertebrae (e.g. L1-L5, T9-T12, S1) and intervertebral discs, up to ~20 label classes
host: MICCAI CSI 2019 MRSpineSeg Challenge (authors' repo; public mirror on Baidu AI Studio / PaddleSeg MedicalSeg)
description: MRSpineSeg provides 172 sagittal T2-weighted lumbar spine MRI volumes in NIfTI with multi-class segmentation masks of individual vertebrae and intervertebral discs (up to ~20 classes).

label_schema: Multi-class voxel segmentation with 20 classes total (label 0 = background) confirmed by out_channels=20 / n_classes=20 in the training code
Labels 1-10 are the 10 vertebrae; labels 11-19 are the 9 intervertebral discs (verified: evaluate.py treats mask values <=10 as vertebrae and >10 as discs)
Label 1 - S (sacrum)
Label 2 - L5
Label 3 - L4
Label 4 - L3
Label 5 - L2
Label 6 - L1
Label 7 - T12
Label 8 - T11
Label 9 - T10
Label 10 - T9
Label 11 - disc L5/S1
Label 12 - disc L4/L5
Label 13 - disc L3/L4
Label 14 - disc L2/L3
Label 15 - disc L1/L2
Label 16 - disc T12/L1
Label 17 - disc T11/T12
Label 18 - disc T10/T11
Label 19 - disc T9/T10
details.file_structure: Sagittal MR volumes and their integer-label segmentation masks are stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz) files, organized into two-stage folders (data/coarse and data/fine) each with in/nii/original_mr (images) and in/nii/mask (masks), plus HDF5 (h5py) caches generated for training; cases are named Case1 through Case215.
details.extra: The repository references 215 cases (Case1-Case215); it uses a two-stage coarse-then-fine framework where the coarse and fine stages share the same raw MR volumes but differ in mask resolution. The exact vertebra-vs-disc integer ordering (vertebrae 1-10, discs 11-19) is confirmed from the code, and individual level names follow the official MRSpineSeg Challenge convention.

name: Gold Atlas
full_name: Gold Atlas Male Pelvis MR-CT Dataset
multi_sequence_detail: T1- and T2-weighted MRI with deformably registered CT
segmentation_detail: Radiotherapy-relevant pelvic organ delineations by 5 expert observers with consensus and STAPLE masks
description: The Gold Atlas dataset provides paired T1/T2-weighted MRI and registered CT DICOM data of 19 male pelvises with multi-observer organ delineations for MR-to-CT synthesis and segmentation research.

label_schema: Radiotherapy organ-at-risk segmentation of the male pelvis: 5 expert observers independently delineated NINE organs per patient on the T2w MR images, plus an agreed consensus contour and a STAPLE estimate per structure
Confirmed delineated structures (8 of the 9, verbatim from a paper that used the consensus contours): Anal canal
Penile bulb
Prostate
Seminal vesicles
Bladder
Rectum
Left femoral head
Right femoral head
A ninth organ is delineated (paper states 'nine organs') but its exact name could not be confirmed from accessible sources
details.file_structure: All data is DICOM: per patient a T1w MR, a T2w MR, a CT, and a CT deformably registered to the MR; organ contours are stored as DICOM RTSTRUCT (per-observer x5, a consensus, and a STAPLE per structure), delineated on the T2w MR and transferable to the registered CT via the provided deformation vector fields.
details.extra: Multi-reader dataset: 19 male patients acquired at 3 departments; contours were drawn on T2w MRI (so they align to MR, and to CT only via the supplied MR-to-CT deformable registration); intended for training/validation of automatic segmentation and MR-to-synthetic-CT methods.

name: MSLesSeg 2024
multi_sequence_detail: T1-weighted, T2-weighted, and FLAIR per timepoint; scans registered to MNI-152 and skull-stripped
segmentation_detail: Manual MS lesion masks annotated on FLAIR by two operators and validated by an MS specialist neurologist
description: MSLesSeg 2024 is a longitudinal multi-center MS brain MRI dataset (~90 patients, ~150 timepoints) with T1/T2/FLAIR sequences and expert-validated FLAIR lesion segmentations in NIfTI format.

label_schema: Binary MS lesion segmentation mask (foreground = lesion, background = non-lesion)
Label 1 - multiple sclerosis lesion
Label 0 - background / non-lesion brain tissue
Masks annotated on FLAIR; T1-w (lesions hypointense) and T2-w (lesions hyperintense) used for lesion characterization
Single lesion class only (no per-lesion-type or per-region subclasses)
details.file_structure: NIfTI (.nii.gz) organized as patient/timepoint folders; training subjects have Px/Tx/ folders with Px_Tx_T1.nii.gz, Px_Tx_T2.nii.gz, Px_Tx_FLAIR.nii.gz and the ground-truth Px_Tx_MASK.nii.gz, while test subjects provide only the three modality volumes (no mask released).
details.extra: Multi-reader protocol: a neurologist identified lesion locations, two context-aware operators segmented each lesion on FLAIR (using T1/T2 for characterization), and an MS specialist neurologist visually verified and manually corrected the masks; lesions not visible on at least two consecutive slices were excluded. Dataset spans roughly 90 patients with 1-5 longitudinal timepoints each (~150 timepoints total).

name: Lausanne TOF-MRA Aneurysm Cohort
full_name: Lausanne Brain Aneurysm TOF-MRA Cohort (OpenNeuro ds003949)
multi_sequence_detail: Time-of-flight MR angiography (TOF-MRA) plus a T1-weighted structural volume for each subject
segmentation_detail: Voxel-wise binary manual aneurysm segmentation masks for patients in BIDS derivatives (manual_masks); also skull-stripped volumes and N4 bias-corrected derivatives
other_labels: probabilistic vessel atlas co-registered to subject space
description: OpenNeuro cohort of 284 TOF-MRA brain scans (157 aneurysm patients, 127 healthy controls) with voxel-wise manual aneurysm segmentation masks and T1w volumes in BIDS format for detection and segmentation research.

label_schema: Binary voxel-wise aneurysm segmentation mask (foreground value 1 = intracranial aneurysm, 0 = background)
Only patient subjects carry aneurysm masks; healthy-control subjects have no lesion mask
198 aneurysms annotated across the 157 patients (patients may have multiple masks)
Annotations are manual 'weak labels' (oversized/enlarged region annotations, ~4x faster to draw than tight voxel-wise contours) per Di Noto et al.
details.file_structure: BIDS layout in NIfTI (.nii.gz): raw anat folders hold each subject's TOF-MRA and T1w volumes, while derivatives/manual_masks holds skull-stripped TOF-MRA volumes plus binary aneurysm mask(s) for patients; additional derivatives include N4 bias-field-corrected volumes and registration transforms/vessel atlas.
details.extra: 284 total subjects (127 healthy controls / 157 aneurysm patients, 170 females) from Lausanne University Hospital; companion paper Di Noto et al. (arXiv:2103.06168, 'Towards Automated Brain Aneurysm Detection in TOF-MRA'); controls provide only skull-stripped volumes with no masks.

name: ChestX-Det
segmentation_detail: Polygon instance-segmentation contours plus bounding boxes for 13 thoracic disease categories (Atelectasis, Calcification, Cardiomegaly, Consolidation, Diffuse Nodule, Effusion, Emphysema, Fibrosis, Fracture, Mass, Nodule, Pleural Thickening, Pneumothorax), annotated by three board-certified radiologists
description: Extension of ChestX-Det10 with 3,578 NIH chest X-rays annotated with polygon instance-segmentation masks and bounding boxes for 13 thoracic abnormalities by three radiologists.

label_schema: 13 thoracic disease/abnormality categories, each an instance with a bounding box AND a polygon segmentation contour
Categories: Atelectasis, Calcification, Cardiomegaly, Consolidation, Diffuse Nodule, Effusion, Emphysema, Fibrosis, Fracture, Mass, Nodule, Pleural Thickening, Pneumothorax
Per-instance bounding box: [x1, y1, x2, y2] = left-top, right-bottom corner coordinates
Per-instance polygon: point-set of mask-contour vertices, e.g. [[[x, y], [x, y], ...], ...]
details.file_structure: Chest X-ray images (from NIH ChestX-14) with annotations stored in two JSON files, ChestX_Det_train.json and ChestX_Det_test.json, where each record has fields file_name, syms (category labels), boxes, and polygons; images distributed as downloadable zip archives.
details.splits: Official train/test split provided via ChestX_Det_train.json and ChestX_Det_test.json; 3578 images total (from NIH ChestX-14). Exact per-split image counts not stated on the official README.
details.extra: All images annotated by three board-certified radiologists; annotations provide co-registered bounding boxes and polygon instance-segmentation contours for the same findings.

name: TBX11K
other_labels: bounding boxes for active/obsolete (latent) TB lesions
image-level classes: healthy, sick but non-TB, active TB, latent/obsolete TB
host: mmcheng.net (Nankai University Media Computing Lab); train/val via Google Drive & Baidu, test on CodaLab
description: 11,200 chest X-rays (512x512) with image-level TB categories and bounding-box annotations of tuberculosis lesions, with the test set held out for an online CodaLab challenge.

label_schema: Image-level classification into 4 categories: Healthy, Sick & non-TB, Active TB, Latent TB (a few images are both Active & Latent TB)
Detection/bounding-box category 1 - ActiveTuberculosis
Detection/bounding-box category 2 - ObsoletePulmonaryTuberculosis (latent TB)
Detection/bounding-box category 3 - PulmonaryTuberculosis (uncertain/unknown TB type, used only in the held-out test set)
TB regions are marked with bounding boxes; healthy and sick-but-non-TB images have no boxes
details.file_structure: 512x512 chest X-ray images (PNG, downscaled from ~3000x3000 originals) organized in an imgs/ folder by class, with box annotations provided both as Pascal-VOC XML ([xmin,ymin,xmax,ymax]) and as COCO-style JSON ([x,y,width,height]).
details.splits: Total 11,200 images: train 6,600 (3,000 healthy / 3,000 sick&non-TB / 473 active TB / 104 latent TB / 23 active&latent), val 1,800 (800 / 800 / 157 / 36 / 7), test 2,800 (1,200 / 1,200 / 294 / 72 / 24 active&latent + 10 uncertain TB).
details.extra: Test-set bounding-box annotations are withheld for the online evaluation challenge; the test set additionally contains an 'Uncertain TB' subcategory whose TB type is not disclosed.

name: NODE21
full_name: NODE21 Nodule Detection and Generation Challenge
size: 4,882 frontal chest radiographs (1,134 with 1,476 nodule bounding boxes; 3,748 nodule-free) plus LUNA16-derived CT nodule patches
other_labels: 50x50x50mm CT nodule patches for generation track
license: permissive (aggregated from JSRT, PadChest, ChestX-ray14, Open-i; sources permit redistribution)
description: Public NODE21 training set of 4,882 frontal chest radiographs (1024x1024 MHA) with lung-nodule bounding boxes plus LUNA16 CT nodule patches, for a chest-nodule detection-and-generation challenge.

label_schema: Detection task: pulmonary nodule bounding boxes on frontal chest X-rays (nodule = single positive class)
Bounding box fields per nodule: x, y, width, height, label
label = 1 indicates nodule present; label = 0 indicates no nodule (negative image)
Generation track: JSON/CSV specifying target (x, y, width, height) locations where synthetic nodules should be placed
1,134 positive images annotated with 1,476 nodule bounding boxes by trained chest radiologists
details.file_structure: Images provided as 1024x1024 .mha files (original and OpenCXR-preprocessed versions); nodule bounding-box annotations stored in a metadata.csv (columns x, y, width, height, label), with a separate simulated_metadata.csv used for the generation track; distributed as dataset_node21.zip (~37.2 GB) on Zenodo.
details.splits: Public training set: 4,882 frontal chest radiographs (1,134 positive with nodules, 3,748 negative). Sequestered experimental test set: 281 CXRs, 166 positive. Generation track evaluated on 1,000 nodule-free CXRs. (Official train/val split not sub-divided.)
details.extra: Positive images sourced from JSRT, PadChest, ChestX-ray14, and Open-I; the generation track additionally provides CT nodule patches from LUNA16 for synthesizing nodules.

name: JSRT
full_name: Japanese Society of Radiological Technology Standard Digital Image Database (chest radiographs)
size: 247 posterior-anterior chest radiographs (154 with a lung nodule: 100 malignant, 54 benign; 93 without nodules); 2048x2048, 12-bit, 0.175 mm pixel spacing
segmentation_detail: No masks in the base dataset; lung/heart/clavicle segmentation masks are available via the separate SCR (Segmentation in Chest Radiographs) add-on dataset.
license: Free for research use (registration/request via JSRT)
description: Classic public reference database of 247 high-resolution 12-bit posterior-anterior chest radiographs (154 with lung nodules) widely used as a benchmark for nodule detection and, with the SCR add-on, lung/heart/clavicle segmentation.

label_schema: Base JSRT is an image-level classification/detection dataset (no pixel masks in the base release)
247 chest radiographs total: 154 with a lung nodule + 93 without a nodule
Nodule cases split by diagnosis: 100 malignant, 54 benign
Per-case metadata: patient age, gender (sex), diagnosis (malignant or benign)
Nodule location given as X and Y pixel coordinates (plus a simple diagram of nodule position)
Nodule subtlety: 5 groups by degree of subtlety (extremely subtle -> obvious)
SCR add-on (separate download) provides manual segmentation reference standards for: lung fields (left + right lung), heart, and clavicles (left + right)
details.file_structure: Base JSRT images are distributed as raw files (2048x2048 matrix, 0.175-mm pixels, 12-bit grayscale, no header, big-endian raw data), one file per case, with the age/sex/diagnosis/nodule-coordinate/subtlety metadata supplied in an accompanying list/text file; SCR segmentations are a separate download providing per-structure binary masks and landmark/point files for lung fields, heart, and clavicles keyed to the JSRT filenames.
details.splits: Base JSRT has no official train/test split. The SCR segmentation add-on defines two folds for cross-validation: Fold 1 = all odd-numbered JSRT images, Fold 2 = all even-numbered images (train on one fold, test on the other).
details.extra: Segmentation masks are NOT part of the base JSRT release; lung/heart/clavicle masks come only from the separate SCR (Segmentation in Chest Radiographs, van Ginneken et al., 2006) add-on. A simplified 'mini-JSRT' database (imgcom.jsrt.or.jp/minijsrtdb) also exists for classification/segmentation/regression teaching tasks.

name: SIIM-FISABIO-RSNA COVID-19
other_labels: image-level bounding boxes for pulmonary opacities
study-level labels: negative for pneumonia / typical / indeterminate / atypical COVID-19 appearance
description: COVID-19 chest radiographs in DICOM format with four-class study-level appearance labels and image-level bounding boxes annotating pulmonary opacities.

label_schema: Two prediction tasks: study-level 4-class classification + image-level opacity detection
Study-level (train_study_level.csv), 4 mutually-exclusive one-hot columns: 'Negative for Pneumonia', 'Typical Appearance', 'Indeterminate Appearance', 'Atypical Appearance'
Image-level (train_image_level.csv): a single detection class 'opacity' for COVID-19 lung opacities, plus 'none' for images with no findings
Bounding boxes given in the 'boxes' column as a JSON list of {x, y, width, height} in pixel coordinates (NaN/empty when 'none')
'label' column is a PredictionString, e.g. 'opacity 1 xmin ymin xmax ymax' or 'none 1 0 0 1 1' (class, confidence=1, box coords)
details.file_structure: Chest radiographs stored as DICOM files in nested train/<StudyInstanceUID>/<SeriesInstanceUID>/<image>.dcm folders; annotations provided in two CSVs (train_study_level.csv with study-level one-hot classes keyed by '<id>_study', and train_image_level.csv with per-image boxes/labels and StudyInstanceUID).
details.splits: Train: ~6,334 chest scans (studies) in DICOM; official test set is hidden (split into public/private leaderboard, ground truth not released).
details.extra: Bounding-box and appearance annotations were produced by a panel of board-certified radiologists; test-set labels are not public since it was a Kaggle competition.

name: MIDRC
full_name: Medical Imaging and Data Resource Center
anatomy: chest/thorax (COVID-19-focused), expanding to oncology and other organ systems
size: hundreds of thousands of imaging studies across tens of thousands of patients
segmentation_detail: Expert annotations including segmentations are available for select cohorts/challenges but not across the full commons
host: MIDRC (University of Chicago / Gen3 data commons)
description: MIDRC is an open federated data commons of DICOM chest radiograph and CT (and expanding MRI/oncology) imaging studies with clinical metadata, accessible to registered researchers via the Gen3 data portal under a data use agreement.

name: Parse2022
segmentation_detail: Pulmonary artery segmentation labels refined from annotations by 10 clinicians (multi-level pulmonary artery structures)
license: Research use with signed data agreement
description: A contrast-enhanced CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) dataset of 200 volumes with refined multi-annotator pulmonary artery segmentation labels for the MICCAI 2022 PARSE challenge.

label_schema: Binary voxel-level segmentation of pulmonary artery in 3D chest CT
Label 0 - Background
Label 1 - Pulmonary artery (whole/multi-level arterial tree as a single refined mask)
details.file_structure: 3D chest CT volumes and their voxel-level segmentation masks are both stored as compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz) files, with each case providing a paired image volume and label volume (image ~512x512x228 to 512x512x376, 1 mm slice thickness).
details.splits: 100 training cases with labels, 30 open validation cases, 70 closed test cases (200 total)
details.extra: Pulmonary artery masks were refined from annotations by 10 clinicians; despite the single-class binary label, evaluation uses multi-level Dice and multi-level HD95 that weight peripheral vs. central artery branches.

name: VESSEL12
segmentation_detail: Reference standard for probabilistic lung blood vessel segmentation (vessel/non-vessel voxel annotations)
description: A chest CT dataset of 20 scans with reference annotations for benchmarking automatic lung blood vessel segmentation methods.

label_schema: Binary vessel annotation at sampled points: vessel (label 1) vs non-vessel (label 0)
Submissions are probabilistic per-voxel vessel likelihood (8-bit unsigned char, 0-255), evaluated by ROC / AUC (Az) against the point labels

Reference points drawn from lung-field locations (vessel-centerline local maxima + random points) manually classified into 5 tissue categories: vessel, lung parenchyma, fissure, airway/airway wall, and lesion, then collapsed to vessel vs non-vessel for scoring
Only points with unanimous agreement of 3 independent human observers are kept in the evaluation set
details.file_structure: CT scans and per-scan lung masks are MetaImage MHD/RAW pairs (ITK-compatible), delivered in .tar.bz2 archives; reference annotations for the example scans are provided as CSV/point lists giving voxel coordinates (x,y,z) with a binary vessel/non-vessel label.
details.splits: 20 challenge CT scans (used as the held-out test set, reference labels withheld) plus 3 example scans released with reference annotations; no dedicated training split.
details.extra: Multi-reader reference standard: a point enters the evaluation set only when all three independent observers agreed on its label; full dense voxel segmentation masks are not provided (evaluation is point/ROC-based).

name: CAS2023
segmentation_detail: Voxel-wise intracranial cerebral artery (vessel) segmentation masks on TOF-MRA volumes
description: CAS2023 is a time-of-flight MRA challenge dataset providing intracranial cerebral artery segmentation annotations for vessel segmentation.

label_schema: Binary voxel-wise segmentation mask (single foreground class)
Label 0 - background
Label 1 - intracranial cerebral artery / vessel
Evaluation scored on both whole-vessel and stenosis regions (DSC, DSC_stenosis, AHD, AHD_stenosis, combined SCORE)
details.file_structure: 3D TOF-MRA volumes and their masks stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz) in nnU-Net-style layout (imagesTr/labelsTr for training, imagesTs for test); one label volume per image with foreground=artery.
details.splits: 100 training cases with public masks; 50 test cases with hidden (organizer-held) masks
details.extra: The scoring emphasizes stenosis: alongside overall Dice (DSC) and average Hausdorff distance (AHD), separate stenosis-region metrics (DSC_stenosis, AHD_stenosis) are reported, so masks are used to evaluate narrowed/pathological vessel segments specifically.

name: ReMIND
full_name: The Brain Resection Multimodal Imaging Database
size: 114 subjects; 43.6 GB (preop MRI 4 sequences, 301 intraop MRI series, 320 intraop 3D US series)
paired_text_detail: Clinical data CSV files with patient demographics, histopathology, and molecular test results
multi_sequence_detail: Preoperative multiparametric MRI (T1, contrast-enhanced T1, T2, T2-FLAIR) plus intraoperative 3T MRI and 3D intraoperative ultrasound
segmentation_detail: 356 segmentations (NRRD and DICOM-SEG) of preoperative tumor, cerebrum, ventricles, and residual tumor
description: Pre- and intra-operative multimodal imaging from 114 neurosurgical brain-tumor patients including preoperative multiparametric MRI, intraoperative MRI, and 3D intraoperative ultrasound with 356 tumor and anatomical segmentations.

label_schema: Segmentation masks (binary label maps, one structure per file) covering 6 structure types across 114 patients, 356 total segmentations
Preoperative whole tumor (113 segmentations)
Preoperative tumor target - the tumor region specifically targeted for resection (3 segmentations)
Previous resection cavity - from prior surgery in reoperation cases (21 segmentations)
Intraoperative residual tumor - segmented from iMRI (58 segmentations)
Cerebrum - automatic segmentation via Brainlab (89 segmentations)
Ventricles - automatic segmentation via Brainlab (54 segmentations)
details.file_structure: Each structure is stored as a separate segmentation file in two provided formats: NRRD (original) and DICOM-SEG (converted from NRRD via dcmqi), accompanying the DICOM source images (preop native/contrast-enhanced T1, T2, T2-FLAIR MRI, intraoperative 3T MRI, and reconstructed 3D intraoperative ultrasound series).
details.extra: Cerebrum and ventricle masks are automatic (Brainlab) surgical-planning segmentations while tumor/target/cavity/residual masks are clinical/manual; cerebrum, ventricle, and tumor masks were made preoperatively whereas residual-tumor masks were made intraoperatively from iMRI. The official page documents structure names and per-structure counts but does not specify a combined multi-label integer value scheme (masks are per-structure), so no integer indices are reported.

name: Burdenko-GBM-Progression
multi_sequence_detail: MRI T1, T1C, T2, FLAIR sequences plus planning CT
segmentation_detail: RTSTRUCT GTV/CTV/PTV radiotherapy target volumes and 10 anatomical organ-at-risk structures (eyes, lenses, optic nerves, brain, brainstem, chiasm, head contour); follow-up tumor volumes for a subset
other_labels: genomic data (IDH1/2 mutation n=110, MGMT methylation n=92)
license: NIH Controlled Data Access (images); CC BY 4.0 (clinical data)
description: Radiotherapy planning MRI and CT with RTSTRUCT tumor and organ-at-risk segmentations, RTPLAN/RTDOSE, and genetic/clinical response data from 180 glioblastoma patients treated 2014-2020, hosted on TCIA.

label_schema: RTSTRUCT radiotherapy target volumes: GTV (Gross Tumor Volume)
RTSTRUCT target volume: CTV (Clinical Target Volume)
RTSTRUCT target volume: PTV (Planning Target Volume)
Organ-at-risk structures (10): Eye Left, Eye Right
OAR: Lens Left, Lens Right
OAR: Optic Nerve Left, Optic Nerve Right
OAR: Brain, Brain stem
OAR: Chiasm
OAR: external body/Head contour
Follow-up GTV segmentations for a subset of patients (longitudinal tumor tracking)
Per-patient clinical outcome label: treatment response status (tumor progression / pseudoprogression / treatment response)
Genomic labels (subset): IDH1/2 mutation status (110 pts: 97 negative, 13 positive); MGMT promoter methylation (92 pts: 55 negative, 37 positive)
details.file_structure: All imaging is DICOM: MR sequences (T1, T1C, T2, FLAIR) in original acquisition space and topometric CT, with radiotherapy files as DICOM RTSTRUCT (segmentations), RTPLAN, and RTDOSE aligned to the topometric CT; clinical/genomic labels are provided in a supplementary spreadsheet.
details.extra: 180 primary-glioblastoma patients (645 studies, 4,956 series). RTSTRUCTs are registered to the topometric CT rather than the MRI space; a containerized ANTs-based MRI-to-CT registration tool is provided via GitHub. Supplementary clinical data also includes anonymized surgery and death dates.

name: RHUH-GBM
full_name: Rio Hortega University Hospital Glioblastoma Dataset
size: 40 patients; ~600 series, 37,425 DICOM images (15.96 GB) plus 2.9 GB NIfTI
multi_sequence_detail: Multiparametric MRI: T1w, T2w, FLAIR, T1w contrast-enhanced (T1ce), and DWI-derived ADC maps across preoperative, early postoperative, and recurrence/follow-up timepoints
segmentation_detail: Expert-validated tumor subregion segmentations (enhancing tumor, necrosis, peritumoral/edema region) in NIfTI for preoperative, postoperative, and follow-up scans
other_labels: clinical data (age, sex, Karnofsky performance score, survival, resection extent, therapy, IDH status, ATRX mutation, Ki-67 index)
description: Multiparametric brain MRI (T1w, T2w, FLAIR, T1ce, ADC) from 40 glioblastoma patients with >95% resection spanning preoperative, early postoperative, and recurrence scans, with expert-validated tumor subregion segmentations and clinical data.

label_schema: Label 1 - Necrosis
Label 2 - Peritumoral signal alteration (edema and non-enhancing tumor tissue)
Label 3 - Enhancing tumor
details.file_structure: Brain-extracted preprocessed MRI images and their tumor subregion segmentation masks are distributed as NIfTI files (with the primary imaging also available as DICOM), organized per patient across three time points (preoperative, early postoperative within 72h, and follow-up at recurrence).
details.extra: Segmentations were auto-generated with DeepMedic and then reviewed and manually corrected by two expert neurosurgeons; 40 patients with roughly 720 segmentation series across the three time points.

name: Colorectal-Liver-Metastases
full_name: Preoperative CT and Survival Data for Patients Undergoing Resection of Colorectal Liver Metastases
segmentation_detail: DICOM-SEG segmentations of liver, liver remnant, hepatic veins, portal veins, and tumors
description: Preoperative portal-venous contrast-enhanced CT with liver, vessel, and tumor DICOM-SEG segmentations and survival data from 197 patients undergoing resection of colorectal liver metastases.

label_schema: Segment: Liver (whole liver)
Segment: Liver_Remnant (tissue predicted to remain after resection)
Segment: Hepatic veins
Segment: Portal veins
Segment: Tumor_x (each tumor lesion labeled individually, e.g. Tumor_1, Tumor_2, ...)
details.file_structure: Per-patient folders (e.g. CRLM-CT-XXXX) each contain the original DICOM CT series plus DICOM Segmentation Objects (DICOM-SEG), where each segment is identified by its Segment Label header; segmentations were converted from native MHD files using dcmqi, and clinical data is provided separately as CSV.
details.extra: 197 patients (197 studies, 394 series, ~17,836 CT images); DICOM-SEG segments are identified by textual Segment Label (not fixed integer class indices), and case CRLM-CT-1074 was scanned prone, giving a different image orientation than other cases.

name: HCC-TACE-Seg
full_name: Multimodality Annotated HCC Cases with and without Advanced Imaging Segmentation
multi_sequence_detail: Pre- and post-procedure (multi-phase) CT imaging studies
segmentation_detail: Manual DICOM-SEG segmentations of liver, tumor, and blood vessels
description: Pre- and post-procedure CT of 105 HCC patients who underwent TACE (2002-2012), with manual liver/tumor/vessel DICOM-SEG segmentations and treatment-outcome data.

label_schema: Segmentation of liver (whole organ)
Segmentation of tumor / mass (HCC lesion)
Segmentation of blood vessels (portal vein and abdominal aorta)
details.file_structure: Per-patient multiphase contrast CT stored as DICOM series, paired with manually curated segmentation masks distributed as DICOM-SEG objects (converted from the original NIfTI masks); companion clinical/outcome data provided as an XLSX spreadsheet.
details.extra: 105 confirmed HCC patients (211 studies, 677 series). Segmentations were created semiautomatically with ThermoFisher AMIRA and then manually clinically curated by radiologists; the XLSX companion file adds demographics, TACE treatment/response, molecular testing, and follow-up (time-to-progression, overall survival). Note: patient HCC_001 has a documented (clinically insignificant) dimension mismatch between its SEG and CT files.

name: Adrenal-ACC-Ki67-Seg
full_name: Voxel-level Segmentation of Pathologically-proven Adrenocortical Carcinoma with Ki-67 Expression
segmentation_detail: Voxel-level segmentation of the adrenal lesion, created semi-automatically with AMIRA then manually refined by a radiologist
description: Contrast-enhanced CT (2006-2018) of 53 pathologically-proven adrenocortical carcinoma patients in DICOM with voxel-level tumor segmentations and Ki-67 index labels for radiomics research.

label_schema: Binary voxel-level segmentation of the adrenal tumor/lesion (single foreground class: tumor vs. background) from contrast-enhanced CT
Segmentation created semi-automatically with AMIRA, then manually refined by an experienced radiologist
Companion clinical/histopathology label: Ki-67 proliferation index (prognostic marker for local recurrence) per patient
Additional clinical fields: demographics, diagnosis, follow-up, and tumor measurement data
All 53 patients have pathologically confirmed adrenocortical carcinoma (ACC)
details.file_structure: DICOM CT image series with matching tumor segmentations stored as DICOM-SEG; clinical/Ki-67 data supplied separately as an XLSX spreadsheet; distributed as a single TCIA archive (53 subjects, 65 studies, 177 series, ~18,255 images).
details.extra: No official train/val/test split; segmentation is a single-lesion binary mask, and the Ki-67 index is a per-patient regression/prognostic label rather than an image annotation.

name: Pediatric-CT-SEG
full_name: Pediatric Chest/Abdomen/Pelvic CT Exams with Expert Organ Contours
segmentation_detail: Expert contours of up to 29 organ structures in DICOM RTSTRUCT format
