description: Pediatric chest/abdomen/pelvis CT from 359 patients (ages 5 days to 16 years) with expert contours of up to 29 organs in RTSTRUCT format for autosegmentation and CT dosimetry research.

label_schema: Expert manual contours of up to 29 anatomical structures per subject, stored as named ROIs in a DICOM RTSTRUCT file (not integer-indexed masks); some subjects are missing structures outside the scan range or not reliably identifiable in young children
Pelvis structures: Bladder, Rectum, Gonads, Prostate, UteroCervix, Femoral Head Left, Femoral Head Right
Abdomen structures: Small Intestine, Large Intestine, Spinal Canal, Gallbladder, Kidney Left, Kidney Right, Spleen, Liver, Stomach, Pancreas, Duodenum, Adrenal Left, Adrenal Right
Thorax structures: Heart, Esophagus, Lung Left, Lung Right, Thymus
Breast structures: Breast Left, Breast Right
Whole-body structures: Skin, Bones
details.file_structure: Distributed on TCIA as DICOM: each of the 359 subject directories holds the axial CT image slices as DICOM files plus a single DICOM RTSTRUCT (RTSS) file containing all named organ contours for that subject.
details.extra: Pediatric chest-abdomen-pelvis / abdomen-pelvis CT covering ages 5 days to 16 years; contours are single-expert (no multi-reader annotations), and structure names follow Table 2 of Jordan et al., Medical Physics 2022 (PMC9090951).

name: Meningioma-SEG-CLASS
full_name: Segmentation and Classification of Grade I and II Meningiomas from MRI: An Open Annotated Dataset
multi_sequence_detail: MRI T1, T1 contrast-enhanced (T1-CE), and T2-FLAIR sequences
segmentation_detail: Manual tumor contours (DICOM SEG) on T1-CE and T2-FLAIR images, reviewed by a central nervous system radiation oncology specialist
other_labels: clinical data (demographics, subtype, surgery type, tumor location, atypical features)
license: CC BY 4.0 (clinical data); NIH Controlled Data Access Policy for images
description: Multiparametric brain MRI (T1, T1-CE, T2-FLAIR) from 96 treatment-naive intracranial meningioma patients with expert manual tumor segmentations and pathologically confirmed WHO grade I/II classification, hosted on TCIA.

label_schema: Segmentation: manual tumor contour of the hyperintense T1-contrast-enhancing tumor on T1-CE MRI
Segmentation: manual tumor contour of the hyperintense T2-FLAIR signal on T2-FLAIR MRI
Classification: pathologically confirmed WHO grade - Grade I vs Grade II meningioma
Clinical/metadata labels: grade, subtype, type of surgery, tumor location, and presence of atypical features
details.file_structure: TCIA collection organized per subject; MR images and tumor contours are stored as DICOM (segmentation as DICOM radiation-therapy structure sets), and clinical/demographic/diagnosis data is provided in a separate XLSX spreadsheet.
details.extra: 96 treatment-naive subjects (180 studies, 674 series); tumor contours were reviewed by a central nervous system radiation oncology specialist and histopathology grading was reviewed by two subspecialty neuropathologists plus one neuropathology fellow.

name: Pancreatic-CT-CBCT-SEG
full_name: Pancreatic CT and Cone-Beam CT with Organ-at-Risk Segmentations
multi_sequence_detail: Paired diagnostic-quality planning CT (helical, contrast) and cone-beam CT (CBCT, mostly limited-view acquisitions)
segmentation_detail: Expert manual organ-at-risk delineations (stomach with first two duodenal segments, small bowel) by radiation oncologists, provided as RTSTRUCT contours on both planning CT and CBCT
description: Paired planning CT and cone-beam CT scans with expert organ-at-risk RTSTRUCT segmentations and RTDOSE from 40 locally advanced pancreatic cancer patients undergoing ablative radiotherapy, hosted openly on TCIA.

label_schema: Organ-at-risk (OAR) segmentations as named RTSTRUCT ROIs (no integer label indices; structures identified by ROI name)
SDPC - Stomach with first two duodenal segments, delineated on planning CT
SDCB - Stomach with first two duodenal segments, delineated on CBCT
BSPC - Small bowel (remainder) delineated on planning CT
BSCB - Small bowel (remainder) delineated on CBCT
Contours were manually delineated by radiation oncologists; ROIs may carry an 'UNAPPROVED' flag indicating research-verified but not clinically approved status
details.file_structure: DICOM images (planning CT and CBCT) with expert contours stored as separate DICOM RTSTRUCT (RT Structure Set) files per study; structures are referenced by ROI name (e.g., SDPC, BSCB) rather than integer voxel labels.
details.extra: 40 patients; CBCT includes both limited-view (200 degrees) and full-view (360 degrees) acquisitions. Version 2 adds a multi-reader subset: 10 original-format CBCT scans repeat-delineated by two independent observers for inter-observer variability analysis.

name: GLIS-RT
full_name: Glioma Image Segmentation for Radiotherapy: RT Targets, Barriers to Cancer Spread, and Organs at Risk
multi_sequence_detail: Contrast-enhanced 3D T1 and 2D T2-FLAIR MRI plus planning CT with registrations (REG)
segmentation_detail: Manual RTSTRUCT delineations of RT targets (GTV/CTV), organs at risk (brainstem, optic chiasm, optic nerves, eyes, cochleae, lacrimal glands), and anatomical barriers on a 75-case subset (falx, tentorium, ventricles, cerebellum)
license: TCIA Data Usage Policy (NIH Controlled Data Access)
description: Glioblastoma and low-grade glioma cases from 230 patients with expert-delineated radiotherapy targets, organs at risk, and anatomical barriers on co-registered planning CT and MRI in DICOM, hosted on TCIA.

label_schema: Named ROI contours in RTSTRUCT (no integer indices); RT targets and normal structures
RT targets: GTV (gross tumor volume), CTV (clinical target volume)
Organs at risk: brainstem, optic chiasm, optic nerves, eyes, cochleae, lacrimal glands
Anatomical brain barriers (75-case subset): falx cerebri, tentorium cerebelli, transverse and sagittal brain sinuses, ventricles, cerebellum
230 glioma patients: 198 glioblastoma (GBM), 23 anaplastic astrocytoma, 5 astrocytoma, 2 anaplastic oligodendroglioma, 2 oligodendroglioma
details.file_structure: DICOM throughout: per-patient CT and MR image series (contrast-enhanced 3D-T1 and 2D T2-FLAIR) with manual delineations stored as DICOM RTSTRUCT files referencing the image series.
details.extra: The anatomical-barrier annotations (falx, tentorium, sinuses, ventricles, cerebellum) cover only a 75-case subset used in the MICCAI 2020 'Anatomical Brain Barriers to Cancer Spread' challenge; the target/OAR delineations cover the full 230-case collection.

name: Prostate-MRI-US-Biopsy
full_name: Prostate MRI and Ultrasound With Pathology and Coordinates of Tracked Biopsy
paired_text_detail: Biopsy pathology outcomes with Gleason grades and MRI ROI suspicion scores per tracked biopsy core (XLSX spreadsheets)
multi_sequence_detail: T2-weighted plus diffusion-weighted imaging (ADC, high b-value) for 837 subjects
segmentation_detail: Prostate gland surfaces and suspicious ROI segmentations as STL surface files with tracked biopsy core locations
description: TCIA collection of tracked MRI-ultrasound fusion biopsy data from 1,151 prostate cancer patients, including multiparametric MRI (DICOM), 3D ultrasound, Gleason pathology outcomes, tracked biopsy coordinates, and STL prostate/ROI segmentations (v2 updated 2023).

label_schema: ProstateSurface - semi-automatic segmentation of the prostate gland (MRI traced in Profuse, ultrasound traced in Artemis fusion system)
Target - region of interest suspicious for cancer; MRI ROIs manually traced by radiologists, ultrasound ROIs derived via nonrigid MRI-to-US registration (avg accuracy 3-4 mm)
Multiple targets per case labeled Target1, Target2, ... in filenames
Biopsy core locations: tracked 3D coordinates from Artemis mechanical-arm kinematics, given in both ultrasound and MRI reference frames
details.file_structure: DICOM MR/US images per patient; segmentations delivered as binary STL 3D surface files (prostate and target surfaces, ~274 MB); biopsy core and target coordinates stored in XLSX spreadsheets; a BiopsyVectors.mrml (3D Slicer) file and a ZIP of biopsy-overlay packages are also provided.
details.extra: STL surfaces are not voxel label maps, so they must be converted (e.g. rasterized in 3D Slicer) to use as segmentation masks; MRI biopsy coordinates are only ~70% complete, and Version 2 adds diffusion-weighted MRI for 837 participants.

name: LUNA25
size: Over 4,000 annotated low-dose chest CT exams for development, 468 hidden test cases, 300 reader-study cases
description: A low-dose chest CT benchmark of over 4,000 annotated exams for lung nodule malignancy risk estimation in lung cancer screening.

label_schema: Task: binary lung-nodule malignancy risk classification (per nodule) on low-dose chest CT
label: 0 = benign, 1 = malignant (confirmed in baseline train.py: label.sum() counts malignant samples)

Each row = one nodule annotation keyed by AnnotationID; CSV columns: PatientID, SeriesInstanceUID, StudyDate, AnnotationID, CoordX, CoordY, CoordZ (nodule world coordinates), Age_at_StudyDate, Gender, label
No segmentation masks or nodule diameter class labels provided - only a point location per nodule plus the binary malignancy label
Clinical metadata accompanying each nodule: Age_at_StudyDate and Gender
details.file_structure: Low-dose chest CT scans stored as MHA volumes (read with SimpleITK, one per SeriesInstanceUID) alongside a CSV of per-nodule annotations (coordinates + binary malignancy label + clinical info); the official baseline consumes preprocessed patches as per-annotation .npy image files plus a .npy metadata file (origin/spacing/transform), and inference on Grand-Challenge takes an MHA image, a nodule-locations.json (coordinates + annotationIDs) and clinical information (Age, Gender).
details.splits: Public training/development set of >4,000 annotated low-dose chest CT exams; 468 hidden test cases; separate 300-case reader study. No official train/validation split - participants split the provided CSV themselves.
details.extra: Data is derived from the NLST (National Lung Screening Trial); test-set labels are hidden (evaluation only on Grand-Challenge), and the 300-case reader study compares AI algorithms against a panel of international radiologists.

name: AbdomenAtlas 1.0
anatomy: Abdominal organs (spleen, liver, kidneys, stomach, gallbladder, pancreas, aorta, IVC)
segmentation_detail: Voxel-level masks for eight structures: spleen, liver, kidneys, stomach, gallbladder, pancreas, aorta, and IVC
description: One of the largest fully annotated abdominal CT segmentation datasets, with 5,195 volumes labeled for eight organs.

label_schema: Voxel-level segmentation masks for 9 abdominal structures (8 organs, kidneys split into left/right)
Label 1 - aorta
Label 2 - gall_bladder
Label 3 - kidney_left
Label 4 - kidney_right
Label 5 - liver
Label 6 - pancreas
Label 7 - postcava (inferior vena cava / IVC)
Label 8 - spleen
Label 9 - stomach
AbdomenAtlas1.0Mini notes improved label quality for aorta and kidneys
details.file_structure: One folder per case (e.g. BDMAP_00000001) containing ct.nii.gz (the CT volume), combined_labels.nii.gz (a single multi-class mask using the integer label values above), and a segmentations/ subfolder holding per-organ binary masks (spleen.nii.gz, liver.nii.gz, pancreas.nii.gz, aorta.nii.gz, gall_bladder.nii.gz, kidney_left.nii.gz, kidney_right.nii.gz, postcava.nii.gz, stomach.nii.gz); all files are compressed NIfTI (.nii.gz).
details.extra: AbdomenAtlas1.0Mini contains 5,195 annotated CT volumes (the full AbdomenAtlas 1.0 has 8,448); most organ masks are AI-generated pseudo-labels refined via the authors' active-learning pipeline, not fully manual annotations.

name: FLARE 2024
full_name: MICCAI FLARE 2024 Abdominal CT Organ Segmentation (Task 2: Laptop)
size: Thousands of partially labeled and unlabeled abdominal CT scans (large semi-supervised set)
segmentation_detail: Abdominal organ segmentation labels (multi-organ) with large unlabeled data for semi-supervised learning; related FLARE tasks also cover pan-cancer tumor segmentation
description: An abdominal CT organ segmentation benchmark emphasizing efficient, resource-constrained (laptop) inference and semi-supervised learning from large unlabeled data.

label_schema: Voxel-wise multi-organ segmentation mask, 13 abdominal organs (label 0 = background)
Label 1 - liver
Label 2 - right kidney
Label 3 - spleen
Label 4 - pancreas
Label 5 - aorta
Label 6 - inferior vena cava (IVC)
Label 7 - right adrenal gland
Label 8 - left adrenal gland
Label 9 - gallbladder
Label 10 - esophagus
Label 11 - stomach
Label 12 - duodenum
Label 13 - left kidney
details.file_structure: 3D CT volumes and their integer label masks are stored as NIfTI (.nii.gz) in nnU-Net-style folders (imagesTr/labelsTr); image files carry a _0000 modality suffix (e.g. FLARE22_Tr_0001_0000.nii.gz) and the matching mask shares the case ID (FLARE22_Tr_0001.nii.gz) with voxel values 0-13.
details.splits: Codabench comp 2320 = FLARE 2024-2025 Task 2 (Abdominal CT Organ Segmentation on Laptop), reusing the FLARE22 abdominal CT dataset: 50 labeled + 2000 unlabeled training CT scans, 50 visible validation cases, and ~200 hidden test cases.
details.extra: Semi-supervised setup: only 50 training cases carry ground-truth 13-organ masks while 2000 are unlabeled; the 'on Laptop' task adds strict efficiency/resource constraints (GPU memory and runtime). FLARE 2024 also ran companion tasks with different labels (Task 1 pan-cancer tumor segmentation, Task 3 MRI abdominal organs).

name: M3D-Seg
size: ~5,772 3D CT images with 149,196 3D mask annotations (~240 GB), aggregated from 25 datasets
paired_text_detail: Text descriptions of semantic labels supporting text-based and referring segmentation; the broader M3D suite adds VQA and report data
segmentation_detail: 149,196 3D masks across organs including kidney, liver, pancreas, spleen, lung, colon, heart, bone and vessels, stored as .npz sparse masks
description: A large aggregated 3D CT segmentation dataset from 25 public sources with ~149K 3D masks and text-linked semantic labels supporting semantic, referring, and reasoning segmentation.

label_schema: Voxel-level 3D semantic segmentation masks; integer voxel value = category ID (0 = background), mapped to a class text via dataset_info.json/dataset_info.txt
Aggregates 25 CT segmentation sub-datasets (folders 0000-0024), each keeping its OWN class list and its own ID->label mapping (no single unified label space)
0000 CHAOS: liver (1 class)

0002 AMOS22 (15 classes): spleen, right kidney, left kidney, gall bladder, esophagus, liver, stomach, aorta, postcava, pancreas, right adrenal gland, left adrenal gland, duodenum, bladder, prostate/uterus
0003 AbdomenCT-1k: liver, kidney, spleen, pancreas
0004 KiTS23: kidney, kidney tumor, kidney cyst
0005 KiPA22: Renal vein, Kidney, Renal artery, Tumor

0007 BTCV (13 classes): spleen, right kidney, left kidney, gallbladder, esophagus, liver, stomach, aorta, inferior vena cava, portal vein and splenic vein, pancreas, right adrenal gland, left adrenal gland

0011 TotalSegmentator (~104 classes): adrenal glands, aorta, autochthon, brain, clavicula, colon, duodenum, esophagus, femur, gluteus muscles, heart chambers/myocardium, hip, humerus, iliac artery/vena, kidneys, liver, lung lobes, pancreas, portal/splenic vein, pulmonary artery, ribs 1-12 L/R, vertebrae, etc.

0001 HaN-Seg (30 head-and-neck classes): carotid arteries, brainstem, oral cavity, cochlea, esophagus, eyes, lacrimal/submandibular/thyroid glands, larynx, optic chiasm/nerves, parotid, pituitary, spinal cord

MSD sub-datasets: 0018 colon (cancer), 0019 hepatic vessel, 0020 liver (+tumor), 0021 lung (nodule), 0022 pancreas (+tumor), 0023 spleen; plus 0006 KiTS19, 0008 Pancreas-CT, 0009 3D-IRCADb (~47 classes), 0010 AbdomenCT-12organ, 0012 CT-ORG, 0013 WORD, 0014/0015 VerSe19/20 (vertebrae), 0016 SLIVER07, 0017 QUBIQ, 0024 LUNA16
term_dictionary.json provides ChatGPT-generated text definitions per class, enabling text-prompted/referring segmentation
details.file_structure: On disk as M3D_Seg/<0000-0024>/<sample_id>/ folders each holding image.npy (CT volume) and a sparse mask_(shape).npz (foreground stored as scipy sparse, shape encoded in the filename); each sub-dataset folder also contains its own <id>.json split file, with global dataset_info.json/dataset_info.txt (ID->label text) and term_dictionary.json at the root.
details.splits: No single global split; each of the 25 sub-datasets defines its own train/test partition inside its per-dataset JSON file (no validation split). Totals: 5,772 3D images and 149,196 3D mask annotations.
details.extra: Labels are per-sub-dataset with inconsistent naming/granularity (e.g. 'aorta' vs 'arota', dozens of noisy free-text livertumor variants in 0009 3D-IRCADb), so voxel category IDs must be resolved through each sub-dataset's own mapping rather than a shared taxonomy. README lists FLARE22 among the 25, but the authoritative dataset_info.txt names folder 0010 'AbdomenCT-12organ'.

name: OpenMind
full_name: OpenMind: Large-Scale Brain MRI Pretraining Dataset
size: ~114K MRI images (1.04 TB) curated from 800 OpenNeuro datasets across 30+ scanners
multi_sequence_detail: 23 different MRI modalities/techniques including T1w, T2, inplaneT2 and others
segmentation_detail: Includes deface masks and anatomy masks (not tumor/organ segmentation targets)
description: A large-scale brain MRI pretraining dataset of ~114K NIfTI images spanning 23 sequence types curated from 800 OpenNeuro datasets across 30+ scanners for self-supervised foundation models.

label_schema: Self-supervised pretraining corpus - NO expert segmentation/classification targets (no tumor/organ/lesion labels)
Companion 'deface_mask' - mask delineating the anonymized/defaced region of each volume (for reconstruction/inpainting-aware SSL)
Companion 'fb_mask' (anatomy/foreground mask) - mask delineating voxels that contain actual anatomy vs. background/empty space (e.g. for brain-extracted images)
Per-image metadata labels: MR modality/technique (23 types, 100% coverage), scanner manufacturer/model/field strength (100%)
Demographic/clinical metadata (partial coverage): Age (~70%), Sex (~77%), Handedness (~35%), Health status (~26%), Race (~12%), BMI (~16%), Weight (~1.5%)
details.file_structure: Modified BIDS layout of gzipped NIfTI (.nii.gz) volumes organized by OpenNeuro dataset ID / subject / session / anatomy; each image has a sibling folder '<image>__Data/' holding deface_mask.nii.gz and fb_mask.nii.gz, and a top-level openneuro_metadata.csv maps relative paths plus all metadata for the ~114k images.
details.extra: ~114,000 3D head/brain MRI volumes aggregated from ~800 OpenNeuro datasets (>30 scanners); masks are algorithmic/companion aids for SSL, not human-annotated diagnostic labels, and the HuggingFace viewer's 45/193 'rows' are dataset-index groupings, not sample-level train/test splits.

name: fastMRI Prostate
full_name: fastMRI Prostate: A Public, Biparametric MRI Dataset
multi_sequence_detail: Axial T2-weighted and axial diffusion-weighted (DWI) sequences (biparametric)
license: fastMRI Data Sharing Agreement (research use only)
description: Raw multi-coil k-space data from 312 prostate MRI exams acquired on 3T scanners, comprising axial T2-weighted and diffusion-weighted acquisitions for accelerated reconstruction research.

label_schema: Slice-level labels indicating the presence and grade of prostate cancer
Labels provided at slice-, volume-, and exam-level for both the T2-weighted and diffusion-weighted (DWI) sequences
Intended target task: detection/classification of clinically significant prostate cancer (csPCa)
details.file_structure: Per-exam HDF5 (.h5) files containing raw multi-coil k-space measurements plus calibration/phase-correction data (and coil sensitivity/ESPIRiT maps) with separate axial T2-weighted and axial diffusion-weighted acquisitions; annotation label files are distributed alongside and keyed to the exam filenames (the released classification code consumes the label files and filenames directly).
details.extra: Dataset comprises 312 biparametric prostate MRI exams acquired on 3 Tesla scanners (axial T2 + axial DWI), released April 2023 as an expansion of the fastMRI dataset; the original release centers on raw k-space for reconstruction, with the cancer labels supporting a downstream csPCa classification task (separate T2 and DWI models). Exact official train/validation/test counts could not be verified.

name: fastMRI Breast
multi_sequence_detail: Axial dynamic contrast-enhanced (DCE) 3D GRASP sequence acquired at 3T
license: fastMRI Data Sharing Agreement (research use only)
description: Raw k-space data from 300 clinical breast DCE-MRI exams acquired on 3T scanners with a 3D GRASP sequence for accelerated MRI reconstruction research.

label_schema: Case-level clinical/pathology labels (no pixel annotations)
Lesion status: negative / benign / malignant
Cohort composition: 51 exams no suspicious lesion, 159 benign, 90 malignant
Malignant lesion type: invasive ductal carcinoma (55), invasive lobular carcinoma (10), mixed (7), ductal carcinoma in situ (18)
Additional per-case metadata: patient age, menopause status, reason for exam, laterality of malignant findings, modality malignancy first observed, tumor grade, tumor biomarkers
details.file_structure: Per-case folders named by patient code (e.g. fastMRI_breast_003_2) each holding raw radial k-space in HDF5 (.h5) plus reconstructed clinical images in DICOM; all case-level labels/metadata are supplied separately in an Excel spreadsheet (breast_fastMRI_final.xlsx), not embedded per pixel.
details.splits: Split 80:20 into training and testing cohorts (lesion-type ratios preserved); no separate validation split defined.
details.extra: No segmentation masks or bounding-box annotations are provided — the authors state the dataset is currently less suitable for supervised segmentation. 284 patients / 300 exams (16 patients rescanned ~1 year later), axial DCE-MRI acquired with a 3D GRASP golden-angle radial sequence at 3T.

name: K2S
full_name: K2S: From Undersampled K-space To Automatic Segmentation Challenge
segmentation_detail: Bone and cartilage tissue segmentation masks paired with fully-sampled/undersampled k-space and reconstructed images
description: Grand Challenge dataset of 300 training and 50 test 3D fat-suppressed FSE-Cube knee MRIs providing 8x undersampled multi-channel k-space, fully-sampled images, and bone/cartilage segmentation masks for joint MRI reconstruction and segmentation.

label_schema: 6 tissue segmentation classes total, grouped for scoring into bone and cartilage
Femur (bone)
Tibia (bone)
Patella (bone)
Femoral cartilage
Tibial cartilage
Patellar cartilage
Evaluation collapses classes into 2 groups (bone, cartilage) scored with volume-weighted Dice (weights inversely proportional to structure size)
details.file_structure: 3D fat-suppressed FSE-CUBE knee MRI provided as 8x-undersampled multi-channel k-space plus fully-sampled auxiliary reconstructed images, with paired dense bone/cartilage segmentation masks; k-space, images, and masks are distributed as HDF5 (.h5) files and predicted test masks must also be submitted as HDF5.
details.splits: 300 training/validation cases (no official validation split — participants create their own) and 50 test cases
details.extra: Test-set segmentation ground truth was never released; fully-sampled images are given only as training auxiliary data (not available for test-set inference), and the organizers state no data is shared after the challenge concluded.

name: M4Raw
full_name: M4Raw: A Multi-contrast, Multi-repetition, Multi-channel MRI k-space Dataset for Low-field MRI Research
multi_sequence_detail: T1-weighted, T2-weighted, FLAIR, and GRE contrasts, each with multiple repetitions for multi-repetition averaging
description: Multi-channel brain MRI k-space data from 183 healthy volunteers scanned on a 0.3T low-field whole-body scanner, with T1/T2/FLAIR/GRE contrasts and multiple repetitions to enable low-field MRI reconstruction and denoising research.

name: ACRIN-NSCLC-FDG-PET
full_name: Data from the ACRIN 6668 Trial NSCLC-FDG-PET
paired_text_detail: Clinical XLS spreadsheets: demographics, diagnosis, treatment, follow-up, and measurement data (anonymized)
segmentation_detail: Tumor segmentations available separately via the BAMF AIMI Annotations initiative (not part of the primary TCIA release)
description: 242 NSCLC patients from the ACRIN 6668/RTOG 0235 trial with serial pre- and post-chemoradiotherapy FDG PET/CT DICOM imaging plus clinical and outcome spreadsheets.

label_schema: Primary TCIA release provides no image segmentations; annotations are reader quantitative measurements and clinical forms, not pixel labels
Reader measurements per lesion: SUVmax (maximum single-voxel standardized uptake value) and SUVpeak (mean SUV in a 0.75-1.5 cm ROI), by institutional and central readers
Quantified FDG uptake targets: primary tumor, regional lymph nodes, and common sites of distant metastasis
Clinical form categories: Demographic, Diagnosis, Follow-Up, Measurement, Treatment
Companion BAMF segmentation: single foreground class = FDG-avid lesion (tumor), restricted to pulmonary/pleural region (Label 1 = lesion; binary mask)
details.file_structure: Primary TCIA release stores per-patient DICOM images (PET, CT, NM, MR, CR, DX, SC) with clinical/reader data in XLS/CSV spreadsheets; the separate BAMF AIMI companion provides tumor masks as DICOM-SEG (one per scan, or NIfTI .nii.gz fallback), distributed via Zenodo record 8352041 (lung-fdg-pet-ct.zip), not in the TCIA release.
details.splits: Clinical/reader spreadsheets are split into File Set 1 (75% random sample) and File Set 2 (25% random sample); no official train/val/test split for the imaging or segmentation annotations.
details.extra: BAMF masks are auto-generated by an nnU-Net FDG-PET/CT lesion model (trained on AutoPET 2023, ensembled over 5 folds) with lesions limited to lung/pleura via TotalSegmentator; only ~10% of cases were reviewed/corrected by a radiologist (plus a non-expert), so most masks are unverified AI output.

name: ACRIN-HNSCC-FDG-PET-CT
full_name: ACRIN 6685: A Multicenter Trial of FDG-PET/CT Staging of Head and Neck Cancer
paired_text_detail: Clinical data (classification, demographic, diagnosis, follow-up, measurement, treatment) provided as XLS/CSV/PDF with data dictionary
segmentation_detail: Tumor annotations available via related analysis result 'ACRIN 6685-Tumor-Annotations'
license: NIH Controlled Data Access (imaging); CC BY 4.0 (clinical)
description: 260 head-and-neck cancer patients from the ACRIN 6685 multicenter trial with FDG PET/CT staging imaging, tumor annotations, and pathology-correlated clinical data hosted on TCIA.

label_schema: Lesion-level tumor annotations (head and neck squamous cell carcinoma) - no fixed integer class map; each annotated lesion is one structure
3D segmentation contours of measurable tumor lesions
Seed point per lesion (auto-generated, radiologist-reviewed)
Negative-findings assessment per scan (indicates no annotatable lesion present)
Inclusion criteria: lymph nodes >1 cm short-axis and other lesions >1 cm; max 5 lesions per scan timepoint, no more than 2 per organ
Measurement standards followed: RECIST 1.1 (CT/MR) and PERCIST (PET)
details.file_structure: DICOM data organized per subject on TCIA; annotations distributed as DICOM RTSTRUCT (segmentations), plus seed points and negative-findings assessments, referencing the source CT and attenuation-corrected PET (and MR) series.
details.extra: Multi-reader: scans initially annotated by an international team of radiologists (MBBS or higher) then reviewed by US-based board-certified radiologists; 258 subjects / 481 studies / 2,085 series in the 'ACRIN 6685-Tumor-Annotations' analysis result (a subset of the 260-subject parent collection).

name: ACRIN-FMISO-Brain
full_name: ACRIN 6684: Assessment of Tumor Hypoxia in Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma Using 18F-FMISO PET and MRI
paired_text_detail: ~30 clinical forms (~2,000 elements) with demographics, diagnosis, treatment, follow-up, and pathology biomarkers (MGMT status, HIF1-alpha, GLUT1, CAIX, CD31, alpha-SMA); provided as CSV/XLSX
multi_sequence_detail: Multiple MRI sequences alongside 18F-FMISO PET and low-dose CT
segmentation_detail: Binary DICOM VOI masks: 18F-FMISO PET hypoxia maps and MRI enhancing tumor lesion masks
license: CC BY 3.0 (clinical data); NIH Controlled Data Access (imaging)
description: TCIA collection of 45 newly diagnosed glioblastoma patients imaged with 18F-FMISO hypoxia PET plus MRI and low-dose CT, including binary DICOM hypoxia and enhancing-tumor VOI masks and detailed clinical forms.

label_schema: Binary VOI mask, value 1 = inside region of interest, value 0 = outside
18F-FMISO PET hypoxia VOI: hypoxic voxels defined by tumor-to-cerebellar activity ratio greater than 1.2
MRI enhancing tumor lesion VOI: contrast-enhancing tumor delineated on calibrated pre/post-contrast T1 series
details.file_structure: Two sets of binary segmentation masks, each stored as a single DICOM volume (voxel value 1 inside the VOI, 0 outside); provided as separate downloads for the hypoxia PET masks and the MRI tumor masks (not DICOM-SEG or RTSTRUCT).
details.extra: Analysis/segmentation collection accompanying the ACRIN 6684 clinical trial imaging (glioblastoma); roughly 45 patients, so masks cover only a subset of the full imaging cohort.

name: ACRIN-FLT-Breast
paired_text_detail: ~40 clinical forms (CSV/XLS): demographics, medical history, treatment, pathology, measurements, follow-up
description: 83 locally advanced breast-cancer patients from the ACRIN 6688 Phase II trial with 18F-FLT and FDG PET/CT imaging for early neoadjuvant chemotherapy response assessment.

label_schema: No image-level segmentations or bounding boxes: no DICOM-SEG/RTSTRUCT provided
Labels are patient-level clinical/outcome variables from ~40 case report forms (several thousand elements) encoded from the ACRIN 6688 trial
Primary outcome: pathologic complete response (pCR) to neoadjuvant chemotherapy
Ki-67 tissue proliferation marker (correlated with 18F-FLT uptake)
Follow-up outcome variables including dates of progression and survival
Demographics (all female, mean age ~51.7 years) and pathology variables
details.file_structure: DICOM images (CT, 18F-FLT and 18F-FDG PET, and OT) organized by patient/study/series on TCIA; clinical form data supplied separately as CSV files accompanied by a Data Dictionary describing each variable and its coded values.
details.extra: 83 patients / 363 studies / 1,480 series / ~688,598 images; PET includes dynamic 45-frame FLT sequences and both attenuation-corrected (AC) and non-corrected (NAC) reconstructions. No annotation masks exist, so labels are tabular clinical endpoints rather than pixel/region labels.

name: Anti-PD-1_Melanoma
paired_text_detail: Patient outcomes and treatment details provided where available
license: CC BY 3.0 (imaging under NIH Controlled Data Access)
description: 47 melanoma patients treated with anti-PD-1 immunotherapy with pre-treatment and follow-up PET, CT, and MR imaging, distributed as DICOM under NIH Controlled Data Access.

name: Anti-PD-1_Lung
paired_text_detail: Supporting clinical metadata including patient outcomes and treatment details provided where available
segmentation_detail: Segmentations available via IDC Zenodo (BAMF AIMI Annotations initiative)
description: FDG PET/CT imaging of 46 lung-cancer patients treated with anti-PD-1 immunotherapy, with pre-treatment baseline and follow-up scans.

label_schema: Segmentation masks (not part of original collection; added via BAMF AIMI Annotations initiative)
Primary target: lung nodule / lesion segmentation (nodules 3-30 mm diameter)
Whole-lung segmentation produced as anatomical context; in the combined mask nodules take precedence over lung
Exact integer label values are not documented in the public model card / source, so they are omitted rather than guessed
details.file_structure: Imaging is DICOM CT and PET (modalities PT/CT/SC) organized by TCIA subject/study/series; BAMF-generated segmentations are distributed separately (IDC / Zenodo AIMI community) as DICOM-SEG (.dcm) files, with NIfTI (.nii.gz, e.g. seg_nodules_ensemble / seg_lesions_ensemble) as a fallback format.
details.extra: Segmentations are AI-generated by a BAMF nnU-Net ensemble and validated against a radiologist and a non-expert reviewer; the underlying TCIA collection (46 subjects, pre-treatment plus follow-up FDG PET/CT for anti-PD-1 immunotherapy lung cases) ships no expert tumor annotations of its own.

name: Soft-tissue-Sarcoma
paired_text_detail: Clinical spreadsheet (XLSX): demographics, histopathological type, tumor grade, outcome follow-up (metastases, survival)
multi_sequence_detail: MRI sequences T1-weighted, T2-weighted fat-saturated, and STIR alongside FDG PET/CT
segmentation_detail: Expert manual GTV_Mass and GTV_Edema tumor contours as RTSTRUCT DICOM objects for all scans
description: 51 soft-tissue sarcoma patients with FDG PET/CT and multi-sequence MRI (T1, T2FS, STIR) and expert manual tumor/edema RTSTRUCT segmentations plus clinical outcome data on TCIA.

label_schema: GTV_Mass - gross tumor volume excluding visible peritumoral edema
GTV_Edema - gross tumor volume incorporating visible edema (available for the 32 of 51 patients who had visible edema)
Contours manually drawn by an expert radiation oncologist and propagated across modalities via rigid registration
details.file_structure: 51 patients of pre-treatment FDG-PET/CT and MRI (T1-weighted, T2-weighted fat-saturated, and STIR) stored as DICOM image series, with the GTV_Mass and GTV_Edema tumor contours stored as RTSTRUCT DICOM objects for all scans.
details.extra: 51 patients / 102 studies / 612 series (~38,283 images); 19 patients developed lung metastases during follow-up, and only 32 patients have a GTV_Edema contour since not all tumors showed visible edema.

name: DeepLesion
size: 32,735 lesions on 32,120 CT slices from 4,427 patients (~225 GB)
license: NIH open access (free for research use)
description: A large-scale CT dataset of 32,735 clinically significant lesions across 4,427 patients, mined from radiologist bookmarks with bounding boxes and RECIST diameter measurements, stored as 16-bit PNG slices.

label_schema: Lesion detection/measurement dataset: each row = one lesion on one key axial CT slice (weak 2D annotation, not full segmentation)
Bounding_boxes: axis-aligned 2D box per lesion (x1,y1,x2,y2), derived from the RECIST measurement
Measurement_coordinates: 8 numbers = the two RECIST diameters (long-axis + short-axis endpoints) marked by radiologists
Lesion_diameters_Pixel_: long and short diameter lengths in pixels
Normalized_lesion_location: relative x,y,z body coordinate of the lesion
Coarse_lesion_type: 8-class lesion category (labeled only for a subset ~9,800 lesions; -1 = unlabeled)
Coarse_lesion_type 1 = bone
Coarse_lesion_type 2 = abdomen
Coarse_lesion_type 3 = mediastinum
Coarse_lesion_type 4 = liver
Coarse_lesion_type 5 = lung
Coarse_lesion_type 6 = kidney
Coarse_lesion_type 7 = soft tissue
Coarse_lesion_type 8 = pelvis
Possibly_noisy flag; plus metadata: Slice_range, Spacing_mm_px_, Image_size, DICOM_windows, Patient_gender, Patient_age
details.file_structure: CT slices are stored as 16-bit unsigned PNG images (intensity = HU + 32768) grouped in folders named by patient_study_series, with a range of slices around each key slice; all annotations are in a single flat CSV file, DL_info.csv, one row per lesion.
details.splits: Official split via the Train_Val_Test column in DL_info.csv (1=train, 2=val, 3=test), an approximately 70/15/15 lesion-level split of the 32,735 lesions.
details.extra: Scale: 32,735 lesions on 32,120 axial CT slices from 10,594 studies of 4,427 unique patients. The Coarse_lesion_type label exists for only a subset of lesions; companion works add richer labels (e.g., LesaNet's 171 RadLex body-part/type/attribute tags text-mined from reports, with 500 test lesions manually annotated by two radiologists).

name: RadImageNet
anatomy: Multiple (whole body; CT/MRI/US across many anatomical regions)
size: ~1.35 million annotated images from 131,000+ patients (CT, MRI, ultrasound)
paired_text_detail: Pathology/anatomy class labels rather than free-text reports or captions
segmentation_detail: Image-level pathology labels, not segmentation masks
license: RadImageNet license (free for non-commercial research; commercial licensing available)
description: RadImageNet is a large open medical imaging database of ~1.35 million CT, MRI, and ultrasound images with anatomy and pathology labels, built as a domain-specific pretraining resource for transfer learning.
